runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXI And Partner
runners.comment: Won June Listed event (5f); came up short in G2/G1 since; first-time blinkers
runners.spotlight: Big effort when second in the Dash at Epsom on Derby day in June and it was a career best when winning a Chantilly Listed event (also 5f, good; Lesslepasser and Ciao Pa' behind) just over a fortnight later; has come up short in a Group 2 and Group 1 since so this drop down in grade should help; first-time blinkers replace usual cheekpieces.

quotes.quote: Great run, Spartan Arrow loves the track and is obviously a bit of a course specialist. Probably wants slightly quicker ground, but he handled it today and I can't really knock him - Hollie Doyle, jockey.

runners.comment: Beaten 1l in a wild finish to this last year; confidence booster latest; respected
runners.spotlight: Landed a hat-trick in the first half of 2024, which culminated with an unchallenged success in a Chantilly Group 2 (5f, heavy) in June; plenty of subsequent good efforts give him a shot today and he had a confidence booster when winning easily in his homeland at Most last month; only beaten 1l, despite finishing seventh, in a wild finish to this race last year; respected.

quotes.quote: I was thinking, going to the gate, that Ponntos was more relaxed than last season. He is very speedy and always runs to the wire - Antonio Fresu, jockey.

runners.comment: Good second in C&D Group 3 over C&D in May hasn't been matched since; passed over
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent but it was a fine effort when second in a Group 3 over C&D (good to soft; Ponntos second, Lesslepasser behind) in May; nowhere near that in handicaps on his last two starts, though.

runners.comment: 2nd behind Monteille in a Chantilly Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in June; not matched since
runners.spotlight: Best run since March when second behind Monteille in a Chantilly Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in June; hasn't matched that form since; ninth in this last year.

runners.comment: Listed win here (7f, very soft) in April hasn't been built on; too soon to write him off
runners.spotlight: Looked to be on the up when landing a Listed race here (7f, very soft) in April but hasn't built on that in two starts since, including when dropped back down to 6f last time; first start at 5f and shaped like that may help last time; far too soon to write him off.

runners.comment: Back to form with June G3 win; 8th of 20 in G1 next; 3-month break but she's respected
runners.spotlight: Came right back to form on her second start for this yard when landing the Group 3 Gros-Chene at Chantilly (5f, good to soft), with Lesslepasser, Ponntos and Ciao Pa' behind, in June; not seen since an honourable eighth of 20 in the Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm; 150-1) a fortnight later; finished an unlucky eighth in this last year; firmly in the mix if she's ready to go.

quotes.quote: A lot of credit goes to to John Hammond [racing manager to Gerard Augustin-Normand] because it wasn't my idea to run. Monteille worked well and he urged me to run. When she picked up she was really good. She really needed her comeback and she was very impressive today. We'll see how she comes out of the race but I think the owners would like to go to Ascot - Mario Baratti, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's been in good form since her win at Chantilly, though we haven't had to do a lot with her. She'll have one light canter in the middle of this week and then the partners will decide together, but Ascot remains an option. She's in the Goffs London Sale before the meeting. 11-06-25

runners.comment: Decent 4th in Deauville Listed race (5f) last month; needs more but that's not unlikely
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Monteille in the Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot but it was arguably a career best, and certainly a firm step back in the right direction, when fourth of 11 in a Deauville Listed race (5f, good; Lesslepasser eighth) last month; needs more again but that's by no means unlikely.

stable_tour.quote: He works on Wednesday but at the moment the five-furlong race is the plan. I've always thought a lot of him and I think he'll turn out to be very good at this trip, though whether he's quite tough enough at this stage we will see. He's getting weight-for-age and we've seen this race fall to three-year-olds, including my own winner Equiano. I thought he ran well when he was third over six furlongs the other day because over that trip he needs to be covered up, otherwise he does too much too soon. He ended up following horses who didn't take him far enough into the race and found himself ten lengths adrift of the winner at one stage. He picked up really well and made up a lot of ground before paying for that late on, on a day when it worked out he wasn't drawn on the right part of the track. Back at five furlongs he won't need holding on to and I could see him running very well.  11-06-25

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.comment: Smashing third of 21 in G1 Commonwealth Cup (6f); drop down to 5f well worth exploring
runners.spotlight: Comfortably won a Chantilly Listed event (6f, good to soft; Estepona third) in early June and she took it up another notch with a smashing third of 21 in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup (6f, good to firm) at Royal Ascot 19 days later; didn't back that up in the 6.5f Maurice de Gheest last time but she shaped as though this drop down to 5f is well worth exploring; interesting.

quotes.quote: This is a filly we needed to switch off - we have no choice, Rayevka is still learning how to race. Mickael [Barzalona] took the ride clean; unfortunately we didn't have one to take her a little bit further, but she picked up very nicely and it is a very good performance to be third in this race. She's entered in the Prix Maurice de Gheest; she's a very nice sprinting filly - I have to see. She will definitely stay at this sort of trip, though. She might be able to do seven furlongs but she will have to switch off. I always liked her - I took her to the Cheveley Park last season because I always thought she was a talented filly, but mentally she was not ready. Her last run we decided to change tactics completely and drop her in behind and see what she could do, and she was very convincing that day. I still liked her so we came here, and I think it was worth it - Francis Graffard, trainer.

Rayevka has always worked like a very good filly but in her comeback run this year she pulled too hard and today the idea was to get her to drop the bit early on. She's a sprinter with a late turn of foot and it's great to see her confirm in the afternoon what she shows in the morning. Mickael Barzalona said he could have made all on her but we wanted her to learn something. She isn't entered in the Commonwealth Cup, so we'll have to have a think about what we do - Nemone Routh, Aga Khan Studs racing manager.

stable_tour.quote: She's under consideration to be supplemented for the Commonwealth Cup. She's taken her race at Chantilly very well and there aren't that many opportunities to run against her own age group. She's always been a very smart worker and she's a sprinter who can really accelerate. She's a very solid sprint-built filly but she must have good ground. We were very happy with her in the Prix Marchand d'Or because she pinged out of the stalls and then Mickael [Barzalona] managed to get her some cover and she dropped the bit. She could do well if she runs that way again.  11-06-25

racecards.verdict: Monteille may well be on the up for in-form Mario Baratti. However, classy 3yo filly RAYEVKA looks interesting on this drop to 5f.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Rayevka, 9/2 Monteille, 5/1 Ponntos, 6/1 Burhan, 13/2 Estepona, 8/1 Spartan Arrow, 25/1 Lesslepasser, 33/1 Ciao Pa'

runners.comment: 0-4 in Group races; won Listed race last November; might be flying a tad high here
runners.spotlight: 0-4 in Group races but he did win a Listed race at Newmarket (soft) by the skin of his teeth last November; finished fourth in all three starts this year, latterly in an 18-runner handicap (also 1m2f, good) at Glorious Goodwood; will need to put his best foot forward.

quotes.quote: The ground is key with Bolster. He's probably pounds ahead of himself on soft, even heavy ground. It makes such a difference. He stays so well. When he runs on good ground the speed horses just have that extra gear, but from three out today he was away and there was no stopping him. We'll speak to connections but it'll be likely one more or no more runs for him this year - James Cowley, trainer's assistant.
I'd say Bolster is a Group 3 horse - that's where we'll go, but he wouldn't want fast ground. We'll have a good look at an autumn campaign for him - Karl Burke, trainer.

runners.comment: 0-3 in Group races; won Listed race last November but got plenty on his plate this time
runners.spotlight: 0-3 in Group races but he also won a Listed race last November, this time at Lyon Parilly (1m4f, very soft); best run since when second in a similar event back at that track in May; has plenty on his plate.

runners.comment: Leading Japanese 3yo; won their Derby in June; on course for the Arc; fascinating
runners.spotlight: Looked the standout Japanese 2yo and he quickly bounced back from an odds-on second in the Japanese 2,000 Guineas when landing the Japanese Derby at Tokyo (1m4f, good to firm) in June; soft or heavy ground would raise a question but he's on course for the Arc in October and brings a fascinating element to this.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.comment: 4-4 until coming unstuck in G1 latest; Arc entrant who warrants respect back down in grade
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; unbeaten in his first four starts, culminating with success in the Group 2 Eugene Adam at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, good to soft; Dumonet fourth) in June; tasted defeat for the first time when racing outside his own age group and well held in the Group 1 Juddmonte International at York (1m2.5f, good to firm; 14-1) in August; that was an unsatisfactory affair, however, and this Arc entrant warrants respect back down in grade.

quotes.quote: Today wasn't straightforward because they went absolutely no pace and Daryz had to drop the bridle before showing proper acceleration. He's a very good horse who is up to Group 1 level and is progressing with every run, although where he goes next we'll have to wait and see. Straight after he won his Listed race we thought he was our horse for the Grand Prix de Paris, but he's done all his winning over a mile and a quarter so there seemed no need to stretch him out straight away. He's entered in the Arc and, while he's yet to encounter really soft ground, he might easily run in the race this year. He's bred to be very good; his dam and granddam were both Group 1 winners and he has five stakes-winning siblings, while he really takes after Sea The Stars. We've taken our time with him and Mickael [Barzalona] said he's taken another big step forward today - Nemone Routh, Aga Khan Studs' racing manager.

quotes.race: Prix Ridgway (Listed Race) (3yo Colts & Geldings) (Grande Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: You can see Daryz is a big, imposing horse so we haven't rushed him. He's always showed us he has quality and this was a good performance. The Grand Prix de Paris has been his target and today was all about telling us if we could go or not,  and he'll head straight there - Francis Graffard, trainer.

runners.comment: Imposing gelding; no mistake up to 1m2f latest; well worth his place in this field
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; imposing gelding who is 3-4, the only defeat coming when he got going too late in a Chantilly Listed race over 1m in June; made no mistake on the step up to 1m2f when making all for a commanding win in a Compiegne Listed event (heavy) last time; almost certainly has his best days in front of him and is well worth his place in this field.

runners.comment: Unbeaten thrice-raced 3yo; just on top in Listed race latest; holds G1 Champion entry
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; 3-3 this year, overcoming adversity to score on his debut at Wolverhampton (AW) in May before running out a convincing winner at Windsor (1m2f, good) the following month; took it up another notch when just coming out on top in the Listed Glasgow at Hamilton (1m3f, good) in July; mixed messages from that form since but hard to argue with an unbeaten 3yo who holds a G1 Champions Stakes entry.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Nahraan is a lovely horse. It wasn't very smooth, the pace was slow, then fast and he got lost going down the hill, which I expected, but I didn't want to let them get away from me and I moved into it a bit too early. I was lucky he held on in the photo and I'd imagine he'll come back to a mile and a quarter now. He's a big, good-looking horse with plenty of scope and hopefully he'll continue on this trajectory - Oisin Murphy, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.owner: Joel Seche, Free Man Racing & Jean-Pierre Vallee-Lambert
runners.comment: Came up short in Group 2s last 2 starts; unraced at two and might still have more to offer
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; won twice at Bordeaux Le Bouscat in the spring, including a Listed race in May, but he's come up short in Group 2s subsequently, including when behind Daryz in June; latest fourth in the valuable Guillaume d'Ornano could be considered a step forward, though, with last Sunday's Prix Niel winner just 3l ahead; might have more to offer.

racecards.verdict: All eyes on Croix Du Nord but the vote goes to powerfully built UTHRED, who surely has it all in front of him.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Croix Du Nord, 11/4 Daryz, 7/2 Nahraan, 9/2 Uthred, 12/1 Bolster, 16/1 Dumonet, 50/1 Daring Prince

racecards.race_name: Super Handicap de la Rentree (3yo+) (Grande Course) (Turf)

runners.breeder: A Chevalier Du Fau, Broadhurst Agency & Ecurie De Montlahuc
Haras D'Etreham, Runnymede Farm Inc & Ecurie Ades Hazan
R M Venn & C J Edwards

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
John Bell & Mme Susanne L Born

racecards.verdict: Loads to consider but the vote goes to ELIMAN, who showed improved form on his last two starts and is interesting on this h'cap debut.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/1 Eliman,  Olympic Goal, 7/1 Clavus,  Prince De Montfort, 8/1 Batal Youmzain,  Sabroso, 9/1 Stangheli,  Siyouking, 10/1 Etatique, 14/1 Johnny Hammer, 20/1 Zen Spirit,  Everillo, 25/1 Grey Tornado,  Melbora

runners.comment: Solid Group-race performer; won G3 over C&D in June; always respected back in this grade
runners.spotlight: Solid Group-race performer who scored for the fifth time at that level when narrowly beating Siam Paragon in the Group 3 Porte Maillot over C&D (good to soft; his second success in that race) in June; never figured in the Group 1 Jacques Le Marois on his only subsequent star; the sole penalty carrier but respected back in this grade.

quotes.quote: Great race. We rode King Gold to get the trip like we did the first time, he actually jumped a little bit slow today and must have thought he was going to go 2000 metres again I guess so I had to wake him up a little bit. I was never worried, he came good into the turn. The winner was too good but it felt like a win to us - Adrie de Vries, jockey.
We will go for the Godolphin Mile with King Gold now and maybe run on Super Saturday too - Marine Henry, assistant trainer.

quotes.race: Arc Prix Maurice de Gheest (Group 1) (3yo+) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: King Gold had a few issues when he was younger but I think spending the winter in Dubai really helped him mentally, and something has really clicked. His work has never been better than in the last few weeks. Live I thought he'd won but on the replay, I thought we were second. Then they said the number eight and it's just crazy - Nicolas Caullery, trainer.

runners.breeder: Haras De S A Aga Khan Scea
runners.comment: Scored impressively at Dieppe (7f) latest; could be improving and he's far from ruled out
runners.spotlight: Signed off with a Clairefontaine success last year and reappeared from a one year absence when scoring at Dieppe (7f, good to soft) five weeks ago; the form of those conditions-race wins needs improving on but he was impressive last time and deserves moving back up into Group company; player.

runners.comment: Rested since poor reappearance in March but would have leading claims if back to his best
runners.spotlight: Clear career best when waltzing off with a Curragh Group 2 last July, form that gives him strong claims here; creditable effort when fifth of 16 in the Group 1 Foret in October but he was a bitter disappointment on reappearance when last of nine in a Curragh Listed race (7f, good) in March; rested since.

quotes.quote: Poet Master is still immature and can be difficult to switch off, but they went a good gallop in front and it suited perfectly. I could get him switched off. I got to the front sooner than I wanted. but he was able to gallop to the line. The ground was perfect for him. I think there is more to come, he is maturing all the time and I think he will keep improving - Sam James, jockey.

quotes.race: Consign With The National Stud Handicap
quotes.quote: Poet Master has got a lot of potential and feels if he'll get a mile no problem. I think there's a Listed race at Haydock he could go for next - Sam James, rider.

Poet Master has got his own ideas about the job, he's still learning, he's still green and he probably wasn't in love with that ground. He'll probably be a very decent horse when he puts everything together. It will have to be black type now but the better the race, the better he'll   be. They went a good gallop there and he was always in his comfort zone - Sam James, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He got very tired in the slow ground on his return in the Gladness Stakes. He wants a bit of cut but he doesn't want it deep and holding like it was at the Curragh and you can put a line through that. He looks stronger than last year and will go for the Listed Spring Trophy at Haydock on May 10. Then, all being well, he'll go back there for the Group 3 John of Gaunt Stakes on May 31. 11-04-25

runners.comment: Essentially a very useful 7f handicapper who has a good bit to find at this level
runners.spotlight: Last time's close fourth of nine in a 6f Chester Listed event (good to firm) was right up with his best but he's essentially a very useful 7f handicapper who has a good bit to find at this level.

stable_tour.quote: He did well from three to four and has been in good form this season, winning at Chester's May meeting and beaten no distance at Newmarket last time. He's got an entry in the Wokingham but the Buckingham Palace over seven furlongs looks tailor-made for him. He wouldn't want it too firm and if he could just get his toe in, that should be fine. 13-06-24

He's a lightly raced colt who won over 7f first time out at Catterick this season. It was a rare runner for me there but it was the right race for him and he won like a nice horse. I didn't want to go to a big track and pick up a lofty handicap mark and that suited perfectly. I've given him an entry for the Jersey Stakes on Saturday, but he had a dirty scope last week and I‘ll have to speak to his owners before we decide whether to go. 21-06-23

runners.comment: Yet to win at this level but he's capable of really smart form; not dismissed
runners.spotlight: 6yo who is yet to make his Group-race breakthrough but he's capable of really smart form, such as when second to a high-class sort in a 1m Listed race here (soft) in May and when being narrowly denied by King Gold in a G3 over C&D (good to soft); bit below that next time but he's not dismissed.

runners.breeder: Al Shaqab Racing & Ecurie De Castillon

prev_owners.owner: Francois Le Corno, Al Shaqab Racing Et Al

runners.comment: Eighth in this last year and balance of form suggests there's plenty of work to do
runners.spotlight: Eighth in this last year; career best when winning a conditions event over C&D (good to soft) in July but only eighth of 18 when a short-priced favourite for a big handicap at Deauville (6f) next time; balance of form suggests there's plenty of work to do.

runners.breeder: Mme I Corbani & Sarl Jedburgh Stud
runners.comment: Fine 2nd of 15 in G1 last year; quiet in two spring C&D runs; remains of interest
runners.spotlight: Solid run of form last year included a Group 3 C&D (soft) success in May and a respectable fourth, when favourite, for this race last year; also finished a fine second of 15 in the Group 1 Maurice de Gheest in between; didn't match those efforts in two runs over C&D in the spring but rested since and she remains of interest.

quotes.quote: Exxtra spent the winter at Haras du Cadran with my good friend Pierre Talvard, and finished very well to be second on her return to action when not at 100 per cent. She goes on any ground but I was delighted to see the rain because she finds extra when it gets soft. She has grown up a lot and her long-term target is the Prix de la Foret back over this course and distance, but seeing how well she's done that she'll be prepared for the Prix Maurice de Gheest at Deauville in August - Carlos Lerner, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Stormed home to land 16-runner Vichy handicap latest; rapid improver; interesting
runners.spotlight: Followed up his win in a Saint-Cloud handicap (7f, good to soft) in June when storming home to land a 16-runner Vichy handicap (7f, soft) five weeks later; rapid improver who is well worth a shot in this company.

runners.comment: Solid efforts in first five starts of the year; form dipped latest; needs to bounce back
runners.spotlight: Solid efforts in her first five starts of the year included a reappearance Listed Chantilly win (5.5f, very soft) in March; form took quite a dip in Deauville Group 3 (1m, soft) one month ago, though; trip a possible excuse that day but needs to bounce back.

racecards.verdict: Plenty to consider but STOLEN KISS is firmly on the up and he can successfully graduate to Pattern company.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Poet Master, 4/1 Roshvar, 5/1 King Gold, 6/1 Siam Paragon,  Exxtra, 7/1 Polyvega, 8/1 Stolen Kiss, 16/1 Omicrone, 33/1 Divine Libra

racecards.race_name: Betwright Bet The Wright Way Apprentice Handicap

runners.comment: Well beaten after racing too freely on both stable starts; has a lot to prove
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden (including two bumpers) who was well beaten after racing too freely on first two starts for new stable (1m2f/1m4f); eased in grade today and should handle the ground but still has a lot to prove.

prev_owners.owner: D B Clark & A R Bentall

runners.comment: Coped far better than the others with heavy ground at Ffos Las on Tuesday; has 4lb penalty
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old but coped far better than the others with the heavy ground at Ffos Las (1m2f) on Tuesday, cantering clear to win easily by over 9l; commands respect under 4lb penalty here.

runners.breeder: Saleh Al Homaizi & Imad Al Sagar
runners.comment: Absent since substandard AW run in April but has 2-2 record on heavy ground
runners.spotlight: Absent since safely held on Tapeta in April but his penultimate AW run was a good effort and his 2-2 record on heavy ground brings him into each-way calculations.

prev_owners.owner: K Sohi Mrs I Corbani & Partners

runners.comment: Nine-time course winner; current mark is workable but he'd appeal more on better ground
runners.spotlight: Nine-time course winner, on ground ranging from firm to good to soft; still on workable mark after 3lb rise for last month's C&D success and encountered traffic when only ninth on heavy ground here 11 days ago but, nevertheless, he'd appeal much more on a quicker surface.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: I was expecting a good performance from Blue Hero, although the trip was a question mark. We thought he'd get it, but he's improved from a three-year-old. He's a little bit tricky in his head, but he's physically improved from last year. He likes it here, but doesn't have to stay here - it's coincidence we run a lot here. I was worried the ground would be on the soft side, but he's handled it well - Adrian Wintle, trainer.

runners.comment: Veteran who is much more prolific on AW; effective on soft turf but others appeal more
runners.spotlight: Much more prolific on AW but has a soft-ground C&D win on his CV; did not enjoy run of the race when sixth in 1m Chepstow classified in July, after a break, but still needs to build on that performance here.

runners.comment: Consistent at Ffos Las since returned to turf this summer; engaged 7.15 here last night
runners.spotlight: Returned to turf with four pretty good efforts at Ffos Las (1m-1m2f) this summer, the last three for current stable, and seems quite versatile regarding ground; due to run in 7.15 here last night; will warrant consideration if he contests this race.

runners.comment: Back in form last month and was a heavy-ground C&D winner last autumn; high on the list
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with two below-par runs in July but last month's Ffos Las second (1m4f, soft) was much better, and she was a heavy-ground C&D winner off today's mark last autumn; high on the list.

quotes.race: Celebrate @LeicesterRaces Christmas Parties Fillies' Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Bramble Jelly made hard work of it. At home she won't lead anywhere so all of her schooling has been when getting a lead and being upsides one and when no one wanted to make the running I sort of knew my fate as she was jamming the brakes on going down into the hurdles so I had to get the revs up high enough to get away with it. The dam won first time over hurdles at Fontwell and this one jumped well at home so we kept it simple - she had a freebie in front really - and I think she'll be a different horse with a lead - Ben Ffrench Davis, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

prev_owners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

runners.comment: Returned from layoff with quiet run last month but has no ground worries here
runners.spotlight: Won four times over hurdles (once on heavy ground) and once on the Flat during productive 2024; returned from layoff with a quiet run last month but has possibilities here if that outing brought him on.

runners.comment: Unplaced all 11 starts and has puny BHA rating of 34
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all 11 starts and has puny BHA rating of 34; needs this first run on turf slower than good to soft to unveil huge improvement.

runners.comment: Not disgraced on stable/hurdle debut but subsequently well beaten on Flat return
runners.spotlight: Disappointing maiden on the Flat in Ireland (seven starts); not disgraced on stable/hurdle debut in July but beaten 17l on the Flat since (albeit over 1m6f in better race than this) and difficult to enthuse over.

runners.comment: Placed in AW classified when upped to 1m2f month; claims if heavy ground suits her
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only six runs and looked suited by step up to 1m2f when placed in AW classified race last month; could have a say if suited by the forecast heavy ground here (unraced on turf slower than good).

runners.comment: Placed at 50-1 on AW last week; tried new trip-and-ground combination here
runners.spotlight: Placed for first time in 12-race career when 50-1 second at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last week but it's hard to predict whether this new trip-and-ground combination will be her cup of tea.

racecards.verdict: Zambezi Magic won easily on Tuesday but is opposed with BRAMBLE JELLY, who scored over C&D on heavy ground last autumn.
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 Zambezi Magic, 4/1 Bramble Jelly, 7/1 Bobby Dassler, 10/1 Blue Hero,  Stormy Lady, 12/1 Alioski,  My Brother Mike,  Daany,  Spinning Dancer, 25/1 Positivia, 33/1 The Dark Baron, 100/1 Tilsworth Silver

runners.comment: Shaped quite well on debut (7f) but soundly beaten twice since
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite well before fading into seventh on debut at Leicester (7f, good to firm) in June but soundly beaten twice since and subsequently gelded; wears first-time cheekpieces, moves up in trip and encounters much slower ground on this nursery debut.

stable_tour.quote: We thought he'd be a lot sharper than he has proved. We've given him a break and gelded him and we'll try to bring him back in the second half of September for a nursery over a mile on ground with a bit of give in it. Hopefully, off a mark of 53, we can get him winning. 20-08-25

runners.comment: Showed first glimmer of promise in her final qualifying run; up in trip for nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Struggled on first two outings (6f/7f) but last month's seventh of nine in a good Lingfield maiden (7f, good) was more encouraging, and further progress is possible if she handles the slower ground on nursery debut.

runners.comment: Only fourth of six when favourite for nursery debut but may yet do better
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify favouritism when only fourth of six on nursery debut at Lingfield (7f, AW; first-time cheekpieces) last month but could still have a bigger run in him if this very different test suits.

runners.comment: First two nursery runs (5f/6f, good) were respectable but this is a very different test
runners.spotlight: Started off in nurseries with two very respectable sprint runs on good ground but didn't fire at Brighton (5.2f, good to firm) a fortnight ago and it's hard to tell how well she'll cope with this very different test.

runners.owner: Mrs T Burns & Mrs S Loughnane
runners.comment: Close second on both nurseries, more recently on soft ground at Chepstow (1m)
runners.spotlight: Close second in both her nurseries, more recently when upped to 1m at Chepstow (soft) 12 days ago; only 1lb higher here and will be a tough nut to crack if aided by new cheekpieces.

runners.comment: 0-7 now but set very honest pace when clear second at Salisbury last month
runners.spotlight: 0-7 now but set very honest pace when clear second at Salisbury (1m, good to soft) last month, and it will be interesting to see whether Gina Mangan is very positive again on the slower ground here.

runners.comment: Beaten at least 23l when a big-priced outsider for his three qualifying races
runners.spotlight: Beaten at least 23l when a big-priced outsider for his three qualifying races (5f-7f); gelded last month and sports first-time blinkers this nursery debut; transformation needed.

racecards.verdict: David Loughnane's REJJIEN has made a solid start to her handicap career, and her proven ability on soft ground is a big selling point.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Rejjien, 11/4 Karakula Dancer, 7/2 Lady Lauren, 13/2 Isthatu, 10/1 Takeitorleaveit, 16/1 Amalfi Bluebell,  Lynxman

racecards.race_name: Betwright All Bun & Games Bath Summer Stayers' Series Final Handicap (Qualified Horses) (GBBPlus)

runners.comment: Safely held at York last time but won over 2m1f on heavy ground here last autumn
runners.spotlight: Only beat two rivals when scoring at Lingfield (2m, good to firm) in June and was safely held in deeper race the following month but last year's 2m1f course win on heavy ground proves she's well equipped for this test.

quotes.quote: Russian Rumour stayed really well. She kept going strongly, handles the surface and the race worked out well - Simon Walker, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: We took her to York last time because she'd been jumping out of her skin at home. It didn't go very well and she's been a bit sore since, so she'll probably return in the autumn. I doubt she'll carry on next year, but we'll have a go later in the season. She owes us nothing, though, and I think she's done incredibly well. 20-08-25

runners.comment: C&D winner in June and ran well on AW last month but untested on soft/heavy ground
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D (good) in June and was creditable third in Racing League event at Southwell (2m, AW) last month but has avoided previous engagements on soft/heavy ground (although has won on good to soft).

stable_tour.quote: He's bonny and wins in his turn. He won two starts ago at Bath over 1m6f but was disappointing when last of four at Lingfield on Saturday. That was over 1m3½f and he needs further, but he does tend to win one and then run disappointingly the next time. That's just him. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Went close on fast ground here (1m5f) last month but may find this race too taxing
runners.spotlight: Scrambled home by a short head at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) in July and has since gone close over 1m5f here (firm) but was below form when last in action and might be stretched by 1m6f on slow ground.

runners.comment: Four wins on fast ground this year; today's conditions are a serious worry, though
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive 4yo who has collected four fast-ground wins this year (1m2f-11.6f; twice here); didn't fire on good to soft ground last time, though, and today's conditions are a serious worry.

runners.comment: Won in good style here (11.6f, heavy) recently and still has low mileage; good chance
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won in good style here (11.6f, heavy) 11 days ago and is now 2-7; 9lb higher in a stronger race today but could easily have more to offer over the longer trip; needs to be taken very seriously.

runners.comment: C&D winner in July but form has dipped lately; first run on ground slower than good
runners.spotlight: Two wins this summer, latterly when awarded four-runner C&D (firm) contest; not yet fully exposed after only eight starts but his form has dipped lately and this will be his first run on ground slower than good.

runners.comment: Won gamely from the front here last month but conditions have probably turned against him
runners.spotlight: 9yo who had consistent spell over hurdles between his fast-ground Flat wins here in April (1m6f) and August (1m5f); does not have much margin for error off current mark, though, and conditions have probably turned against him.

quotes.quote: Man Of The Sea won well. Mr Mulholland said before the race he was in great form. He was a bit outpaced the last day at Windsor but he stays all day. He pinged the gates, I kept hold of him and he ran all the way to the line - George Rooke, rider.

quotes.race: Come Racing 11th November Nursery Handicap (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Man Of The Sea jumped and travelled, I was able to sit off the good pace and when I got stuck into him he saw it out to the line. He has plenty of experience and it stood to him. He did it well and hopefully there's another day in him - Billy Lee, rider

runners.comment: Needs to better recent efforts but is on a good mark and has run quite well on heavy going
runners.spotlight: 2-8 on AW but 0-17 on turf; needs to better recent efforts but is now 5lb lower than when close third over C&D (good) in June, and his only previous run on heavy ground (in 2023) was very respectable.

runners.comment: Four hurdle wins this year; flopped over C&D recently but might bounce back
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles (all on good ground but heavy also suits) for current stable since March; flopped when favourite for soft-ground C&D handicap 11 days ago but has usual visor refitted today; can bounce back.

quotes.quote: Tara Iti is bred to go on this testing ground. He travelled well despite being a little keen early. I took it up early enough, but he kept it up all the way to the line - Andrea Atzeni, jockey.

runners.comment: Slow-ground specialist; did not have race run to suit last time; still on very good mark
runners.spotlight: Slow-ground specialist; well below best form this season but did not have race run to suit his patient tactics when never-dangerous fourth over C&D (soft) this month and continues to lurk on a dangerous mark.

quotes.quote: Corsican Caper loves this type of ground, so he'll probably keep going through the autumn. He's not hard on himself and hopefully there's a bit of improvement in him. He'll cope with a bigger track as well and anything from a mile-six to two miles will suit him - Kevin Frost, trainer.

runners.comment: Won over 2m this summer but not at best lately and probably wants faster ground
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score off this mark at Chepstow (2m, good to firm) in July and also has a soft-ground success on his record but both runs last month were below par and conditions are probably not ideal for him today.

runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.comment: Veteran; runs this track well but below form this year; this looks too competitive
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is suited by this track and seems very versatile regarding trip/ground; soundly beaten over 2m1f here yesterday, though, and will probably find this race too competitive.

stable_tour.quote: He was unlucky at Bath last time when he got turned sideways. At least with Fact Or Fable you can semi-predict whenever he's going to win but old Uther is more unpredictable. He won three times in a week just before he turned eight. He's versatile at any trip from a mile and a quarter to two and a quarter miles. He enjoys it and pays his way every year, which is nice. I wouldn't mind a few more horses like him. I raced him in France a fair bit as a two- and three-year-old and he had won €98,000 in prize-money and was still a maiden. 04-07-24

racecards.verdict: Having waited all year for his favoured slow ground, well-handicapped 5yo CORSICAN CAPER was stymied in a tactical race here this month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Skimming Along, 6/1 Francesco Baracca, 7/1 Bohemian Breeze,  Man Of The Sea,  Tara Iti, 8/1 Corsican Caper, 10/1 Russian Rumour, 12/1 Offiah's Boy, 14/1 Fair Dinkum, 16/1 Black Smoke, 20/1 Iwantmytimewithyou, 66/1 Uther Pendragon

racecards.race_name: Betwright Safer Gambling Bath Summer Sprint Series Final Handicap

runners.comment: Progressive filly who with 3-7 strike-rate for new stable this year year (one win on soft)
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly with 3-7 strike-rate for new stable this year year; gained two of those wins on firm ground here (5.7f/5f) and the other over 6f on soft at Windsor; not yet proven on heavy but still a major player.

stable_tour.quote: She's unraced and was meant to run at Newmarket but banged herself in her box- she was a bit naughty. She'll probably run at Ascot this month. 08-05-24

runners.comment: Three fast-ground wins this season (two over C&D); others may handle conditions better
runners.spotlight: Three fast-ground wins this season, the last two over C&D; didn't run badly on soft ground at Sandown (5f) a fortnight ago and once ran quite well on heavy but others may handle today's conditions better.

runners.comment: Good 5f winner on good ground here in June; player if suited by today's conditions
runners.spotlight: Produced career-best performance here (5f, good) in June, travelling well before asserting inside final furlong; untested on ground slower than good to soft but could plausibly be seen to even better effect over today's slightly longer trip.

stable_tour.quote: She's in the 1,000 Guineas but disappointed us on her last start at Southwell. Her work had been really smart leading up to that, but she seems to have gone backward in her coat. It's been a bad spring with the weather, especially for fillies, so we're going to be patient. We like her a lot and she's shown plenty at home - we didn't put her in the Guineas for nothing. 17-04-24

runners.comment: Looked as good as ever when winning over bare 5f on heavy ground here 11 days ago
runners.spotlight: Probably best known as a Chepstow regular but looked as good as ever when fending off Secret Handsheikh to win five-runner race here (5f, heavy) 11 days ago; today's trip will also suit; one to consider.

quotes.race: In Memory Of Late Sylvia Taylor Handicap
quotes.quote: Savannah Smiles was brilliant and it was a really good win - she's blossomed this year! We bought her last summer and she didn't really do an awful lot, so we gave her a break and she's come back a different horse. She's kept her form all the way through the all-weather and I thought I'd give her another go on the turf and she's never looked back - Grace Harris, trainer.

runners.comment: Placed twice since last month's C&D win and seems versatile ground-wise
runners.spotlight: Placed twice on slow ground since last month's narrow C&D (firm) win, latterly when second to Savannah Smiles here (5f, heavy) 11 days ago; remains on a workable mark; probably won't be far away.

quotes.race: Get Your Ladbrokes Daily Odds Boost Handicap
quotes.quote: Secret Handsheikh was a convincing winner last time and went up 9lb but he loves this surface. He skips off the surface and has such a good attitude - Hollie Doyle, jockey.

runners.comment: Beat a next-time-out winner last month and also ran well at Sandown on Friday
runners.spotlight: Beat a next-time-out winner when scoring at Ffos Las last month and probably didn't have an ideal track position when fourth at Sandown (also 5f, soft) on Friday; respected if she makes a quick reappearance here.

quotes.race: DragonBet Proud Sponsors Of Ffos Las Racecourse Handicap
quotes.quote: Four Adaay is not as good as she was, but it's another win to her name and it's been a long time between drinks - Rod Millman, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's been racking up the seconds and has been very unlucky. She hasn't won for a long time and has deteriorated a little bit, but she's been very consistent. She's probably better at 5f than 6f and has dropped to a well-handicapped mark. Although she has kept finding one too good, I hope she can win a race before the end of the season. 27-08-25

runners.comment: Narrow winner of this race in 2024; quite consistent lately but may need better ground
runners.spotlight: Led close home to land this prize off the same mark last year (good ground) and has been fairly consistent in recent months but confidence in her would be higher on better ground.

quotes.quote: Vaunted tries her heart out. She didn't get a run last time, she wouldn't have won but she'd have been second - Nigel Tinkler, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: Won at Bath, so I'm really pleased and hopefully it's a sign we've a smashing bunch to look forward to. She came out of the race well. Whiles he is professional, she wasn't harried to be there and will progress. Early-season form is tricky to piece together, but she is a nice prospect. 22-04-21

They went a fair gallop when she won at Pontefract two starts ago and probably stopped in front of her. It might have made her look better than she was, but she's a lovely filly and proved that when only narrowly beaten at Newmarket on Saturday. I was very pleased with that. The track suited her on Saturday and, like many of mine, she's very genuine. We'll try to keep her in those fillies-only races over the summer. 27-07-22

runners.comment: Narrow winner at Brighton in May but below that level lately; unproven on slow ground
runners.spotlight: Came good at 14th attempt, with narrow success at Brighton (6f, good) in May, but recent efforts have been below that level and he's 2lb wrong today; first slow-ground run since 2yo debut.

runners.comment: Not beaten far on heavy ground here this month but has very modest strike-rate (1-33)
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when fourth of five in recent 5f race won by Savannah Smiles (heavy ground) but is effectively on the same mark from 2lb out of the weights today and has very modest 1-33 strike-rate.

quotes.quote: All Not Just Yet's form was on slow or soft ground so we thought the ground might be too quick. He was slowly away but got a good position. I probably got there too soon, he didn't do a whole pile in front but he is nice and done it well. Hopefully now he can step forward now having proven himself on that ground. - Adam Caffrey, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: Scott Dixon Racing , M Mahony , L Bond

runners.comment: Dual fast-ground winner this season; 2lb wrong for first run on ground slower than good
runners.spotlight: Dual 5f winner on fast ground this season (once here) but is 2lb out of the weights for this first encounter with turf slower than good, and others look stronger.

runners.comment: Course winner on heavy ground but needs to raise game after some below-par efforts
runners.spotlight: Led close home for fast-ground C&D win in June; effectively only 1lb higher from out of the weights today and also has a heavy-ground course win on his record but needs to up his game after some below-par efforts.

racecards.verdict: Progressive filly CINDY LOU WHO kept on strongly to score on soft at Windsor in July, and her other two wins this year were gained here.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Cindy Lou Who, 9/2 Asinara, 5/1 Four Adaay, 13/2 Savannah Smiles, 7/1 Secret Handsheikh, 10/1 Moe's Legacy,  Vaunted, 16/1 Not Just Yet, 20/1 Big Time Rascal,  Sisters In The Sky, 25/1 Banana

racecards.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.breeder: Mount Coote Estates & Mr T Petherick
runners.comment: 46,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to a useful 7f/1m winner; enters calculations
runners.spotlight: 46,000gns breeze-up 2yo; third foal; half-sister to 7f/1m winner Juantorena (RPR 98); dam French 1m1f-1m4f winner, half-sister to and Italian Listed-placed 6f-1m winner; stable has pretty good strike-rate with newcomers.

runners.comment: Shaped with some promise on debut but was very disappointing favourite last time
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a 5f Listed 2yo winner; shaped with more promise than bare form suggests when last of eight in valuable Newmarket maiden (6f, good to firm) in July but flashed tailed repeatedly and was very disappointing favourite at Lingfield (5f, AW) last month; tricky to weigh up.

runners.owner: Angelica Spence, A D Spence & I Madueke
runners.comment: Good third of 12 at Salisbury in June, albeit over 6f on fast ground
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly who posted a sound effort when third of 12 at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) in June, her third start; not seen again since but has strong claims here if the different trip-and-ground combination suits.

runners.comment: Always behind when 50-1 for debut at Ascot (5f) in May and not seen again since.
runners.spotlight: Always behind when 50-1 for debut at Ascot (5f, good to firm) in May and not seen again since.

runners.comment: Out of a Listed-winning half-sister to an Abbaye winner; market may guide
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to four winners including Glorious Caesar (9.4f/1m2f AW; RPR 81), Electric Love (5f; 77) and Charged Up (7f 2yo; 74); dam 6f and 7f (Listed) 2yo winner (103), half-sister to Abbaye winner Glass Slippers; well worth a glance in the betting before this debut.

runners.comment: Out of a fair 5f 2yo winner; hooded for debut; probably best watched
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners, out of a fair 5f 2yo winner; hooded for debut; probably best watched.

runners.comment: Ran well when third at Brighton a fortnight ago, albeit the form isn't anything special
runners.spotlight: Prominent for a long way when third of seven at Brighton (5.2f, good) a fortnight ago, her second start; that form isn't anything to go wild about but she might improve again if she handles the slower ground here.

runners.comment: Drops in trip after fairly respectable fourth over 7f on soft ground this month
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key debut (6f) when keeping on for fairly respectable fourth at Chepstow (7f, soft) 12 days ago; it's hard to predict whether this drop to 5f will pay dividends but her ability to handle soft ground counts for something.

racecards.verdict: She is untested on ground slower than good but SUPREME DIAMOND  looks the one to beat in this run-of-the-mill maiden.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Supreme Diamond, 7/2 White Island, 5/1 Arrabbiata, 6/1 Sharpness, 7/1 Lady Lucky Vegas, 16/1 Imthequeenofsheba, 20/1 Southern Spirit, 50/1 Legendofmilo

runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore
runners.comment: Kept on well for clearcut win on soft ground at Chepstow 12 days ago; must be considered
runners.spotlight: Doubled his win tally at Chepstow (5f, soft) 12 days ago, where he was always doing enough to repel the running-on Marcus; 5lb higher today but also has form on heavy ground and over C&D; must be considered.

stable_tour.quote: He was second at Bath the other day. He could end up being a little bit ground dependent. You wouldn't probably run him on anything with firm in it but he'll be a horse who'll make a name for himself as the year goes on. There's plenty more to come from him on ground with a bit of juice in it over six or seven furlongs. 04-07-24

runners.comment: Ffos Las winner in July; contender here if new cheekpieces have any positive effect
runners.spotlight: Just held on to open account in three-way photo-finish at Ffos Las (5f, good) in July, and both subsequent runs over that C&D (good/soft) were perfectly respectable; contender if first-time cheekpieces have any positive effect here.

stable_tour.quote: We've only got five or six juveniles. He's the one who's the most forward, the rest are a little bit slow to come to hand. He was fifth the other day when topweight in a nursery at Leicester behind Tees Aggregates. He ran okay but the ground was probably a bit quick for him. He's been fourth a couple of two-year-old before the season's end.times too and I'm pretty sure he'll win as a 07-08-24

runners.comment: Well beaten on both outings this summer and has a ground query today
runners.spotlight: Returned from six-month break with two very disappointing runs on good ground this summer (6f/7f) and was recently withdrawn from a C&D contest with heavy ground deemed unsuitable; others have much less to prove.

runners.comment: 0-26 now but ran on well for second behind Call Time at Chepstow (soft) recently
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 26 starts but has knocked on the door this season; ran on well for clear second behind Call Time at Chepstow (5f, soft) 12 days ago and, on that evidence, this stiffer track may suit him better.

runners.comment: 17-race maiden; not beaten far on latest outing but the form looks dubious
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden; not beaten far when third of five at Chepstow (5f, good) last month but that form is very tricky to evaluate (36-rated winner beat 1-12 favourite), and he's untested on ground slower than good to soft.

runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner last October but placed only once from nine runs this year
runners.spotlight: Effectively 11lb lower than when winning on heavy ground at Windsor (5f) last October but placed on only one of her nine appearances this year and evidently not as good as she was.

stable_tour.quote: She's probably one of our lower-rated horses but she's very genuine in her grade and she normally wins one or two races every season. She was only beaten a neck at Windsor last time and is very consistent. She gives her owner- who is very enthusiastic and who also bred her- a lot of fun. She'll run ten or 12 times a season and is always knocking on the door. 11-07-24

runners.comment: Placed over C&D in May but out of sorts lately and makes very limited appeal
runners.spotlight: Placed off today's basement mark over C&D (firm) in May but this regressive 6yo has been out of sorts lately and makes very limited appeal.

prev_owners.owner: Mrs T Burns And B R Millman

runners.comment: Without a win since 2022 but placed twice in recent weeks (once on soft); e-w claims again
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2022 but placed twice in recent weeks, latterly when third behind Call Time and Marcus at Chepstow (5f, soft) 12 day ago; also suited by this track; considered each-way.

runners.comment: 17-race maiden who managed only fifth in recent Chepstow handicap won by Call Time
runners.spotlight: Started off for Grace Harris with some encouraging runs on fast ground in May/June but has gone the wrong way since; only fifth of eight in recent soft-ground Chepstow handicap won by Call Time and now 0-17.

runners.comment: Well beaten all three starts; now makes handicap debut from basement mark
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on both 2yo starts (5f/6f) and also when reappearing in recent Windsor novice (6f, soft); makes handicap debut from a basement mark but could be considered only if attracting some market support.

racecards.verdict: Resurgent 4yo CALL TIME (nap) kept on far too strongly for Marcus and Coco Hill at Chepstow recently and can follow up.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Call Time, 4/1 Marcus, 11/2 Son Of Astar, 6/1 Coco Hill, 8/1 Bama Lama, 12/1 Off The Bench, 14/1 Rogue Endeavour, 25/1 King's Gem,  Tilsworth Ony Ta,  Happy Happy

runners.comment: Not disgraced when fifth of nine in 1m Salisbury handicap 16 days ago; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Brighton (1m) in August and not disgraced when fifth of nine in 1m Salisbury handicap 16 days ago; shortlisted.

quotes.race: Sapporo Japanese Premium Lager Brighton Mile Challenge Handicap (Brighton Mile Challenge Trophy)
quotes.quote: Uncle Dick did this last summer; he ran really well in the charity race and then followed it up with a big performance, just as he's done today. I was fairly confident coming into the race. I knew there'd be plenty of pace on, which would suit him. He came flying home, finding that extra gear he's been missing this year. He knows this course like the back of his hand, and I know what I've got and I know what he can do -Oliver Carmichael, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Carlsberg Danish Pilsner Brighton Mile Challenge Handicap (For the Brighton Mile Challenge Trophy)
quotes.quote: Uncle Dick is an absolute legend. He's won for all my apprentices either this year or last and just loves it at Brighton, he's just a different horse there. He'll have to go again on Thursday. If I don't run him in that there's not another race he can run in at Brighton this year so we might as well give it a go. He's such a good horse to have and the Brighton Mile is an amazing race to win. He's usually winning 2,500GBP races so it's nice to win one worth four times more - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

runners.comment: Finally got off the mark for his current yard over C&D last month; not taken lightly
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark for his current yard over C&D last month; up 6lb but he scored readily there so is not taken lightly.

runners.comment: Second but awarded race at Salisbury latest; remains unexposed at this trip and a player
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward on just his second go in handicaps when runner-up at Salisbury (1m) 16 days ago, hampered last 1f and awarded race; up 2lb but he remains unexposed at this trip; player.

runners.comment: Yet to fire in a trio of runs this term, beating only one at Newbury (1m2f) 17 days ago
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f juvenile winner but yet to fire in a trio of runs this term, beating only one at Newbury (1m2f) 17 days ago; he needs to take a big step forward.

runners.comment: Made it three wins in his last four starts over C&D 11 days ago; one to keep on right side
runners.spotlight: Made it three wins in his last four starts in C&D handicap 11 days ago; figures on a career-high mark now but he remains one to be interested in.

quotes.race: 'Phil Saunders Miracle 70th' Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Arjad has always been a bit too clever. We've dropped him in the last couple of times and he seems to really enjoy it. Before that we had him a bit handier, but he was a bit resentful - Rod Millman, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He's come back to form with a ­vengeance with two wins and two seconds in his last four runs. All of them have been at ­Chepstow and we've held him up, running him a bit ­differently. The first time he ran well over 7f, ­winning on his side, but the ­winner came and did him late on the ­opposite side of the track. We always thought he was a bit ­clever, but then he won his next two over a mile and seems to have turned a ­corner. On ­Monday he was ­second again in the £35,000 ­final of the ­Chepstow Mile Series. If he's in the mood, he's still ­potentially well ­handicapped. 27-08-25

runners.comment: Not disgraced when fourth at Southwell 20 days ago; considered on first go over 1m
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden who wasn't disgraced when fourth of ten in 7f Southwell handicap 20 days ago given he raced wide; in the picture on his first go over 1m off a slipping mark.

runners.comment: Good second in 1m Sandown handicap in July; needs considering after a break
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Chepstow (7f) in June and comes here on the back of a good second of six in 1m Sandown handicap following month; needs considering after a break.

runners.comment: Multiple course scorer; runner-up at Sandown 16 days ago; he must enter calculations
runners.spotlight: Multiple course scorer who got right back on track (in a refitted hood) when runner-up in 1m Sandown handicap 16 days ago; he must enter calculations off the same mark.

quotes.race: Download The Vickers.Bet App Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Let's say the Albus Anne can be a bit sassy and Georgia (Dobie) has done brilliantly on her - Fay Thomas, owner.

runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore
runners.comment: C&D winner; good second at Windsor 13 days ago; she can go well again nudged up 1lb
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in April who was refitted with cheekpieces when a good second of 12 in 1m Windsor handicap 13 days ago; she can go well again nudged up 1lb.

stable_tour.quote: Coulsty has been doing well and has produced a couple of nice two-year-olds. She's got plenty of size and scope like most of mine. She's only a couple of weeks off a run. The jockeys who have ridden her absolutely love her but she'll be your seven-furlong type of horse. 04-07-24

runners.comment: Not disgraced when sixth at Kempton 26 days ago; shortlisted off a 2lb lower mark here
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden who wasn't disgraced when sixth of 11 in 1m Kempton handicap 26 days ago; shortlisted off a 2lb lower mark here.

runners.comment: Consistent maiden for Tom Clover in 2024; needs to hit ground running for her new yard
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who signed off for Tom Clover with a solid fifth of eight in 1m Kempton handicap last August; subsequently changed hands for 4,600gns and she needs to hit the ground running for her new yard here.

runners.comment: Hooded for her new yard when beating just one in 7f Newmarket handicap 22 days ago
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and she was tried in a hood for her new yard when beating just one in 7f Newmarket handicap 22 days ago; others appeal more.

racecards.verdict: Lots with chances but Clive Cox's Salisbury scorer MUM'S ANGEL looks to have more to offer over 1m so can defy a 2lb weights rise here.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Mum's Angel, 9/2 Albus Anne, 6/1 Sant Alessio,  Ajrad, 8/1 Bizou, 11/1 Dapper Gee Gee, 12/1 Uncle Dick,  The Sweet Escape,  Thiscouldbefun, 14/1 Pivotal Days, 20/1 Princess Jastar, 25/1 Lady Kameko

racecards.race_name: I Love Julie Parkes Handicap (GBBPlus Race) (Div I)

runners.comment: AW might be blamed for latest start but he is now switched from cheekpieces to blinkers
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins (1m2f-1m4f) as progressive 3yo; no joy this term and he's been inconsistent, one of the better efforts when a close second from the front under Fred Daly in small field; AW might be blamed for latest start but connections also now switch from cheekpieces to blinkers.

runners.comment: Won easily at Salisbury (1m2f) in July; way below form on both starts since
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably sole start here (2024); snapped a losing sequence when easily winning a six-runner handicap at Salisbury (1m2f) in July; way below form on both starts since, though.

runners.comment: Best when close third over C&D latest; hooded first time that day and came home well
runners.spotlight: Made debut in May and fair form for minor honours on all six starts, best when third of eight in handicap over C&D (good to firm) on latest; hooded first time that day and came home well from last place, beaten less than 1l, so there's hope he can build on that.

prev_owners.owner: Qatar BS, China Horse Club & D Howden

runners.comment: Ran well last two starts but does not look the best handicapped; back from ten weeks off
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins from December to April and his form has been just as good on turf (good to firm) on last two outings; however, he does not look the best handicapped; returns from ten weeks off.

runners.comment: Won at Newbury (1m2f) in July and has continued to run with credit; place claims
runners.spotlight: Won at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) in July and has continued to run with credit, including one run on good to soft; place claims.

runners.comment: Won when upped to 9.4f (AW) 12 days ago; perhaps this further step up in trip will help
runners.spotlight: Has not progressed on turf (0-9; unraced on softer than good); however, seen mostly at about 7f and he raised his game to win at Wolverhampton (AW) 12 days ago when trying 9.4f; back up 3lb but perhaps this further step up in trip will help.

runners.comment: Raised his game upped to 1m2f for last three starts; clearcut win here on second occasion
runners.spotlight: Hard to settle and has worn a hood for last four outings; raised his game, though, with the move up to 1m2f for last three starts, coming from off the pace, including for a clearcut win here (good; unraced on softer) on penultimate occasion; 6lb higher this time but he's in calculations.

runners.comment: Won by nearly 3l at Epsom (1m2f, good) three weeks ago for a career best; back up 4lb
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with a 1m2f nursery win on soft; rather mixed this term but winning by nearly 3l at Epsom (1m2f, good) three weeks ago was a career best, sticking to his task very well up the near rail; back up 4lb but a serious player if that latest form is reproduced.

runners.comment: Unconvincing for this handicap debut but a bit too early for the debut run to be forgotten
runners.spotlight: Fair form at Newbury (1m, good) in April on debut but soundly beaten both starts since, 20-1 at Sandown last time when trying 1m2f (there are stamina influences in his pedigree); unconvincing for this handicap debut but it's a bit too early for the debut run to be forgotten.

runners.comment: Gelded now; needs improvement on this handicap debut but he is bred more for this trip
runners.spotlight: Fair form in three novice/maiden events over 7f/1m, best effort on second start (AW); gelded since latest; needs improvement on this handicap debut but he is bred more for this trip.

runners.comment: Off for 11 weeks; form case can be made but others have more pressing claims
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in a small field last August (1m2f, heavy); prominent in betting when switched from cheekpieces to blinkers for last two starts, running respectably on first occasion but meeting traffic problems on latest; off 11 weeks; form case can be made but others have more pressing claims.

racecards.verdict: Stormy Monday and Forest Caper are key contenders but the vote goes to TELEPATHIC.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Stormy Monday, 9/2 Telepathic, 5/1 Arbitration, 6/1 Forest Caper,  His Finest Hour, 10/1 Bluecoltrane, 12/1 Patrol, 14/1 Papagei, 16/1 Crystal Mariner,  Lola Moon, 20/1 Solar Bentley

racecards.race_name: I Love Julie Parkes Handicap (GBBPlus Race) (Div II)

runners.comment: Won from off the pace at Pontefract (1m) and Doncaster (1m2f; career best) last two starts
runners.spotlight: Probably acts on good to soft; came from off the pace to score at Pontefract (1m, good; back to form) and Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm; a career best) on last two outings; the latter was from Whiskey Pete and today's 1.20 runner Forest Caper; up another 2lb but he's been on a roll.

quotes.quote: I've been saying to the owner that Project Geofin stays and I was proving the point. He let me have my own way and he's a stayer who'll get further - he'll get a mile and a quarter or a mile and a half. You can ride a race on him at this trip but you can't at seven furlongs - Geoff Oldroyd, trainer.

runners.comment: Out of form this year; has now changed trainers again and cheekpieces return
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins (on good, soft and heavy) from the front for Eve Johnson Houghton; down the field on all three starts for Kevin Philippart de Foy this February-May and has now changed trainers again; the cheekpieces which went on for his final win last year now return for the first time since.

quotes.race: British EBF Ruby Anniversary Nursery Handicap (Sire And Dam Restricted Race)
quotes.quote: High Point ran a nice race at Windsor last time and we were quite confident coming in as he is a galloping type. He could get a mile and three quarters next year - George Downing, rider.

runners.comment: 1st and 2nd over 1m2f lately and the Haydock race ten days ago did not pan out ideally
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; 3yo who has won two of his six handicaps, including when upped to 1m2f at Ayr on penultimate start (travelled strongly before asserting in tidy fashion); Haydock race ten days ago did not pan out ideally and he probably had no chance with the winner anyway, but he beat the other 12; same mark today and he remains one to be interested in.

stable_tour.quote: A big scopey horse who made his debut last month. He tends to be a bit keen, so we rode him cold and he encountered traffic problems. He has the potential to win his maiden and be a decent horse later in the season. 14-04-25

runners.comment: Won by a head at Goodwood (1m2f) last time but it is at that track that she has excelled
runners.spotlight: Made a belated reappearance last month and won by a head at Goodwood (1m2f, good to firm) eight days later, gamely making most; up 2lb today but the chief concern is that she has excelled at Goodwood for last three wins.

runners.owner: David Kilburn, Mrs Corbett, Chris Wright
runners.comment: Six races, winning 1m2f handicaps on good at Nottingham and Newbury on last two
runners.spotlight: Six races, winning 1m2f handicaps on good at Nottingham and Newbury on last two; latter was at 4-9 under a penalty after eight-day break, when ridden by Archie Young, with a much smaller margin of victory but the first two drew clear; up another 3lb but could still be capable of better.

prev_owners.owner: Anthony Ramsden, Nigel Skinner & Ron Hull

runners.comment: Close second to Project Geofin over C&D last month, in an otherwise grim season
runners.spotlight: Close second to Project Geofin over C&D last month off a 1lb lower mark; chance on that but he's been out with the washing on his other seven starts this season, including six days ago on his second AW start.

quotes.race: Play Coral "Racing-Super-Series" For Free Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: It was testing and we were scratching our heads in the final stages as we hadn't got the job done yet, fortunately he got his head in front. I thought I was going to win easily two furlongs out, but Whiskey Pete was waiting the whole last furlong and went away when one came to him - Hector Crouch, jockey.

runners.comment: So nearly won two in a row when back up to 1m2f, checked at a crucial point last time
runners.spotlight: Opened his account at Newmarket (1m2f, good to soft) in July and beaten a nose at Sandown (1m2f, soft) 16 days ago; checked at a crucial point in the latter and 3lb rise probably underestimates him; major player.

runners.breeder: Al Rabban Racing/Lope De Vega Syndicate
runners.comment: Three wins this season and eyecatching on last two starts, but all over further than this
runners.spotlight: Very hard to knock since joining Sam England in the spring, including with three 1m4f wins; often contested amateurs' races of late, catching the eye when finishing close up at Catterick (his effort was left too late) and Haydock (failing to get run of the race) on last two outings; they were also over a bit further than this, however.

quotes.race: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (For Riders With 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
quotes.quote: I don't think what's happened has kicked in quite yet. I got a slow break away, but Made All travelled really nicely and sort of settled me down in a way. Then I waited and waited - Rebecca Hitchen, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: 3yo whose last four races have yielded two wins and two seconds; needs plenty of respect
runners.spotlight: 3yo whose last four races have yielded two wins and two seconds, all when held up and he seems effective at 1m2f and 1m4f; the first two of those races were on turf (good to soft/good) and the last two on AW, winning by a head with strong finish in a Racing League race at Southwell last time; raised another 5lb but needs plenty of respect.

quotes.quote: I gave Say What You See a very poor ride. When I turned in [to the home straight] I wheeled him out into the middle of the track on his own and, for a young horse, I should have kept him in amongst them. He's a good type of horse, he's still learning the job and he's not fully furnished yet and he'll be an even better four-year-old. I should have kept him with a bit of cover out of the headwind but luckily enough he won - Billy Garritty, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Going beyond 1m for first time needs to turn things around dramatically; dam 1m5f winner
runners.spotlight: Won 7f AW maiden last October for Charlie Hills but beat only two rivals this season across his first four handicaps; going beyond 1m for first time needs to turn things around dramatically but his dam was a 1m5f winner.

runners.comment: Made a creditable stable debut in June but heavy defeats on both starts since
runners.spotlight: 0-4 for Jessica Harrington; third of six at Haydock (10.5f, good) in June on stable debut was creditable but heavy defeats on both starts since.

racecards.verdict: Some strikingly in-form contenders may be led home by SOMETHING SPLENDID who looked so unlucky at Sandown 16 days ago.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Nightsinwhitesatin, 5/1 Project Geofin,  Something Splendid,  Say What You See, 13/2 Obito, 8/1 Pink Lily, 9/1 Made All, 20/1 High Point,  Whiskey Pete, 40/1 Jodhpur Blue,  Broadstone

runners.comment: Group 3 win at two didn't help his handicap mark and this term is turning into a struggle
runners.spotlight: Group 3 win last season didn't help his handicap mark and this season is turning into a struggle, with this headgear combination tried last time at Chester.

quotes.race: Tattersalls Stakes (Registered As The Somerville Tattersall Stakes) (Group 3) (Colts And Geldings)
quotes.quote: Oisin Murphy just got off and said, 'Is he in the Dewhurst?'. But I expect I've made the most terrible balls [up] of it. The Waco Kid did have an entry but I think after he was only third at Listed level I took him out of the Dewhurst and the Lagardere. There's the Criterium International, so we might go there as he handles soft ground but we'll see. The Breeders' Cup is also an option as well. What's so lovely about this horse is that with every run he's got better - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He hasn't changed a vast amount physically from two to three but his work has been pleasing and he'll be ready early. I'd imagine he'll start in the Craven, because he's shown a liking for Newmarket. He's in the 2,000 Guineas, as well as the French and German Guineas. He strikes me as more of a solid Group 3 horse than a potential Group 1 horse at this stage. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Ground was soft for Sandown win so could do with the forecast showers arriving in time
runners.spotlight: Only real blip this season came in the Britannia at Royal Ascot; now on a career-high mark after a 3lb rise for winning narrowly at Sandown (7f, soft) and could do with the forecast showers arriving in time; fair shout if they did.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a cracker at Chester last week, just getting touched off by El Burhan, and seemed to handle the track well. He's been doing a lot of his training with Harvard Sound as they're similar types. He's in good form. 14-05-25

runners.comment: Easy winner of his last two races and he's dangerously unexposed now into a handicap
runners.spotlight: Both turf runs over 7f on good ground; untroubled to win at Newmarket and Haydock (early July), the latest a weak affair at long odds-on (gelded beforehand); posted a smart RPR last time and he's dangerously unexposed now into a handicap.

runners.comment: Useful 2yo but has finished well down the field in 7f/1m handicaps this term
runners.spotlight: Lots to like about his profile at two (6f/7f) but has finished well down the field in 7f/1m handicaps this term, latterly tried in cheekpieces.

quotes.race: Juddmonte British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A/B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)
quotes.quote: Tiger Mask did well. We thought he'd run a good race as the form of his last run is working out very well. He wasn't doing a lot in front, he was looking at the stand and pricking his ears a bit so I do think he'll improve again - Pierre-Louis Jamin, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He hasn't run this season but he's getting there now and could make his return in the Buckingham Palace. He had some good form last season. 12-06-25

runners.comment: Firmly in the mix on the strength of his latest fourth in a strong handicap at Goodwood
runners.spotlight: Last year's two wins were at 6f (one on good to soft) but this is looking his trip after competitive shows in strong handicaps at Sandown and Goodwood; it was a deep race last time and two of the three ahead of him dominated an Ascot handicap last weekend; firmly in the mix.

quotes.race: Jenningsbet Britain's Biggest Independent Bookmaker EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Defence Minister is a nice horse who has shown some nice ability in the mornings. It was not easy to say he was going to win first time out as this was a nice education day for him, and hopefully his next race will show what he can really do. He was slowly away, but Saffie [Osborne] gave him all the time he needed to catch up and we've beaten the second favourite today which is good. It's not easy to put him in a novice race, but next but we'll give him plenty of time and it could be that we go seven furlongs with him next time - Hamad Al Jehani, trainer.

runners.comment: Newbury winner in April; close fifth at Goodwood after a break; arrives here fresh
runners.spotlight: Confined to good or quicker ground since finishing second on good to soft on last season's debut; easily a career best when winning by 2l at Newbury in April (7f; 6lb lower) and had another lengthy break prior to finishing a respectable fifth at Goodwood; arrives here a fresh horse and wouldn't be exposed just yet.

runners.comment: Won two minor races before enduring a troubled run when 13-2 for a warm handicap
runners.spotlight: Won two minor events in May (7f; good to firm/good) and found some trouble in running when 13-2 for a big-field handicap at Glorious Goodwood (good to soft); easy to give him another chance off this mark.

quotes.race: Download Fitzdares New World-Class App Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: El Matador went to Nottingham and we were expecting him to win. He just got tired that day on very testing ground and he was beaten by a very decent animal in Falconer. I'm not entirely sure what the team want to do with him, but having won over seven furlongs opens up a few more possibilities because he looked like he might be a bit of a galloper last year. For him to have got the job done over what we knew would be shorter than ideal, and on quick ground, gives us a few options - Hector Crouch, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Strike-rate of 3-8 but at sprint trips and whether he wants 7f remains to be seen
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 3-8, with one of those wins on good to soft; endured a wide trip when placed at Chester last time but was beaten 4l and whether he wants 7f remains to be seen (confined to sprinting thus far).

runners.comment: Useful 2yo; made a winning reappearance and there were excuses last time
runners.spotlight: Finished in close proximity to smart horses as a 2yo and made a successful reappearance (having been gelded) at Newmarket in June (7f, good to firm; 4lb lower); finished last at Ascot two months ago (1m) but got upset in the preliminaries and raced freely; wouldn't be exposed just yet; has won on good to soft but disappointed on soft.

runners.comment: Progressed well this season and good reason to believe he can improve on last two efforts
runners.spotlight: Nursery winner at two but a sharp improver this season with two wins including one over C&D; needed a stronger pace when his hat-trick bid came up shy and the drop back to 6f found him when tried in cheekpieces (now removed) at the Shergar Cup; remains of interest off this sort of mark.

quotes.quote: I'm absolutely delighted. Up The Pace is best on softer ground but he seemed to handle the quicker surface so that opens up a few more options. He was probably only 80 per cent fit so there's a bit to work on still - Edward Smyth-Osborne, trainer.

runners.comment: Latest form looks better now but this still demands his best form yet off 5lb higher
runners.spotlight: Didn't appear to have all that much in hand at Newmarket (good to soft) or Southwell (again 7f; AW) but the runner-up last time has franked the form in an Ascot classified race; this still demands his best yet off a 5lb higher mark.

quotes.race: Best Ticket Deals Online At leicester-racecourse.co.uk Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We've always liked Rogue Diplomat and he's shown so much at home. We tried him over too far at Chelmsford last year and we're delighted to see him win. He's a different horse this year. James is such a good trainer and has done a great job with him - Tony Elliott, founder of the Rogues Gallery, owners.

runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on as expected since promising plenty at two; stamina to prove at 7f
runners.spotlight: Ran in the Gimcrack after his winning debut at two; 0-5 this campaign and wasn't overly convincing with his finishing effort when close up at Chester and likewise with his stamina upped to 7f at Goodwood; others appeal more.

stable_tour.quote: He's a high-class horse. He'll have an entry in the Norfolk Stakes as well ? Steve Parkin [owner whose horses run under the Clipper Logistics banner] was keen to put him in, in case it's a weak race ? but for me the Coventry should be the race. His half-sister Dramatised won the Queen Mary there and the track should hold no fears for him. He looks very exciting. 10-06-24

runners.comment: Sole defeat came on heavy ground in last season's Group 2 Mill Reef; brings potential
