runners.spotlight: Sole defeat came on heavy ground in last season's Group 2 Mill Reef; made all at Kempton 18 days ago (7f novice) and the front two came well clear; the runner-up has since let that form down but this colt clearly brings potential into handicaps.

runners.comment: Nursery winner off 5lb lower; will need to come on plenty for his recent Ascot run
runners.spotlight: Sole win in six came in a big-field 7f nursery at York last August off 5lb lower; below that level in his subsequent races but he might strip fitter for the recent Ascot run.

quotes.race: sensory-junction.co.uk Autism Awareness EBF Stallions Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: I was tempted to run Angel Hunter in the sales race earlier on the card, but it looked like a big step. He's a lovely horse and has a few nice entries, including in the sales race at Doncaster, but he's a horse for next year.

runners.comment: Only 1-10 but running better this season than his form figures may suggest
runners.spotlight: Only 1-10 but running better this season than his form figures may suggest and contested a Group 2 on his penultimate run; he's not without hope off a reduced mark, provided conditions don't soften up.

stable_tour.quote: He finished third in the Richmond Stakes last year and fifth in the Doncaster sales race, three places behind the French Derby winner. He had a couple of niggly issues through the winter, but ran a good race on his comeback at Newcastle, where he met all the traffic - otherwise he'd have been in the four, at least. He hit the line well and is one to keep on the right side of. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Absent since early April but has the form to feature if on his game after a break
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in six races last season (all bar one at 7f), proving himself worthy of this sort of mark; absent since finishing third to Back In Black at Newbury in April but has the form to feature if on his game.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (IRE Incentive Race) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: I'm relieved Spell Master didn't come second like the others! He's a grand horse who's improving with every run and should keep progressing. Kamekos' are very similar and seem to thrive on their racing and try very hard - Andrew Balding, trainer.

runners.comment: Responded well to cheekpieces when tried once last season and he could be a big price
runners.spotlight: Won twice last season; reappearance was encouraging, then ran at Royal Ascot before finding trouble in running when close up at Chester; easy to forgive last time as well given that was his AW debut and he finished lame; has a bit to prove over this far as peak form is at shorter, but responded well to cheekpieces when tried once last season and he could be a big price.

quotes.quote: I don't think everyone would've won on Brighton Boy and he wasn't concentrating on what he should've been on. He's a big horse and we'll get our heads together as he's got a pretty bright future ahead of him - Anna Lisa Balding, wife of trainer.

runners.comment: Newbury was workmanlike and he's 4lb higher in a deeper race this time
runners.spotlight: Debut winner last year and has gradually found his form this campaign, doubling his tally when trying 7f for the first time at Newbury (good; unraced on slower); that was no more than workmanlike and he's 4lb higher in a deeper race.

runners.comment: Dual 7f winner but was fine back over 6f when winning a Class 2 at Brighton recently
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; an upwardly mobile 3yo who is a dual winner at this trip and had too much class for mostly older opposition back over 6f at Brighton two weeks ago; raised 4lb but looks a good bet to give his running.

racecards.verdict: Loads with chances. Defence Minister has to be on the shortlist but there's reason to believe BRIGHTON BOY might outrun his odds.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Indian Springs, 6/1 Back In Black, 8/1 Sarab Star, 9/1 Defence Minister, 10/1 God Of War, 12/1 Pietro,  El Matador,  Spell Master, 14/1 Morte Point, 16/1 Up The Pace,  Rogue Diplomat, 20/1 Showering, 22/1 Lakers, 25/1 Andesite,  Angel Hunter, 28/1 Brighton Boy, 33/1 The Waco Kid, 40/1 Intrusively, 50/1 Tiger Mask

runners.owner: Venice Consulting S A & Gilberto Gambini
runners.comment: Fairly encouraging 5th of 9 last month on belated reappearance and he could build on that
runners.spotlight: He's run just once this year but that was a fairly encouraging performance when fifth of nine at Chepstow (7f, good to soft) last month; he could build on that and he's on a competitive mark on last season's best efforts over 6f and 7f; effective in big-field handicaps and not ruled out.

quotes.race: Sky Bet City Of York Stakes (Group 2)
quotes.quote: I was very pleased with Vafortino. He's a tough horse. We decided to keep him away from handicaps after the Wokingham and it's paying off. He's a bit better with more juice in the ground. It was the plan from quite a long way out to come to York for the prize money. He runs very well fresh - Kevin Phillipart de Foy, trainer.

That was a worry today - it can get a bit messy today in these big fields and Kevin told me that Vafortino can take a bit of time to warm to the task so I was trying to look for a bit of room at the top of the straight. Thankfully, he got me out of trouble - James Doyle, jockey.

We knew Vafortino was a good horse and he ran in some very good races last year. He travelled well and we knew he was going to have a chance at the end as he stays further than this trip. I think there are more good races for him - Kevin Philippart de Foy, trainer.

quotes.race: Buy Online At Irish Yearling Sales Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Vafortino was a bit keen early on but he stuck at it well. He's still a bit babyish and ran a bit green late on. Shane said he galloped last the line and took a bit of pulling up so he could be smart - Brendan Powell, assistant trainer

stable_tour.quote: He ran well on his comeback at Gowran Park and he will mix and match between stakes races and good handicaps. 19-04-21

runners.comment: Third in the Wokingham and fourth in the Bunbury Cup; could still have more to offer
runners.spotlight: Four-time 7f winner who was a close third of 28 in the Wokingham at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) when wearing a first-time tongue-tie; fourth in the Bunbury Cup since (7f, good to firm); he could still have more to offer and is a possible player if conditions suit (unproven on slow ground).

quotes.quote: Ten Pounds has run huge, first time back at six [furlongs]. Trevor thought he had the winner in his sights two down. I think in a normal year horses do stop - it's hard to make the running here. I suspect looking at the Jersey, they're not making up ground quite as much. Some of the jockeys were suggesting it's just a bit loose from watering. He's run huge, very happy with him - Harry Charlton, trainer.

runners.owner: Doug Graham, Ian Davison, Alan Drysdale
runners.comment: Hasn't won for just over two years but went close in a big field at York three weeks ago
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for just over two years and whether the extra half-furlong suits remains to be seen, but he kept on well for a head second of 20 at York (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago and that performance bodes well for a prominent showing today; versatile ground-wise.

quotes.quote: Commanche Falls has been better than ever. He did well to win at Newbury because the ground was against him and Ireland last time came too soon, but the horse that beat him [Moss Tucker] just won the big sprint [Flying Five]. He probably ran better than we thought there. He's in a few races, including at the Curragh again, but the ground is important to him. He won't run a lot more this season as he's done so well - Michael Dods, trainer.

Between the three and the two-furlong marker I felt like I was getting a bit stuck in the ground really. Commanche Falls does race behind the bridle but normally you can keep your momentum going, whereas today I just felt like I wasn't getting any momentum. There wasn't much room and I just had to switch him to get him to think again and, once he did that, he really put his head down and I thought he did it nicely in the end - Conor Beasley, jockey.

quotes.race: Jebel Ali Racecourse & Stables Dash Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: I was very confident. On his last run Commanche Falls was third behind some smart fillies at York, though this ground would be plenty quick enough for him. If he's off the bridle at halfway, you know he's going to come home strong. Once he met the rising ground and I gave him a dig he went about his job really well - Connor Beasley, jockey.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Ayr Gold Cup Handicap (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: Commanche Falls has run a blinder but Connor (Beasley) said they didn't carry him far enough, he's travelling so much better in his races. But it was a super run with the weight and I'm delighted with him, he'll probably go in a Listed race now - Michael Dods, trainer.

I was a little behind the bridle but I needed to keep tabs on the front ones. The race fell apart in front of me but when I felt Hollie coming to me I felt Commanche Falls go again and really stick his head down. I sat on him on Wednesday and I was very happy with him coming into this and I think he's the sort of horse who just comes to light at this time of year and it's a great training performance to bring him back to win this again - Connor Beasley, jockey.

Commanche Falls is just a horse who has got better and better with each run. Every time he comes to a gig he keeps putting up his best performance ever. The ground was a question mark today but I knew once I got him rolling, he'd pull out all the stops - Connor Beasley, jockey.
We always thought Commanche Falls wanted a bit further but he's done it again over this trip. He has a good mind and got the job done - Connor Beasley, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a great servant. The big handicaps suit him because he needs a proper gallop in a race and needs to be off the bridle. He won a Group 3 at Newbury and two Listed races last season but they weren't exactly great races for the level and so he got away with the step up from handicaps. This year, though, they've been tough races. We had him entered in the Stewards' Cup but the problem was that when the weights came out he was on a mark of 110. However, by the time of the race he had dropped to 107 and we felt there was no point running him off a mark 3lb higher than his actual rating, so that's why we missed the race.  He ran on Saturday at York, finishing a great third behind Elmonjed. I've entered him in the Beverley Bullet on Saturday but I'm not sure whether he'll run yet. There are other races coming up at York and there's the Ayr Gold Cup. 28-08-24

runners.breeder: Hellwood Stud Farm & R C Dollar
runners.comment: Down to a dangerous mark and his best form has come on slow ground; interesting
runners.spotlight: Placed in Group 3/Listed races on slow ground last term; he hasn't hit the same heights this season but encounters with a slow surface have been scarce and he's down to a dangerous mark; interesting; rain would aid cause.

quotes.quote: When I rode Purosangue the last day, he bumped into a really nice horse of Karl's (Burke, trainer). I was very confident he'd stay the six and he stayed well. He was a willing partner who has been so consistent all year and deserved to finish off the year on a high note. He wouldn't be the biggest but he's solid. He's got great mental strength and I'd be confident he'd train on" - P J McDonald, jockey.

We're just thrilled Purosangue is back on form - he created a very good impression first time out, and we just thought he did things the wrong way round at Newmarket. That was much more like it, and he was beaten by a very good horse. He won on pretty good ground at Haydock so I think he's quite versatile. He looks like he'll be made for the Roses at York - Andrew Balding, trainer.

runners.comment: Went very close in the Lincoln here in March and things didn't go his way on AW last month
runners.spotlight: Went very close in the Lincoln here (1m, good to soft) in March and mid-division in the Racing League at Newcastle (7f, AW) last month, when held-up in a race in which the first three raced prominently; cheekpieces return (blinkers were tried last time); capable of having a say.

quotes.quote: Oliver Show has run a massive race. Huge credit to Charles Eddery and Chas Robinson who brought him back from Bahrain. He's a progressive horse - I thought he was maxed out where he is but he looks like there may be a little bit left. He goes well fresh and I'd say we'll probably work back from the Royal Hunt Cup - George Boughey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: I'm leaning towards the Buckingham Palace at the moment. He's a strong-travelling horse who should really be suited by Ascot. The plan had been to go to the Victoria Cup if it didn't work out in the Lincoln, but he ran so well in that and was an unlucky loser, so we thought we'd wait for the royal meeting. 09-06-25

runners.comment: Has been keeping on well in competitive 6f h'caps; this trip could be right up his street
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has been beaten less than 1l in competitive 6f handicaps on his last three starts, most recently third of 20 at York; he's kept on well each time and today's extra yardage could be right up his street; unraced on slow ground.

quotes.race: BetUK. It's Where The UK Bets Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That went to plan really. Very smooth, very simple A to B. When I gave him a squeeze Pocklington put the race to bed very well. I thought Hollie (Doyle, jockey on runner-up) would make it but I didn't want it to turn into too much of a sprint because he hit a bit of a flat spot the last day. I thought the quicker we get through the gears and striding out, the more chance I've got. He's an exciting horse - Harry Russell, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Not easy to fancy on this year's form but well handicapped and rain would be a positive
runners.spotlight: He's not easy to fancy on this year's form but is well handicapped on last October's third in a Group 3 at Ascot (6f, soft) and significant rain would be a major boost to his claims.

quotes.quote: Russet Gold is a classy horse, a half-brother to Daahyeh who I won the Albany on. He'll stay six furlongs but his quality got him through the race - David Egan, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: There's no reason to think he won't keep improving with age and I'd love to place him to win a Listed race. He should be ready to start in the second half of May. 11-04-25

runners.comment: Has gone off the boil on last three starts but the return to Doncaster may spark a revival
runners.spotlight: Has gone off the boil on his last three starts but he won here (7f, good) in June and his course form-figures read 131 (including on soft ground); on a handy mark if the return to Doncaster sparks a revival.

quotes.quote: Akkadian Thunder ran a blinder. David [O'Meara, trainer] was keen to have him nice and relaxed early. He said, 'Ride him to have him coming home'. I followed Oisin [Murphy] everywhere and we had a great run through. When we got in the clear, I thought he might pick up and get to Oisin, but fairness to the winner who picked up and won well. Our horse has run a great race - Billy Lee, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: A good horse last year, he'll start off in the Victoria Cup. I thought he wanted only fast ground and then he ran a stormer on soft when second at Ascot in September. He's got plenty of ability and a lot of speed, and straight tracks suit him well. 14-04-25

runners.breeder: S A S Elevage Du Haras De Bourgeauville
runners.comment: Has never raced over a trip this short but of interest on close second in the Bunbury Cup
runners.spotlight: He has an in-and-out profile and has never raced over a trip this short, but he was a clear second behind a well-handicapped winner in the Bunbury Cup in July (7f, good to firm) and ran pretty well when seventh of 17 at York (1m, good to firm) last time; also effective on good to soft; not ruled out.

quotes.quote: Fair play to Jamie Spencer, who said after he rode Aalto last time that he had a big one in him but didn't know where or when. We may have a look at the International Stakes at Ascot after that - Ian Williams, trainer.

runners.comment: Progressive filly who won at York's Ebor meeting; could play another leading role
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly who made it three wins for the year (1m/7f) when prevailing in a big field at York (7f, good to firm) last month; she hit the front over 1f out at York and a 5lb rise may not prevent her playing another leading role, provided the ground suits (unproven on a slow surface).

quotes.quote: I'm delighted for Royal Velvet's owner-breeder Susie Hartley. Going back to 7f from a mile has been the right decision and having William on board always helps, particularly at this track - William Knight, trainer.
They went a bit quick up front because of the loose horse. Royal Velvet basically picked up the pieces - Cieren Fallon, jockey.

quotes.race: Bet £5 Get £20 At tote.co.uk Racing League Race 3 Handicap (Trainers Registered To Racing League)
quotes.quote: Royal Velvet has won really well there today. I did think the flat track and the easy mile really suits her and she likes that ground. She was quite backward last year and she's just really coming to herself. I think she could be quite useful - William Knight, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Inconsistent but won two starts ago and has some strong form here; could go well
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 6yo who hasn't gone beyond a bare 6f for a long time and finished down the field at York in July when last seen; however, he won at Pontefract (6f, good) earlier that month and has shown some strong form here in the past (including a soft-ground win); could go well.

quotes.quote: We popped the blinkers on Aleezdancer as he's been around a fair while now and thought he might be a bit cute. He's very genuine, but needed something a bit different and Neil said he worked a treat. He was quite lazy in the first furlong but then came good and travelled into it. They went fast but he loves a pace like that, I was always confident a furlong down that they couldn't keep going and he was coming good. He was a little bit unlucky in the Stewards' Cup last year. He's had two quick races so we'll freshen him up now and make a plan for the rest of the year - Kevin Ryan, trainer.
Aleezdancer is not very big but he's gutsy and he loves this ground. My main hope for him is the Ayr Gold Cup. He's no good in the summer as he likes cut in the ground - Jack Berry, joint-owner.

stable_tour.quote: He was impressive at York, then hated the track at Epsom, which can happen. He's ground dependent, he needs ease in it. 10-06-24

runners.comment: Continues to drop down the weights but hasn't shone on his last two outings
runners.spotlight: 2yo Group 3 winner in 2023; with a new yard this season and there were encouraging signs in May; continues to drop down the weights but hasn't shone on his last two outings and has to raise his game.

quotes.quote: It's very exciting, I was really happy with Indian Run. He got a bad trip all the way round on the outside and didn't get any cover but he found and found, which is great. He's in the Dewhurst so we'll probably look there. He's so unfurnished he needs time so we'll give him a nice break - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

quotes.race: Greatwood Charity 25th Anniversary British EBF Crocker Bulteel Maiden Stakes (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Indian Run is very unfurnished and was quite colty in the paddock, so did pretty much everything wrong before the race, but he did everything right in it. It looked a nice race and Danny said it rode like a nice race. We've thought quite a bit of him for a while and he's not entered in any posh races, but probably will be soon - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's in the Stewards' Cup, along with Solar Aclaim, but he's the opposite in that he wouldn't want it too soft, so they won't both run. He's a talented lad - we've tweaked his wind since his last run at Windsor, where we were a little bit disappointed with his finishing effort. I think he's got a nice race in him. Possibly not at the top level of handicap sprinting, but we know he's decent as he won a Group race as a two-year-old for his previous trainer, so it shows a good level of ability is there. I don't think we've quite worked him out but hopefully that wind operation will give us the extra bit to win a race as he's been running pretty well. 23-07-25

runners.comment: Beaten 18 times since winning 2023 Wokingham; needs to better this summer's form
runners.spotlight: Beaten 18 times since making a winning stable debut in the 2023 Wokingham; his seventh of 20 at York three weeks ago was a step back in the right direction but he needs another today to exploit his falling mark.

quotes.race: Arc Prix Maurice de Gheest (Group 1) (3yo+) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Saint Lawrence has run an absolute stormer and I think if the pace had been stronger, he'd have nearly won. He's definitely improving and he's proved himself at Group 1 level; he's taken a big step up from winning a handicap at Ascot - Hollie Doyle, jockey.

Saint Lawrence has always shown us plenty at home, he's a very nice horse to have around and I'm just delighted to get the job done on first time of asking. I fancied all three of them, I made sure Hollie chose which one to ride so it wasn't my fault and she chose right! - Archie Watson, trainer.

It was probably trainer error as a two-year-old thinking as he is by Al Kazeem Saint Lawrence would be fine over seven furlongs. He is entered in the King's Stand. James (Doyle, jockey) said he can go five or six and we might look at the Leisure Stakes at Windsor which is an easy six in about two weeks' time - Roger Varian, trainer.

quotes.race: Denford Stakes (Listed Race) (Formerly The Washington Singer Stakes)
quotes.quote: Saint Lawrence has looked a nice one and was one of ours we nominated early on in the first week (to get in certain races). Although he's by Al Kazeem he'd shown up well in May on the gallops and we thought he could be a Chesham horse. He didn't go down that route, but wasn't disgraced in the Pat Eddery or Superlative, although those races probably didn't go his way. I'm not sure about plans. Andrea said he'd get a mile, but I don't think he necessarily has to next time. He's had five runs, so maybe one or two more this season and the Royal Lodge can favour experience. Now he's a Listed winner, we have to aim at Group 3s and Group 2s and there are no soft options so we'll see. He'll be better on better ground - Roger Varian, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He could run in the Abernant at the Craven meeting or we might wait for a valuable 6f handicap at the Guineas meeting. Although he's not a regular winner, his runs in the Palace House and King's Stand last season were really good. He's been gelded, which might help, and he could be a Wokingham horse. 06-04-23

He won at Listed level as a two-year-old and has progressed this season without adding to his two career wins. His best run was when second to Rohaan in the Pavilion Stakes at Ascot in April, which was a superb effort. I'm looking forward to campaigning him in the autumn and he should get his head in front again as he's pleasing us at home. 18-08-21

runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Adrian McAlpine
runners.comment: Returned to form when third at Thirsk recently and he might not be far away
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when third of 14 at Thirsk (6f, good) recently and he retains the same mark, which is 6lb lower than when 3l fourth of nine in this last year (soft); might not be far away.

quotes.race: Always Gamble Responsibly With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: We were on it from a long way down, they went very quick in front of me. Spangled Mac's form at six and seven furlongs has definitely benefited him - Billy Loughnane, rider, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.
That's my seventh winner and thanks to Mr Boughey for putting me up. I won on SPANGLED MAC at Kempton and he's improved from there and picked up nicely when I asked him - Connor Planas, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's a fun horse who is probably a little bit on his mark but he's run in both races at Royal Ascot before and I'd imagine we'll pick one closer to the time. He'll pop up at some point but you'd be brave to tell me when. 09-06-25

He never ran as a two-year-old but quickly made up for lost time last year with four wins ranging from 5½f to 7f. I took him to Dubai where he wintered well, and I thought he would win on his final run there but he was drawn wide and Oisin Murphy could not get out of the traffic. His mark has dropped from 97 to 95, which might not sound a lot, but it may be just enough to keep him out of the Wokingham. I am desperate for him to get in that race as I think 6f off a strong pace on fast ground is 100 per cent what he wants. He's in very good form, and we have to make a brave call next week of do we run in the Buckingham Palace Stakes, where he will definitely get in, or wait and hope he makes it in the Wokingham. I wish they were run the other way round. 14-06-23

runners.comment: No threat on last two starts but he's 2-2 at Doncaster, including this race last year
runners.spotlight: He's failed to threaten on his last two starts and needs to get back on track but he's won on both previous visits to Doncaster, in this race last year (soft) and over 5f this June (good); a visor returns (has won in it; replaces cheekpieces); has to be in calculations in view of his record here.

stable_tour.quote: He's won over £200,000 in prize-money since we bought him. His latest win was at Doncaster this month and he just loves soft ground on the grass. He's very effective on the all-weather as well. He's only won three times but he's always consistent and in the money a lot in good races. He'll probably go for next month's Coral Sprint Trophy at York providing the ground is soft. 26-09-24

runners.comment: Has kept on well for second over 6f the last twice; the extra yardage could be a firm plus
runners.spotlight: Three-time French winner (all on good to soft, including at 6.5f) who has worked his way back into form for this yard, finishing runner-up on his last two starts (6f, AW/good to firm); he's kept on well on those last two outings and today's extra yardage could be a firm plus; one to consider.

runners.comment: Front-runner who has won three of his last five starts and is respected
runners.spotlight: Two wins last September (7f, good/good to firm) and another at Haydock (7f, good) this July on second run of the year; again ran well when third at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) latest; unraced on slow ground; there can be optimism this front-runner will be fine at today's trip and he's respected.

stable_tour.quote: He's won three of his last four and I wouldn't say I've been surprised by him. He's been in great form. He didn't look as if he'd stopped improving last time when he won at Haydock and he got a really nice positive ride from Ryan Sexton, who grabbed the race by the scruff of the neck and was always in control. He loves a turning 7f, but he's the sort you might put in the Ayr Gold Cup and think about the Silver Cup with him because they'll go quick - he's not a slow horse at all and you can see him coming home strongly. The only thing about him is that he wouldn't want the ground too soft. He's enjoying the dry summer when one or two others aren't. 23-07-25

racecards.verdict: Last year's winner BALDOMERO (nap) was also on the scoresheet here in June and is taken to maintain his unbeaten record at Doncaster.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Pocklington, 13/2 Commanche Falls,  Ten Pounds, 7/1 Royal Velvet, 9/1 Desert Falcon, 10/1 Purosangue,  Purest Time,  Akkadian Thunder, 14/1 Spangled Mac, 16/1 Oliver Show,  Baldomero, 20/1 Aalto,  Aleezdancer, 25/1 Indian Run,  Russet Gold,  Vafortino, 33/1 Saint Lawrence

racecards.race_name: IPM Group Leger Legends Classified Stakes

runners.comment: An in-form 4yo who won last week but carrying a penalty here could make life awkward
runners.spotlight: His French win was on very soft ground and conditions were heavy when strolling home at Ffos Las five days ago, which followed a third over the round 1m here in July (good); carrying a penalty could make life awkward.

runners.owner: R G Robinson & R D Robinson
runners.comment: Inflated mark doesn't count against him in a race of this nature; holding his form well
runners.spotlight: Completed a 1m hat-trick in June, on the AW, good to firm and soft; his inflated mark doesn't count against him in a race of this nature and he's holding his form well.

runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing
runners.comment: Won twice before faring surprisingly well behind some progressive 3yos
runners.spotlight: Last three races over slightly further at Hamilton, winning Class 5/6 handicaps before running surprisingly well behind progressive 3yos in a Class 3; perhaps flattered from the front last time but enters the equation.

quotes.race: Bet At racingtv.com Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
quotes.quote: Ayr Poet was super and took me the full way. I just sat there and he did his job. I thought coming to the two-furlong pole that he's travelling very well and then I thought today might be the day for that first winner. Jim said let him jump and sit handy and if not, don't panic and he'd be good for me. There's no racing in my family at all and I'm continuing as an amateur as I'm a bit heavy for an apprentice licence, but Jim's been so generous in helping young people like me progress - Megan Davies, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

I thought we had it on the line but we did it the tough way. Ayr Poet knows where the winning post is here. He's a good doer and is from a family we've done well with here, as he's the fourth winner out of his dam, with Euchen Glen and Sir Chauvelin both running this week too - Jim Goldie, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: His brother wasn't too bad as a 12-year-old and I'm sure he'd be quite good as a 13-year-old if I kept him going. He'll win more races, I wouldn't be keeping him going if I didn't think I could. It's been a very lucky family for me. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Exploited a much reduced mark at Windsor and should go well again if he's turned a corner
runners.spotlight: These terms don't appear that favourable at face value but he returned to winning ways in a 1m handicap at Windsor (soft; second run in this headgear combination) and he was worthy of a much higher mark last year; considered.

runners.owner: Peter Clarke Racing And John Wainwright
runners.comment: Likes a long straight so recent defeats at Beverley and Pontefract are forgiven
runners.spotlight: Likes a long straight so recent defeats at Beverley and Pontefract are forgiven, while his sixth at Redcar in between was after a troubled run; could have a say.

runners.comment: All three wins at 7f but has form at 1m; 2-3 for current yard and 2-2 in the tongue-tie
runners.spotlight: All three wins at 7f but has form over this far; 2-3 for current yard and 2-2 in the tongue-tie, winning low-grade handicaps here (good to soft) and at Newcastle (AW); has been going from the front and should make a bold bid.

runners.comment: Hasn't been running well enough of late to believe he'll provide the answer
runners.spotlight: Both wins on Tapeta and 0-10 on the grass, though twice placed; hasn't been running well enough of late to believe he'll provide the answer.

runners.comment: Last time needs forgiving but his previous run was encouraging; cheekpieces return
runners.spotlight: Exposed five-time winner; ran no race at York last time but was bang there at Newmarket the start before (1m, good to firm; ideal ground) and that was also the case the last time he tried cheekpieces (here last August).

quotes.quote: I wouldn't say Siam Fox liked the track on this ground as he hit every undulation, but he got away with it. I didn't want to take any chances as I think a mile is as short as he wants to go. I think a different track and further will bring out more improvement - Silvestre de Sousa, rider.

runners.comment: Only had three races and the close second at Carlisle (1m, good/soft) is the standout run
runners.spotlight: Only had three races and the close second at Carlisle (1m, good to soft) is the standout run; bit risky.

runners.owner: P&h Moorhead, J Collins And S Piper
runners.comment: Southwell last time was a backward step but if forgiven that a case can be made
runners.spotlight: In good form back in the spring at about 1m and his Wolverhampton fourth after a break was no backward step; Southwell was ten days ago but if forgiven that a case can be made.

runners.comment: 5l fourth of ten on handicap debut at Goodwood and he has some upside to him
runners.spotlight: Three of his four runs have been on heavy ground, last time finishing a 5l fourth of ten on handicap debut at Goodwood (1m2f); remains to be seen whether he needs those conditions but there's some upside to him.

runners.comment: 0-4 since debut win; has regressed but could be all the better for being gelded
runners.spotlight: Has regressed since his winning debut in April (7f AW) but only had five races and could be all the better for being gelded.

runners.comment: Some promise last year but not this season and best trip remains a mystery; others safer
runners.spotlight: Some promise last year but not this season and best trip remains a mystery; others safer.

runners.comment: A reproduction of his Nottingham success (1m, good) in July would give him a chance
runners.spotlight: A reproduction of his Nottingham success (1m, good) in July would give him a chance but he hasn't approached that level in his last three races.

runners.comment: Best form yet when second on recent stable debut; every chance on these terms
runners.spotlight: Bits of promise for Marcus Tregoning before changing hands for 18,000gns; ran his best race yet on stable debut when beaten a head at Carlisle (1m1f, good); has every chance on these terms and open to further improvement.

runners.comment: 6f maiden winner; mostly well held in subsequent runs and yet to threaten for current yard
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden for Charlie Johnston last summer; mostly well held in his subsequent races and yet to threaten for current yard.

runners.comment: Reserve; now hooded but even if on a good day these terms are tough
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has been good and bad in his last four races and looks hard to predict; now hooded, but even if on a good day these terms are tough.

racecards.verdict: It could be onwards and upwards for the lightly raced 3yo MOONJID after his encouraging first run for these connections.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Moonjid, 5/1 Beaming Light, 8/1 Susie Sioux, 9/1 Finn Ironside, 10/1 Double Time, 11/1 Golden Pharaoh, 12/1 Ayr Poet,  Quiet Resolve,  Legend Forever, 16/1 Siam Fox,  Chambers,  Four Fifty, 20/1 Eeetee,  Antiquity, 33/1 Louie The Legend, 50/1 Shazani

racecards.race_name: Japan Racing Association Sceptre Fillies' Stakes (Group 3)

runners.comment: Group 3 winner in June; not ruled out but has to bounce back from two lesser performances
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground Listed winner who easily won the Group 3 Princess Elizabeth Stakes at Epsom (8.5f, good) in June; not ruled out under a 3lb penalty but she needs to bounce back from two lesser performances.

quotes.race: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Sponsored By Oddschecker) (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Spiritual loved it in front. She got a bit of space around her today and relaxed. She's a handful and has to go off on her own every day otherwise she's off! It was lovely to win a Group 3 like that. "She's a good filly but just one who tends to worry about the others. She got free on the lead today without having to go flat out. We'll have a look at Group 2 now and she could go to France - John Gosden, joint-trainer.

Spiritual got in a nice rhythm on the front end and has form on slow ground. It all worked out well for us on the day. She kept responding. It's windy up the straight, which might have stopped them making too much ground on us. She's learning and is still lightly raced. She's going the right way so there's no reason to think she can't come on after today - Richard Kingscote, jockey.

runners.comment: Listed runner-up at Musselburgh in June but well beaten on next two outings
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced, out of half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Special Duty; Listed runner-up behind Jabaara at Musselburgh (7f, good) in June; however, well beaten on her next two outings and others are preferred.

quotes.quote: Arolla did it well and travelled nicely. She was a bit keen so I pulled her back and let her settle but she went through the race nicely and won pretty easily. I don't think she minds cut in the ground and it was very testing. She picked up really well and gave me a good feel. I felt like I had plenty left even crossing the line. She pricked her ears when she hit the front and got a bit lonely so should take another step forward - Trevor Whelan, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Close third in 7f Group 3 at Glorious Goodwood and she's one to consider
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Deauville (1m, soft) in July and close third in Group 3 Oak Tree Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) next time; below best when fifth in Group 3 at Sandown (1m, soft) last time but she travelled well and could benefit from this drop back to 7f; she's one to consider.

quotes.race: Princess Elizabeth Stakes (Sponsored By Oddschecker) (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: I'm delighted. Bright Thunder has run a great race and I'm very, very happy with her. She loves the ground - we probably could have done with it being a bit softer - but I think that was probably a career best for her. I'm very happy with her - Karl Burke, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: It was probably a career best from her at Epsom and, if the ground was on the easy side at Ascot, she could make a quick reappearance in the Kensington Palace or the Duke of Cambridge. She loves soft ground and is a talented filly 12-06-25

runners.comment: Listed winner last year but a long way below her best on her three runs this season
runners.spotlight: Went close in Group 3 Princess Elizabeth Stakes at Epsom (8.5f, good to soft) last June but she was a long way below that level on her three outings this season; appears for the first time since June.

quotes.race: British EBF £100,000 2YO Fillies' Series Final (EBF Restricted Race Series) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: This race had been the plan for a while for Chic Colombine and it's a nice prize to win. She proved at Doncaster she handles soft ground but I don't think she necessarily wants it. She's on the up, showed a great attitude there and has gone from strength to strength since her maiden win. She'll be suited by a mile and I imagine she'll be running in a stakes race before long - George Boughey, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF "Carrie Red" Fillies' Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: The real question was the trip. Chic Colombine is bred to be a miler and possibly a ten furlong horse so I think there's a little bit more to come. She's a filly with a three-year-old's pedigree and we'll just bide our time with her - George Boughey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She ran in the same race at Doncaster as Botanical and also wants slower ground, but it was good to see her back on the track. She's a stakes winner already and has been placed in some good races. We'll wait for slower ground and she could go back to France, but it's very much an open book for her at the moment. 07-04-25

runners.owner: Pineapple Stud And Miss C Douglas
runners.comment: Three handicap wins this year; further improvement can't be ruled out but it is necessary
runners.spotlight: Three handicap wins this year (6f, good/good to firm) and another good run when sixth of 27 in the Stewards' Cup at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) last month; also effective at 7f and further improvement can't be ruled out, but it's necessary now upped in grade.

quotes.race: Coopers Marquees EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Circe ran in a quality race at Sandown, she was beaten by two horses with experience. I thought she'd step forward and she has. She's a big horse with plenty of scope and she'll make a nice three-year-old - Sean Levey, jockey.

runners.comment: Fourth in this 12 months ago; mixed form this season but she's capable of being in the mix
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 11 in this 12 months ago (soft); mixed form this season and she blew the start at Glorious Goodwood two runs ago, but she's caught the eye on occasions and was a Listed winner at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) in May; capable of being in the mix.

quotes.quote: We came here for a bit of black type and if Fair Angellica won it was only going to be a bonus. The way she shot clear on her last run at Kempton made me very concerned about the mile at Ascot, and she didn't stay. Ryan [Moore, jockey] said she could come back to six, certainly on softer ground. I've an eye on the Oak Tree [at Goodwood] but we'll see - Richard Hughes, trainer.

David Egan said Fair Angellica was a bit gangly in the early stages but when she hit the rising ground she took off. That's it for this season I think and hopefully she can get some black type next year - Wendy Miller, owner.

stable_tour.quote: We're delighted with her. The ground was too quick at Glorious Goodwood and we couldn't take the chance. She was declared because there was rain forecast and it never appeared, but she seems in great form and Ryan Moore liked her when he won on her. She ran another good race when fourth in the Group 3 Sceptre Fillies' Stakes at Doncaster on Sunday. 18-09-24

runners.comment: Two-time Listed runner-up but well beaten in Group 3 at Sandown recently
runners.spotlight: Listed runner-up at Newmarket (1m, heavy) last September and Chelmsford (7f, AW) this July; however, well beaten in Group 3 at Sandown (1m, soft) recently and her claims aren't compelling on the back of that.

runners.breeder: C R Marks & R J Gallagher
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps this year but didn't threaten in Group 3 two starts ago
runners.spotlight: Two handicap wins in May (6f/7f) and another big run when third in 7f handicap at Newbury recently; however, she didn't threaten in 7f Group 3 at Glorious Goodwood in July and looks vulnerable back up in grade.

runners.comment: Not quite at her best when fifth in Group 3 latest but failed to settle; possible player
runners.spotlight: First past the post in the Group 3 Oak Tree Stakes at Goodwood last July (7f, good to firm; demoted due to interference); not quite at her best when fifth in that same race last time but she raced freely; has shown promise on good to soft ground; possible player if settling better than last time.

quotes.race: Edinburgh Gin Queen Of Scots British EBF Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Jabaara did it nicely and tracked a strong pace from a low draw. She was in front soon enough because of it and Silvestre [De Sousa] gave her a lovely ride. She threw her race away at Goodwood last year in the Oak Tree Stakes and she could well end up back there, with a plan to take in another Listed race at Chelmsford on the way. She is a consistent filly and she's a joy to have in the yard - Roger Varian, trainer.

quotes.race: Visit Qatar Oak Tree Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: The stewards have had the luxury of watching it many more times than me from lots of different angles and they have come to that decision, so we have to congratulate the winner. I think Jabaara was on top for much of the race but maybe she just threw it away. Although we definitely took Jim's lane, I didn't think that he had to take back and I didn't think his momentum was checked. I've seen worse incidents not reversed, so I thought we'd be okay. She was dominant and maybe she got a bit lonely in front. She ran off her true line and has paid the penalty. I'm disappointed, but I'm very pleased with her performance - Roger Varian, trainer.

quotes.race: Tattersalls Falmouth Stakes (Group 1) (British Champions Series) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Jabaara has run a massive race to be second to a good filly. We rolled the dice a bit and it's come off, and to be Group 1-placed after winning two Listed races is very pleasing. If she comes out of this in good shape we'd like to run her in the Group 3 Oak Tree Stakes at Glorious Goodwood - Roger Varian, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: It was nice and straightforward, and a pleasing performance. I was stuck out wide and couldn't push on early, but I was keen to let Jabaara on once she got balanced. She had a fair bit of weight and I didn't want anything to get the jump on her. The Falmouth might be a bit tough for her but Roger [Varian] has placed her nicely and she's knocked off a couple of Listed races. We'll have a bit of fun with her, but life won't be easy - James Doyle,  jockey.

quotes.race: Edinburgh Gin Queen Of Scots Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Jabaara did that nicely. She travelled strongly, quickened up well and obviously has been suited by the return to seven furlongs. I'm delighted for the team, especially when you send one all the way to Scotland. She could suit a race like the Group 3 Oak Tree Stakes at Goodwood - Roger Varian, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Jabaara has always looked smart. I thought she did well there as she was a touch green and he (David Egan) had to take her off heels, switch, and start his run again. We have been fortunate to win the Albany twice before and I think this filly should go straight there as I think she is quite good and she looked it there today. Time will tell us what the form is worth. A strong pace at Ascot over a stiff six on decent ground would suit her I would have thought - Roger Varian, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She runs on Saturday in the Grade 1 Jenny Wiley Stakes at Keeneland, which has been a long-term plan. She had a blowout at Wolverhampton last month and that did its job because her work before she went to America was excellent. Her runs in last season's Falmouth and the Oak Tree justify her being in the race and we'll see how we go. She's very talented and might be campaigned with some imagination this year. There's a nice race at Saratoga in July if she looked suited to that kind of test on Saturday. 11-04-25

She won first time out at Newmarket in the spring before finishing mid-division in the Albany at Royal Ascot. She was unfortunate not to be a place higher when fourth in the Sweet Solera this month at Newmarket. I think the extra furlong of that Group 3 just stretched her too much, but she'll drop back to 6f next time, possibly in the Group 3 Dick Poole Fillies' Stakes at Salisbury on September 7, and I'd expect her to go well. 25-08-23

runners.comment: Won Newbury handicap; further improvement needed but unexposed under front-running tactics
runners.spotlight: Headstrong 4yo who was allowed to stride on in a handicap at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last month and posted a career best to win; unraced on slow ground; further improvement is needed back up in grade but she's lightly raced and unexposed under front-running tactics; not discounted.

quotes.quote: Lou Lou's Gift is probably not the most versatile, but she's got a good amount of ability and it's just about getting things to feel like they're her idea. Today worked out perfectly and the seven furlongs on a nice, straight track helped - Tom Marquand, jockey.

runners.comment: Listed runner-up at Longchamp in April but no impact in Listed races on either start since
runners.spotlight: Went close in Listed race at Longchamp (7f, good to soft) on reappearance but unable to make an impact in Listed races (7f/1m, good) on either start since and she looks up against it.

stable_tour.quote: She's done well from three to four and there will be more to come from her this year. She's a sweet filly and I hope she'll get some black type. She wants 7f or an easy 1m. 16-04-25

runners.comment: Close 3rd in this last year and looked better than ever when winning a handicap last month
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo for Ralph Beckett last season, including a close third in this race (7f, soft), and she looked better than ever when winning a handicap by 2l at the Curragh (6f, good) on last month's stable and seasonal debut; leading claims if performing to the same level here.

quotes.race: Kwiff: Supercharge Your Horse Racing Betting Handicap
quotes.quote: I was going to ride Queen Of Mougins to come home strong, having had a break, but I jumped good and was always very comfortable. We got racing early in the middle part and I didn't really go with them, she came good at the 2f pole and she picked up and quickened well. She'll improve plenty for it, maybe 6f is her trip - Dylan Browne McMonagle, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: BetVictor British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (Supporting The British Heart Foundation)(GBB/IRE Inc')
quotes.quote: Queen Of Mougins a nice filly who has done that well and Rossa was complimentary. He said she was professional, but will improve having been green in front. He thought she might be better on better ground - Joe Tuite, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Listed winner at Longchamp recently; rain would boost her claims; on the shortlist
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile in October 2023 (1m, soft); missed all of 2024 and has worked her way back to form this season, winning a Listed race at Longchamp (1m, very soft) a fortnight ago; rain would boost her claims (although she has run well on good/good to firm); on the shortlist.

quotes.quote: Shuwari saw out the trip well but I think we just got outstayed in the last 50 yards by Ryan and obviously that is a very good filly. I think she liked the ground as it wasn't that soft. Everything went well apart from the fact we didn't win. That will be her for the season and I suppose we will work back from the 1,000 Guineas. I wouldn't be afraid of pitching straight up into a 1,000 Guineas as she is a straightforward filly to train - Ollie Sangster, trainer.

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Rockfel Stakes (Group 2) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: I was very happy, and Shuwari ran great. She came home very strongly. We will see how the next week goes but we could think about coming back for the Fillies' Mile. She had been off for 64 days and she will come on again so will definitely think about the Fillies' Mile - Ollie Sangster, trainer.

quotes.race: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Shuwari will probably want a mile and her dam is by Sir Percy, so I suspect there's a bit of stamina in there and her speed just comes from class. I was originally going to start her over six because she's so speedy. I was really on the fence about running her when we saw the weather report last night but we thought we'd take our chance. Oisin was thrilled, he said it was probably not her ideal ground but she was very tenacious. We'd think about the Rockfel and maybe the Fillies' Mile, but obviously that's ambitious and there's a lot of grass between now and then. She's not going to have tons of racing this year but another few starts. She's got a great attitude and is very straightforward to train, she's at the top of my two-year-olds at the moment - Ollie Sangster, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: At the moment, she seems to be the best we've got and she's always looked good, but when you have small numbers and don't have too many others to compare them with it's hard to know. Her debut at Newbury went well then she did it nicely in a Listed race at Sandown. That form could hardly have worked out any better as the filly she beat was Fallen Angel, who won the Moyglare last weekend, although it looks like she has improved a lot. I imagine Shuwari will improve for some better ground. She had a little setback which meant she missed the Prestige at Goodwood, but it was nothing untoward and we're looking forward to running her in the Rockfel at Newmarket. If she runs well, she could always go on to the Fillies' Mile. She may be a Guineas filly - we'll see after the Rockfel! The dam is by Sir Percy so there's a bit of stamina in there and she could stay further. 12-09-23

runners.comment: Twice third in Listed races; something to find but lightly raced and in excellent hands
runners.spotlight: Last season concluded with a close Listed third at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) and she was third in a Listed race at Chelmsford (7f, AW) this July when last seen; has something to find back up in grade but she's in excellent hands and this is just her eighth run; not ruled out.

quotes.quote: I was very impressed the way Stop The Cavalry picked up there. She loves the ground and the track was fine, but I think this is a filly with a lot of ability - Edward Greatrex, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Realco Equipment Ruby Anniversary EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Stop The Cavalry did it very well. She travelled very strongly and conditions suited her. Ralph (Beckett) was confident beforehand and she knew her job. She's bred to get further but she showed good speed and signs of potentially being a high-class filly - Hector Crouch, rider.

runners.comment: Disappointing on sole run this year but of interest if rediscovering last season's promise
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on sole run this year, when making stable debut in Group 3 at Epsom in June; however, she was progressive for William Haggas last season, winning a Listed race at Ascot (7f, soft) last October, and she'll be of interest if rediscovering that promise in a first-time tongue-tie.

runners.comment: Close Listed second in May but down the field in Group 3 races on next three starts
runners.spotlight: Close second in Listed race for 3yo fillies at York (1m, good to firm) in May; however, she's finished down the field in Group 3 races on her next three starts and needs to turn things around.

runners.comment: Tailed off at Sandown recently but not written off each-way in view of earlier form
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Group 3 at Sandown (1m, soft) just 15 days ago and a risky proposition on the back of that; however, she was third in a Group 3 at Chantilly (1m, good to soft) in July and was held up in a contest that favoured those who raced more prominently when seventh of 13 in the Group 3 Oak Tree Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good) in July; not written off each-way.

quotes.race: Clipper EBF Marygate Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race) (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: I bred Betty Clover her and I'm so wildly excited, I just can't believe it. I thought she had a chance of a place and I thought she had a chance of turning the form round with Running Queen, who got first run on us last time. Georgia gave her the most beautiful ride. I'm very proud of my jockey, she doesn't get many chances and bloody hell she's good. She should be saluted for that.   We have to look at Ascot. I don't know which race. It's a very fast five furlongs at York and she travelled all right into it but we'll have a think - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She was second, beaten only a nose, by last week's Duke of Cambridge winner Crimson Advocate in a Listed fillies' race at Goodwood and was then third in a Group 2 in France. She ran in the Sandringham at Royal Ascot on Friday and I was expecting her to go very well as a Group horse in a handicap but she was pulled up. I think she must have whacked a joint and I'm really happy that William Buick pulled her up as it's better to be safe than sorry. She appears to be fine but we'll make further checks just in case to make sure there's no problem. I think she's very underrated and I've got her in the Falmouth at the Newmarket July meeting. That may be a step too far, but all being well it'll be Group races over a mile for her. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Second in the Fred Darling in April; needs to get back on track but she retains potential
runners.spotlight: Won early this year on first two starts (7f, AW) then second of 15 in the Group 3 Fred Darling (7f, good); eighth of ten in the 1,000 Guineas next time (1m, good to firm) and soundly beaten in Group 3 at Sandown (1m, soft) a fortnight ago when back from a break; has worn a hood on all five starts but not today; has to get back on track but she may have needed that latest outing and retains potential; interesting contender.

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Stakes (Registered As The Fred Darling Stakes) (Group 3) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: It'll be straight to the Guineas. I'm delighted with her. It was Hey Boo's first time on the grass after two wins on the all-weather and she'll come on a ton. She'll improve for the mile and I wouldn't swap her - Jack Channon, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing Value At bet365 Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Hey Boo gave me a great feel. I managed to get her mid-div, she switched off lovely in her race. She picked up well, and I thought she was quite professional for a filly having her first start on a racecourse. She's knuckled down well, I love her attitude, she's done it in a nice style - George Bass, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: She's won her first two starts this year at Chelmsford and I think a lot of her. She's been given a mark of 81, which is fair because that's all she's sort of run to, but I don't think that has scratched the surface on her ability levels. I think on the turf, and in a better field, you'll see her in a better light. She's got an awful lot of talent and she'll go for the Fred Darling now. If she's up to that level then we'll look at the 1,000 Guineas, but if not we could take a step back and build her back up to that grade again through the handicap system. 02-04-25

runners.comment: 3yo who has been running well in defeat in handicaps but has plenty to find
runners.spotlight: 3yo who has run well in defeat in handicaps on all four starts this term; however, she has plenty to find  tackling her first Group race.

racecards.verdict: Last year's 1l third QUEEN OF MOUGINS impressed when comfortably winning a handicap at the Curragh last month and can follow up.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Queen Of Mougins, 9/2 Shuwari, 7/1 Spiritual,  Fair Angellica, 8/1 Lou Lou's Gift,  Bright Thunder, 10/1 Jabaara, 16/1 Stop The Cavalry, 20/1 Havana Pusey, 25/1 Circe,  Fair Point,  Queen's Reign,  Bermuda Longtail,  Miss Nightfall, 33/1 Arolla,  Chic Colombine,  Sunfall,  Betty Clover,  Hey Boo

runners.breeder: P Taplin & Mike Channon Bloodstock Ltd
runners.comment: Low-mileage 3yo who continues to raise his game and likely has even more to offer
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who continues to raise his game, latterly going down narrowly to one of his market rivals at Newbury (5f, good to firm; effective on softer); hard to knock, other than he's having to concede weight all round.

quotes.race: Weatherbys Racing Bank Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Getreadytorumble has a lot of boot and is from a very fast family. He had a few issues at two but has come to hand nicely. The ground was a bit too quick for him, but he had a bit too much class for the others. I'm hoping it will be onwards and upwards when we go into handicaps - Jack Channon, trainer.

runners.comment: Lightly raced dual winner but handicap debut at York was underwhelming; now tongue tied
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden for the Gosdens and a 6f novice here (good to firm) by 4l on debut for current yard; faced a tough task on handicap debut at York but was easy to back (18-1) and trailed in nearly last; drops back to 5f for the first time with a tongue-tie added.

runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League
runners.comment: Back on a good mark and third in this last year, but current form is an issue
runners.spotlight: Capable front-runner on his day and was third in this last year when 6lb higher; however, the handicapper took aim after he won a small-field conditions race at Beverley the run after and he's mostly struggled since.

quotes.race: Eddie And Violet Smith Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It wasn't my plan to come here, but when I saw the entries I said to the lads we're going to have to throw him in as it was only going to be four runners. Fair play to Ali Rawlinson [rider of King Of Stars], he and Mia had a talk as we all know how King Of Stars runs. I said to Mia see how you jump, see how Ali jumps and go from there, as there was no point in the two of them taking each other on because it's a long way home with a bit of give in the ground. Mia gave him a good ride. She's doing really well and she's at the right stable with Eve [Johnson Houghton], and she used her head there. Tees Spirit came into the race in good form and he's been a star this year. Emaraaty Ana had the form in the book, so it was a great performance and I'm chuffed to bits for Mia and the horse - Adrian Nicholls, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

Tees Spirit came here instead of the Beverley Bullet. I was delighted that the ground was so quick and he just seems to be getting quicker. He loves rattling off fast ground over 5f. He's in the Abbaye and he will probably run - Adrian Nicholls, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing League On Sky Sports Racing Handicap
quotes.quote: We haven't done anything different with Tees Spirit but his confidence is sky high and he's on a roll. We only have 20 horses and it's great for us to have one as good as this. He hasn't stopped improving yet and he might be good enough to be aimed at Listed and Group races eventually. He's shown he likes this course, so the Portland Handicap is certainly a consideration for his next race - Adrian Nicholls, trainer.
Tees Spirit was doing everything right at home and had been working nicely. He put the race to bed fairly quickly - Barry McHugh, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He's done brilliantly for us. Winning the Dash at Epsom a couple of years ago was massive for me and then he backed it up in a Listed race at Tipperary. He hit all the heights and had such a good year back then, but what goes up must come down. He had his wind done and it took him a while to come back but Mia's claim really helped with that. He's come back this year in great form, winning first time at Newmarket, after which he was stepped up in class. He came back to form at Sandown the other day and you don't get rated 97 for nothing. There's a 5f handicap on the first day of Glorious Goodwood and a similar race at Haydock on the Sunday of that week and he'll go to one of those depending on the ground. It's good to have a horse to take you to the big gigs. 26-07-24

runners.comment: Potentially dangerous off this mark and the forecast showers are in his favour
runners.spotlight: Flattering to deceive this season as his comeback third at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) has worked out really well but he hasn't quite reached the same heights since, despite further thirds on last two outings; however, it was good ground last week and the forecast showers are in his favour; softening ground would bring him right into it.

runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from his win in July and was out of the money in yesterday's Portland
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on at all from his winning start for this yard at Newmarket in July and was out of the money in yesterday's Portland here.

quotes.quote: Emperor Spirit has a very good record fresh. He came to us with a few issues and was freegoing at home. We put in a lot of work at home and bought him a special bridle to let him relax in his work, and I was pleased two furlongs in today that he was relaxing. He'll probably operate at around the 90 mark and will be a horse for those Saturday handicaps - Michael Keady, trainer.

runners.comment: Player on old form but a case can't be made on what he's shown during light 2025 campaign
runners.spotlight: Off a 3lb lower mark when very nearly winning a 6f Class 2 handicap here last October (soft) on final run for John Ryan; however, a case can't be made on what he's shown during a light 2025 campaign.

runners.comment: Except for his sixth of 19 in the Dash, he's been too quiet for comfort this season
runners.spotlight: An eight-time winner who should be well handicapped but, except for his sixth of 19 in the Dash at Epsom, he's been too quiet for comfort this season.

prev_owners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood
Fine Claret Racing & More Turf Racing
Fine Clarets Racing & Mrs E Burke

runners.comment: C&D winner and nicely treated even without taking into account Warren Fentiman's claim
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins include a C&D success; lightly raced this year and went close at Chester on her penultimate start; led clearly at Goodwood last time until fading over 6f; this looks more suitable and she's nicely treated even without taking into account Warren Fentiman's claim.

quotes.race: Juddmonte British EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Juddmonte/GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: They went a really good gallop, Harvanna stayed on nicely. She improved a lot from first time - Clifford Lee, jockey.

runners.comment: First and second in 5f handicaps before enduring a troubled passage at Southwell
runners.spotlight: An improving 4yo who was first and second in 5f handicaps (AW/soft) before his seventh at Southwell in a race that didn't go to plan; could yet raise the bar higher.

runners.comment: Hasn't won for a while but continues to threaten now and again; blinkers are new
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for a while but continues to threaten now and again, as when narrowly held over C&D a month ago; a tongue-tie returns with new blinkers added and that casts an element of doubt; respected, though.

quotes.race: Top Odds Every Day With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: Hiya Maite was unlucky last time that Jeans Maite was in the race and she was in form. On her day there are not many that can beat her round here, it was quite a competitive race as well - Lewis Edmunds, rider.

prev_owners.owner: Doug Graham, Alan Drysdale & Ian Davison
Doug Graham & The Late Ron Davison

runners.comment: Heavy defeats of late and he could do with the ground softening up back at 5f
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 4 handicap at Ripon on his return in April (6f, good; 2lb higher); heavy defeats have followed and he could do with the ground softening up back at 5f.

quotes.quote: There's only my daughter Sophie who rides Dakota Gold and he just does his work on his own. He's still keen at home and I said to Connor [Beasley, jockey] to just let him bowl along if he's enjoying it. We thought we'd just get away with the quick ground. He'd come on for the run - Michael Dods, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

Dakota Gold loved the ground. He got headed and he fought back and he's still got that enthusiasm for it. He's tremendous. He's not as good as he was, you don't expect them to be, but he can still race to a good level. Although there were only six runners, they were six good horses and he showed a good attitude - Michael Dods, trainer.

Dakota Gold looks better this year than last year - I was never really happy with him in his coat. Six furlongs on good ground is perfect for him. For an eight-year-old to go and do that, he's got some heart. It's nice to see him come back and hopefully he'll go on from here - Michael Dods, trainer.

They were coming to Dakota Gold but he's dug deep. He's finished for the season and we'll look forward to next year. He's getting better, I think he's improving big time. He dug deep here and if you look at his 5f race at Nottingham he was impressive. He'll definitely be looking at Group races next year - Michael Dods, trainer.

It was a plus when Stormy Girl came out as that meant Dakota Gold could dictate it. He loves it here, I couldn't tell you why it brings out the best in him but it's a good, flat track - the best in the country. I'm not ruling out running him again on Wednesday (in a Listed race at Nottingham) but otherwise there's only the Listed race at Doncaster at the end of the season - Michael Dods, winning trainer.

quotes.race: William Hill Beverley Bullet Sprint Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Dakota Gold is back to his best. It's been hard for him, being rated 110 from the start of the season. We thought he was coming back to his best last time in the Great St Wilfrid. He travelled and quickened but he had 9st 10lb and the weight caught him out. He was well placed by the boss, bringing him back here. He sprung the lids and his tactical speed did the job. It was good to get him back in the winner's enclosure -  Connor Beasley, jockey.

quotes.race: UK Hi-Fi Show Live Rous Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: The soft going, five furlongs and the stiff track suited Dakota Gold. He was always in control and showed his guts by outbattling them when they came to him. We will either finish him for the season or we will have a look at the weights in the Wentworth Stakes at Doncaster next month. If that is it for the year, we've got a lot to look forward to next season - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: LNER Supporting CALM Garrowby Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: I would say that is another career best. Connor said Dakota Gold was in trouble when they were coming to him, but once he sensed the horses were there he picked up and he's gone away at the end. He won't run in the Ayr Gold Cup as he has another penalty. There is a Group 3 at Newbury on Ayr Gold Cup day and that's a possibility - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet And Symphony Group Handicap
quotes.quote: We'll go for the Ayr Gold Cup rather than the Portland. Dakota Gold was 3lb well in today and only won three-quarters of a length so hopefully the handicapper won't go silly - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: William Hill Great St Wilfrid Handicap
quotes.quote: Dakota Gold is improving, with another year on him he's getting the six furlongs, he has put the race to bed at halfway - he showed so much speed - Michael Dods, trainer.

Dakota Gold is really a five-furlong horse but the ground came right for him. He had an injury at the end of January after getting a leg caught and cutting it so it's great to see him back. Connor Beasley gets on so well with him and there aren't many as strong as Connor. I'd say he'll be back at five furlongs for the Ebor meeting but he loves this soft ground - Michael Dods, trainer.

Dakota Gold has been difficult as he's a bit of a hot head. He's bad in the gates and is allowed to go in last. He's been running at six furlongs and I do think that's his best trip but he's just too keen. However, I won't be altering his distance now and we'll look for another race providing the ground is not too good - Michael Dods, trainer.

quotes.race: Watch Racing UK In HD Handicap
quotes.quote: Dakota Gold struggled to settle a bit last year but he's been dropped back to 5f and relished it there - Connor Beasley, jockey.
There's an engine there and Dakota Gold showed a good attitude, but he was just a bit keen and I wouldn't be frightened to go back to five furlongs - Michael Dods, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He always runs his race but he's an old horse now. I always felt he would become competitive again when he dropped below 90 and he's shown that this season. He ran well at York off 84 and then won at Redcar off 86 in May, then ran well again at Ripon off 90, which is his current mark. There's a 0-95 at Thirsk on Friday over 6f and he'll probably run there next, but he doesn't want firm in the going description. 28-08-24

runners.comment: Handicapped up to his best and any softening in the ground would come against him
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins this season but handicapped up to his best and any softening in the ground would come against him.

quotes.quote: Reigning Profit was a bit slower away than we'd hoped but it worked out well in the end. He likes it here - Ruth Carr, trainer.

quotes.race: Ripon Races Ladies Day 22nd June Handicap
quotes.quote: He did it well. Reigning Profit broke very quickly so I travelled strongly through the race and I think he was just getting a little tired near the line - James Sullivan, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap (Round 3)
quotes.quote: Reigning Profit is usually a good eater and he's a solid sprinter so I see no reason why he shouldn't go to Goodwood on Friday - Ruth Carr, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: A solid sprinter who had a busy 2024 running 20 times, scoring at Wolverhampton and Hamilton and finishing runner-up four times. A turning track over 5f suits him best. There's a race for him at Pontefract on April 14. 09-04-25

runners.owner: D Caslin, P Wilcock & M Cantillon
runners.comment: Better known for exploits at 5f, so latest fourth of 14 over Thirsk's 6f was respectable
runners.spotlight: Better known for his exploits at 5f, so latest fourth of 14 over Thirsk's 6f was respectable; won off this mark earlier this year and could have a part to play; good to soft fine but perhaps not softer.

quotes.quote: They went very quick - probably too fast - and Duran couldn't lay up with them early on, so we're chuffed to bits with that. That was one of the last real three-year-old-only races of the year we could go for, so we'll run him just once more for the year now - Nigel Tinkler, trainer.

runners.comment: Capable of a bold show but slow starts continue to hold him back
runners.spotlight: Four wins divided between Tapeta and turf (good); slow starts are becoming a habit and, all things considered, he has done well not to be beaten all that far on last two runs at York and Southwell (latterly in new cheekpieces that are now discarded); capable but risky.

runners.comment: Best at Pontefract but has finished runner-up here and he's feasibly handicapped
runners.spotlight: Has won four times at Pontefract and his other two wins were also on a turning track (Beverley); however, runner-up in one of only two visits here and he returns in a good run of form; well handicapped and goes on all ground.

quotes.quote: Ventura Express jumped a little bit slow but he soon travelled well through the race and he picked up and won nicely. I thought he would run well but I didn't quite expect that. He's been gelded which makes a massive difference because I don't think there's much else different than what Richard Fahey does, they're a lot better than us! - Paul Midgley, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been fantastic for us. He pays his way and is worth his weight in gold. The ideal scenario for him is a stiff 5f with an end-to-end gallop. He just failed last time at Pontefract, where they didn't go much of a pace. 14-08-25

runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win
runners.comment: Multiple winner who was second on stable debut; difficult to call him well handicapped
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner formerly with Ger Lyons and Mick Applebly; departed the latter yard for 15,000gns and it was a solid effort in defeat on stable debut at Sandown (5f, soft; winner followed up) last time; difficult to call him well handicapped, however.

quotes.race: Texas At Sandown Park 7 August Handicap
quotes.quote: Blind Beggar had dropped to a good mark and jumped much cleaner today. I was hoping to get a really clean trip for him and then gradually work through the gears. I thought we went an even gallop and I was keen to get the momentum rolling forward because I knew he'd gallop through the line - Luke Morris, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: We bought him at the October sales from Ger Lyons' yard with one win to his name. I've given him three runs on the all-weather since he arrived and he has won two of them at Southwell and Chelmsford. The most recent was six weeks ago and I've deliberately put him away now with a turf campaign in mind. He's effective over 5f and 6f and is up to a mark of 84, which looks quite workable. He's well capable of winning off that and hopefully improving some more. 22-03-23

runners.comment: A three-time winner over 5f on turf but not easily trusted after his last four efforts
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner over 5f on turf but not easily trusted after what he's shown in his last four races.

quotes.race: Boost Your Acca With BetUK Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was very straightforward. Moonstone Boy has been knocking on the door and deserved to win. Iain [Jardine, trainer] and Andrew [Mullen, regular jockey] said to me that he'd come on leaps and bounds from his first run here. He's got a lot of speed - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: A lovely sprinter who wants dig in the ground and with the dry weather his season has been on hold. I think it'll pay dividends in the back end now we're getting rain. He's one to go to Ayr with. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Ability is much respected but he's 1-43 on turf and usually finds a way of getting beaten
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in this 12 months ago; has run plenty of good races in defeat this year and Ascot last week was one of those given he was slowly away; however, he's 1-43 on turf and usually finds a way of getting beaten.

quotes.quote: Rose [Dawes] has given Existent a good ride and steered him through pretty well. No complaints there, and we are very pleased with the run. He's won nearly 50,000GBP in prize money this year just from being second and third - Stuart Williams, trainer.

Existent is a nice straightforward horse to deal with and hopefully he will now go on to the King's Stand Stakes. Tom (Morley, owner) and his mum have won the race twice before and we entered him for it last week. It is a bit of an ask, being rated 101, but we think he definitely warranted an entry and I think it should suit him. I think five is his best trip for us - Stuart Williams, trainer.

The race didn't go right at Ascot and Existent didn't come out of the race the way we would have liked. He's a strong horse now and as long as he's in good order then we'll look to go to the Horris Hill with him - John Gosden, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Getreadytorumble is feared but JER BATT should be a danger to all if the forecast showers arrive in the nick of time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Getreadytorumble, 6/1 Jer Batt, 8/1 Harvanna,  Blind Beggar,  Hiya Maite, 9/1 Tan Rapido, 10/1 Duran,  Ventura Express, 12/1 Super Saiyan,  Reigning Profit, 16/1 Existent, 25/1 Dakota Gold,  Tees Spirit,  Moonstone Boy, 33/1 The X O,  The Bell Conductor, 40/1 Emperor Spirit, 50/1 Almarada Prince

racecards.race_name: Land Of Lights Festival At Gulliver's Handicap

runners.comment: Four wins this year but she needs to produce more than she's managed the last twice
runners.spotlight: Course winner who has won four times this year; not ruled out but those 2025 victories came in small fields and she needs to produce more than she's managed on her last two starts.
