quotes.race: PricedUp Handicap (Jockey Club Grassroots Sprint Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: I wish every horse I had was as game as Hurt You Never. When she hits form, she's very consistent. There aren't many horses who win one or two races, let alone 13. She's a credit to herself and I wouldn't mind a yard full like her - Adrian Nicholls, trainer.

quotes.race: BetUK. It's Where The UK Bets Handicap
quotes.quote: She's a really game mare. I thought Hurt You Never might need the run but she showed she's in great form. Early on I thought I'd gone a bit quick but she just kept powering through - Mia Nicholls, jockey, talking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: She's been a superstar for us. She hasn't stopped winning and she's honest and tough. The soft ground wasn't ideal for her at Doncaster last week, but she was 4lb well in, so we gave it a go and she ran well to finish fourth behind Jojo Rabbit. She's easy to train and ride. She's won ten times and there'll come a point where the handicapper will have a say, but she'll be winning again. 26-07-24

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late Trevor Fox

runners.comment: On a long losing run and below par when last seen but perhaps a break will have helped
runners.spotlight: Without a win since September 2022 and down the field on his last two starts this May; however, perhaps a break will have helped and this course winner twice finished placed in April; not ruled out each-way.

stable_tour.quote: He's done really well over the winter and wants quicker ground rather than slower. We'll look at a summer campaign over six furlongs. Ascot suits him really well and hopefully he could be one for the Wokingham. 02-05-23

He ran a solid second in first-time cheekpieces at Pontefract and we thought he'd win at Thirsk but he got a bump there and it's taken him a long time to get over it. He's back galloping now and, with a bit of luck, he should be running soon. 14-08-25

runners.comment: Now 4lb lower than when winning at Beverley in April but hasn't shone more recently
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year (good-heavy) and he's 4lb lower than when winning at Beverley (5f, good) this April; however, his subsequent form has been mixed and he hasn't shone on his last two starts; a tongue-tie goes on.

quotes.quote: Trilby shows nothing at home. He's so laid-back for a sprinter. He's a pleasure to deal with and, because he's so easy and rideable, he just saves himself, which is great. He's been a great horse for us so far, so anything else he achieves is a bonus. He likes Haydock, and we all like a day out at York, so fingers crossed we'll keep tipping away with him. It will be fun to see what the season brings - Sam England, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: Sam: He's been a cracking buy for 2,000gns. He's best on a stiff sprint track.  Jonathan: He was a little disappointing last time in an amateur riders' race on the all-weather, but I think he's probably better on turf. He was unlucky not towin at Pontefract previously, he got there and pricked his ears in front and got caught. 08-02-24

runners.comment: Runner-up on his last three starts and he can be in the mix once more
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row last summer (6f/7f, good to firm) and he's finished second on his last three starts (5f/6f, good to firm/good), including in a big field; promise on soft ground on debut in 2023; in the mix once more.

runners.comment: Decisive win from the rear at Chester and this 3yo is unexposed under hold-up tactics
runners.spotlight: Some good runs in defeat this year, including over C&D, and he came from the rear to win decisively at Chester last month (5f, good; unraced on softer); this 3yo is unexposed under hold-up tactics and is entitled to respect.

runners.comment: Second of 14 at Thirsk last month and his subsequent run may have come too soon
runners.spotlight: Three 2yo wins last year (including C&D/soft); up and down this season but he was second of 14 at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) last month and his run at Musselburgh six days later may have come too soon; possible player.

stable_tour.quote: He's had a fairly frustrating summer as he's talented, but he started the year high enough in the handicap. He's quick - he wants the fastest five you can get - and he's starting to look well handicapped again. He's just been held up by some ringworm but he's started back breezing and is on the way back now. He goes on the all-weather as well. 04-09-25

runners.comment: Mid-division at Ascot last Saturday in a higher grade than this and he's in with a chance
runners.spotlight: Won five times last autumn on ground ranging from good through to heavy; inconsistent this season but wasn't beaten far in mid-division at Ascot (5f, good to soft) last Saturday in a higher grade than this; chance.

quotes.race: Download The At The Races App Apprentice Handicap (Hands And Heels Race) (Part Of The RE Series)
quotes.quote: Marching Mac is a five-furlong horse now and the stiff trip got him beat yesterday. He's a little bit keen and we had to hold on to him whereas back here in this sort of race, we can let him bowl along. Grace [McEntee] rides out at George's every day and she stuck Fred up for the ride. This horse came from George's as well so there's a lot of George Boughey connection there. He's entered at Chelmsford on Saturday in a 58 handicap because I knew he was 60 yesterday, the handicapper put him up for getting beat last week. Now I can run in a 58, rated 62 with no penalty for winning a kids' race. It's just stuff I love to do -  trainer Phil McEntee speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: In fine form in July and this 3yo is a major player if returning at the top of her game
runners.spotlight: Consistent 3yo who won at Thirsk (5f, good to soft) in July and finished close up on her next two outings (5f, good/good to firm); major player if returning from a 47-day break at the top of her game.

stable_tour.quote: She's won at Catterick in May and is ready to run again. She ran in the Hilary Needler at Beverley last time but she was a bit keen, so she'll  come back to a flat 5f. She's owned by the Dods Racing Club - we have put four or five horses in the club and people get a lot of fun out of it. 28-08-24

runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd
runners.comment: Won at Leicester in June but mixed efforts since and only sixth at Beverley last time
runners.spotlight: Won at Leicester (5f, good to firm) in June on his first run since being gelded but subsequent efforts have been mixed and he was only sixth of ten at Beverley (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago.

runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
runners.comment: Slowly away on last two starts but continues in good form and he's one to consider
runners.spotlight: Slowly away on his last two starts, which is a concern; however, in the circumstances he did well to finish fourth at sharp Chester (5f, good to firm) a fortnight ago and this 3yo won three in a row earlier in the summer (5f/6f, including on good to soft); he's one to consider.

runners.comment: Won at Newbury last month and short-headed at Newmarket since; solid candidate
runners.spotlight: 5yo who is lightly raced for his age and made it five wins from his last ten starts when scoring at Newbury last month (5f, good to firm; has also won on soft); short-headed at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) last time and he's a solid candidate in first-time cheekpieces.

quotes.race: DragonBet - Born From The Betting Ring Handicap
quotes.quote: HK Fourteen has just been learning his trade. He's a big horse with a nice pedigree and he's not been rushed. We've taken it one step at a time. We're very pleased with him. He looked like being a seven furlong horse or a miler but the more we ran him, the less it looked likely. He's a lovely horse and I think he can go on as we're hoping to go on the all-weather with him now - Tony Carroll, trainer.

runners.comment: Won on recent stable debut at Haydock and remains well treated on old form
runners.spotlight: 9yo who returned to form and exploited a reduced mark to win by a neck on his recent stable debut at Haydock (5f, good; effective on yielding in Ireland); remains well treated on old form and could play another leading role.

runners.comment: He can have issues at the start but won this last year and showed some spark two runs ago
runners.spotlight: He can have issues at the start (slowly away and always behind at Southwell last time) but he won this last year (good) and was an encouraging fourth at Yarmouth (5f, soft) two starts ago; capable if things drop right.

quotes.race: Fairfax Saddles Grassroots Series Final Sprint Handicap
quotes.quote: We gave Fantasy Master a racecourse gallop at Chelmsford to get him spot on for today and he's sluiced up. He loves it here and it's a big pot to win. Darryll Holland, trainer.

quotes.race: EBF Slip Anchor Maiden Stakes (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Fantasy Master has always gone well at home and we thought he was a nice horse, but he didn't show much on the AW on his first two starts. He did get a lot of the kickback last time at Southwell and we thought he'd be better on turf. I didn't think he'd win today, but thought he'd run well. He hit the line well despite hitting every undulation and soft ground will suit him better. He's entered in the sales race at Newmarket on Saturday and we'll have a crack if he makes the cut - Mick Appleby, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He raised a few eyebrows last season when winning his first race on turf as a juvenile at odds of 40-1. I was a little surprised to see him open at such a big price and I did have a few quid on him as he had been working very well and we thought he could go a bit. He proved that to be no fluke as he doubled up next time out in his first nursery off a mark of 76. We gave him a long break after his latest start in October and he has done particularly well during the close season, as he looks a much stronger individual now. I think he is a very exciting prospect for this year and he will be among our earlier runners on the turf. 29-03-21

runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr
runners.comment: Won at Beverley in July and not written off, but down the field on his last two starts
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground winner here who made it two wins for the year when scoring at Beverley (5f, good to firm) in July; not written off but he's finished down the field on his last two starts and has to bounce back.

quotes.race: Watch Royal Ascot On Sky Sports Racing Novice Stakes
quotes.quote: Jojo Rabbit got a nice lead this time rather than at Lingfield where he made the running and got hassled out of it. He's a nice type - Hollie Doyle, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: Found things a bit tight on the rail at Wolverhampton on Monday but ran on to be just behind the leaders. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Won two in a row in July and has continued in good form; could be thereabouts
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row in July (5f, good) and has continued in good form, finishing third on his last three starts, including on good to soft and heavy; could be thereabouts.

stable_tour.quote: He just needed the race first time out at Doncaster and he's in good form now. He's won over seven furlongs, but he's quick and is just as good at six furlongs. 23-04-24

runners.comment: 0-11 but perhaps unsuited by Chester last time and he was in good heart previously
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who posed no threat at Chester a fortnight ago but perhaps that tight track didn't suit; in good heart previously, finishing fourth at Newcastle (5f, AW; denied clear run) then third of 15 at York (5.4f, good; unraced on softer); this 3yo could bounce back and have a big say.

runners.comment: Won 2yo maiden last September but has struggled on both runs this term; cheekpieces go on
runners.spotlight: Won 2yo maiden at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) last September and she's dropping down the weights; however, she's struggled on both runs this season and a major turnaround is required in first-time cheekpieces.

quotes.race: Indigo Unified Comms EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (No More Than 3 Runs) (GBB)
quotes.quote: I think Thecla is getting quicker and I think she'll improve for going back to five [furlongs]. She has plenty of natural speed and I was hanging on to her for as long as I could today. She has a good attitude and is a nice filly going forward - Tom Eaves, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Well beaten at Pontefract last time but won over C&D in June on sole run here
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Pontefract last time and it's hard to be confident on the back of that; however, this 3yo won over C&D (good to firm) in June on his sole run here and is worth a second look; not ruled out each-way.

runners.comment: In the picture on his York third in July but no better than 7th across his four runs since
runners.spotlight: Third of 19 at York in July and he's in the picture on that form, even from out of the handicap; however, this 9yo has finished no better than seventh across his last four outings and needs to raise his game.

prev_owners.owner: Chris Kiely Racing Ltd & J Stokes

runners.comment: Seven runs this year and yet to be placed; 13lb out of the handicap today
runners.spotlight: Has run seven times this year and has finished no better than sixth; 13lb out of the handicap today and he would be a shock winner.

racecards.verdict: The 9yo ARNHEM won at Haydock recently on his first run for Jim Goldie and there's still plenty of mileage in his mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/1 Acrisius, 7/1 HK Fourteen,  Arnhem, 8/1 Bona Fortuna,  Brazilian Belle, 10/1 Azuinthejungle, 11/1 Marching Mac, 12/1 Al Hussar, 14/1 Roach Power,  Trilby,  Winchurch, 16/1 Lethal Nymph,  Bonnie's Boy, 20/1 Hurt You Never,  Jojo Rabbit,  Fantasy Master, 25/1 Solar Biricz, 33/1 Thecla,  Bayraat, 66/1 Dunnington Lad

racecards.race_name: Dropkicks Split The G Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Has shown enough in his two runs (Leicester and Ripon) to suggest there is a race in him.
runners.spotlight: Has shown enough in his two runs (fourth at Leicester and Ripon) to suggest that there is a race in him; this uncompetitive contest should see him in the firing line but he's not top of the list.

runners.comment: The most experienced of these, he is still be a maiden but has been runner-up five times.
runners.spotlight: The most experienced of the three, he is unlucky to still be a maiden having been runner-up five times; he faces a straightforward task here after finding a couple of York handicaps a tad too competitive in June and this could be a springboard to a rewarding autumn campaign.

stable_tour.quote: A nice horse who's been very unlucky to still be a maiden. We've probably placed him badly a couple of times and he's bumped into one or two. He's talented and can win off his mark. He could go for the Ayr Bronze Cup but I have a theory 5f on soft ground is going to be his bag. There might be a weak maiden to win with him and he'll strengthen up over the winter again. 04-09-25

runners.owner: M & M Racing, Paul Byrne And Partner
runners.comment: Three races; has not gone on since third on her debut at Hamilton in June last year.
runners.spotlight: Has not gone on since finishing third on her debut at Hamilton (6f, good) in June last year; had wind operation before returning from 14-month layoff when fifth of nine in Newcastle novice (6f, AW) 16 days ago and needs to show improvement on this drop down in trip.

racecards.verdict: It will be disappointing if FARANDAWAY isn't good enough to get off the mark here. Donald can chase him home.
racecards.betting_forecast: 1/3 Farandaway, 4/1 Donald, 6/1 Komorkis

racecards.race_name: Listen To Heart Scotland Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Still a maiden and only fifth over C&D 19 days ago; she needs to get back on track
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and only fifth of seven in nursery over C&D 19 days ago; she needs to get back on track.

runners.comment: Best effort when fifth at Beverley latest; very much one to consider on nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Posted her best effort when fifth of 13 in 7.5f maiden at Beverley three weeks ago; very much one to consider on her nursery debut.

runners.comment: Still looked green when 50-1 fourth at Chelmsford (AW) latest; player on nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Progressive Study Of Man colt who still looked green when 50-1 fourth of ten in 7f Chelmsford maiden  (AW) 24 days ago, showing much improved form; a player now stepping up in trip for his nursery debut.

runners.comment: Arrives in good nick but without having won; he can go well again
runners.spotlight: Arrives in good nick but without having won, headgear left off when fourth of eight in 7.5f Beverley nursery 15 days ago; he can go well again.

runners.comment: Has cut little ice in her three runs; this switch to handicaps needs to spark improvement
runners.spotlight: Has cut little ice in her three runs, seventh of eight in 7f maiden here 18 days ago; this switch to handicaps needs to spark improvement.

runners.breeder: Floors Stud Ltd & The Duke Of Devonshire
runners.comment: Has been brought along steadily; no surprise to see him take step forward on nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Has been brought along steadily, still green when eighth of 13 in 7f Thirsk novice 30 days ago; visor goes on for nursery debut and it will be no surprise to see him take a step forward.

runners.comment: Low-level maiden for M Botti; new connections; lots more required on first go beyond 6f
runners.spotlight: Just a low-level sprint maiden for Marco Botti and starts out for her new connections with lots more required on her first go over this much longer trip.

runners.comment: Little show in her three outings; hood back on with lots to prove going handicapping
runners.spotlight: Little show in her three outings, beating only one in 7f seller here last month; hood goes back on with lots to prove going handicapping.

racecards.verdict: Charlie Johnston's improving PARISIAN SCHOLAR can take another step forward to get off the mark on his first go in nurseries.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Supreme Dancer, 3/1 Truly Special, 7/2 Parisian Scholar, 6/1 Lexington Express, 8/1 Spirit Star, 12/1 Hood Wink, 16/1 Bobbys Pride, 25/1 Lanarra

racecards.race_name: Musselburgh Racecourse Premium Annual Membership Handicap

runners.owner: G P S Heart Of Racing Ltd & Partner

prev_owners.owner: G P S Heart of Racing (Bloodstock) Ltd

runners.comment: C&D winner; below form since finishing second of five at Leicester in June.
runners.spotlight: Won five times last year, one of those over C&D, but below form since finishing second of five at Leicester (1m4f, good to firm) this June; she has dropped in the weights and is dangerous to dismiss back here.

quotes.race: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
quotes.quote: We knew Lincoln Rockstar was really well but we weren't expecting that. She usually just does enough but she's put six behind them! She hit a flat spot during the race but we didn't expect she'd pick up in the way she did. She's entered at Musselburgh on Tuesday but we'll see how she comes out of this. We had a few races planned but the handicapper might take her off that. We'll have some fun with her - Ivan Furtado, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Hasn't fired on the Flat this year and a visor is back on, replacing blinkers.
runners.spotlight: Both Flat wins came at Beverley in May last year when trained by Ollie Sangster; scored over hurdles in May; hasn't fired on the Flat this year and a visor is back on, replacing blinkers.

stable_tour.quote: He's the first horse we've had for Jimmy Fyffe and was rated 71 on the Flat when trained by Ollie Sangster, winning at 1m2f and 1m4f. He's schooled very well and will start in a juvenile hurdle at Musselburgh at the end of November, with a view to looking at some of the lucrative juvenile hurdles if we can. 16-10-24

runners.breeder: Miss K Rausing & Sunderland Holding Inc
runners.comment: Consistent sort who got off the mark at the 19th attempt at Hamilton in July; good claims.
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who got off the mark at the 19th attempt at Hamilton (1m5f, good) in July; versatile trip-wise, he has continued to perform with credit, finishing in the frame over 2m at Beverley and Ripon, latest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); should have no problem with the drop back in trip and holds good claims.

runners.comment: Won over 1m6f here last August; below form since but fighting chance after 3rd last time.
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m6f here last August off 3lb higher but below form since; showed a bit more when third of five over C&D (good) 19 days ago, however, and a further 2lb drop in the weights gives him a fighting chance.

quotes.quote: Rory The Cat is very versatile. He's been in good nick. He won well and I'm sure he can go in again - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: tote.co.uk Hair Of The Dog Juvenile Hurdle (Go North Grey Bomber Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: When we tried Rory The Cat in the cheekpieces he just didn't go but blinkers have just made him. His jumping was perfect, Derek timed it brilliantly and he galloped on so well. It's just a great way to start the year and it's great for The Cool Cats. He'll get a handicap mark now but he'll definitely go for the Listed race here next month and he might even get into the juvenile handicap at Cheltenham, which would be great because he's so professional. He's just a brilliant little horse, as he was only 6,000gns at the sales - Lucinda Russell, trainer.

Inanna is a very nice filly. It was a nice fair track and we decided to send her up there because she had time to organiser herself. She could drop back, she is pretty versatile, but they didn't go much of a gallop. We like her at home and we will see how she comes out of the race. We have a nice bunch of horses, we have kept the numbers down, but hopefully the two-year-olds will be nice - Sean Woods, trainer.

She normally runs over 5f or 6f and might stay 7f if she relaxes. She ran over 7f at Musselburgh last month but was a bit too keen and finished second. quotes.race: Newmarket Academy Godolphin Beacon Project Cornwallis Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: I wasn't that confident beforehand (because of the ground), but Inquisitively is a very tough horse. He took the lead and grabbed the race from the get go. When I saw three horses in line halfway through the race I was a bit worried, but as William says he breathes very well and relaxes. When he asked him to quicken up he put it to bed very nicely. I think he is a five-furlong horse, but we will probably have to try the six (furlongs) later down the line. He was sold to go to Hong Kong, but he worked very well at mine so I asked if I could run him at York. He ran at York and won, and we decided to go for one more today. I would love to keep him, but we will have to speak to the owner. You have a question mark on the Breeders' Cup obviously. It is a long way out and we will have to discuss. It is not out of the question - Kevin Philippart de Foy, trainer.

quotes.quote: Von Baer just wandered around quite a lot. I was left in front a long way from home. I didn't really want to be as forward as I ended up being through the race and he was lonely from the three [furlong] pole onwards. I was just trying to keep his mind focussed in the right direction but he was always pulling out enough and he's obviously a pretty smart horse. I think he won despite the track - he did feel a bit unbalanced in the first half of the race - but the cut in the ground probably helped and he's still very lightly raced so there's plenty left to come - Saffie Osborne, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Bold Lad Sprint Handicap (Premier Handicap)

quotes.quote: I thought seven furlongs might find Big Gossey out, if it was six furlongs I think he would have won easy. Billy said he was just hanging on desperately. An eight-year-old on that ground against three-year-olds, it doesn't matter how much weight they are giving away, it's very hard for young horses to take on a battle-hardened horse like him. Gary O'Brien, who leads him up, does 90 per cent of the work with him and he just occasionally gives him a pipe opener and lunges him and doesn't have to do much with him. When he arrived in the door, I never thought he'd be doing this! A Gutaifan that couldn't be sold without much of a pedigree. Apparently he might come back here! Today was his 40th start at the track - Charles O'Brien, trainer.
Big Gossey got in well at the conditions and it's a perfect sort of race for him. He is in terrific form and we might find a race for him at Dundalk before next year - Charles O'Brien, trainer.

Big Gossey has been great for my career. I was delighted to see the rain as he thrives with a bit of an ease. He just loves it here. I would have loved something to bring me a bit further but I wanted to get rolling on him and not let something get first run - Robert Whearty, rider.

Big Gossey has been a great horse to me this season, he's a legend. He hit the line well when he won the Bold Lad, and over that ground and trip, I didn't think the seven would be a problem - Robert Whearty, rider

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF "Bold Lad" Sprint Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Big Gossey was a bit unlucky the last day when he missed the break. I travelled into it lovely and got a lovely run up the rail. He had a bit of a look when he got there but he stayed going - Robert Whearty, rider.

quotes.race: AK Bets "Higher Limits, Better Prices" Handicap
quotes.quote: Big Gossey is getting better as the year goes on and he loves it up the Curragh. I'm delighted that he's after getting his head in front today. I always thought six was his ideal trip but a fast run five like that suits him down to the ground - Danny Sheehy, rider.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF "Bold Lad" Sprint Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Big Gossey travelled throughout and picked up well. I was probably there a bit early but he saw it out. He has plenty of speed and did everything right - Michael Hussey, jockey.

Big Gossey came on from the last run. They went a good strong gallop which suited him and he settled lovely. When I gave him a squeeze he picked up really well. He toughed it out to the line so I'm very happy with him - Danny Sheehy, jockey.

But he gradually improved and won his maiden last year. I told the owner to hold onto him and thankfully he did - Eddie Lynam, trainer.

keke is a horse I would like to try over six. He's not the brightest horse in the world but when you light him up he can be keen. I would hope there might be a bit of improvement in him - Eddie Lynam, trainer
Keke had a good run last day. Once you get him relaxed he is straightforward. He switched off and travelled well, he was doing enough at the end - Colin Keane, rider

A horse I have a lot of time for. He was most unfortunate not to win on his debut at York last year when missing the break, giving up several lengths and only going down by a head. He has not done a lot wrong since, winning three times as a juvenile and ending up the season on a mark of 92. On his second run this season at York last time he defied top weight with an impressive win over 5f, but the handicapper has really hammered him hard with a 9lb rise. He was going for the Holyroodhouse after that but it will be tough now off 104. We will still head to the royal meeting and see how that pans out, but off his new mark we will have to start looking at Listed and Group 3 races for him. quotes.quote: Fitzella's run a massive race and it's always the 'what if' question when you're second. If Andre had decided against it this morning she would have won a Group 2 in devastatingly impressive fashion and she'd probably be favourite for next year's 1,000 Guineas. But that's not what happened. Luckily the remarkable filly she finished second to is trained a thousand miles away from where my horse is trained and we might find it rather easier to avoid her than if she were with Karl Burke. Oisin said it was definitely a career-best and she's got options; she's in the Moyglare, she's in the Cheveley Park and the Rockfel, while the Breeders' Cup remains an option as well. We were going further clear of the third with every stride so she stayed really well, we were just beaten by a better filly. She's every chance of staying a mile, probably not this year though unless we go to the Breeders' Cup - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

Oisin [Murphy, jockey] was upset when he came in after the Albany because he felt he sat too close to a very strong pace. He did realise and took back, but the damage was done by then and we felt on reflection that it was Fitzella's class which allowed her to finish fourth. The plan today was to hold her up and race more patiently, and to use her finish, but she absolutely pinged the lids and Oisin went to plan b. It worked very nicely and he didn't go hard - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

quotes.race: Betfred 'Supporting Macmillan' EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Fitzella has done it with the minimum of fuss. We've loved her from day one, I think she was our most expensive yearling last year. Everything we've done with her at home, she's learned and done it so much better the next time, which is exactly what she's done today. She's going to go up massively in grade and I think we'll go for the Albany over six as eventually she's potentially a miler. She's gorgeous and beautifully bred - Hugo Palmer, trainer.

quotes.race: COLM QUINN BMW Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Pivotal Attack won like a nice filly. Very professional, jumped and got a position, relaxed lovely and when I straightened up and gave her a squeeze, I liked the way she quickened. Hopefully there's more to come - Gary Carroll, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

Precise is a very professional filly. She has pace, and what you like about her is that when you get down into her she gets seven [furlongs] quite well. She looked comfortable. Just when I hit the front a furlong out she pricked one ear, which is always a nice thing. She travelled good and I hit the line well enough [to suggest] that going up another furlong is an option -  Wayne Lordan, jockey.

runners.breeder: Skymarc Farm Inc And Ecurie Des Monceaux

quotes.race: Jebel Ali Racecourse And Stables Anglesey Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: It was a last minute decision to run Suzie Songs but the beautiful thing about training for Moyglare is that you are allowed to do that. There are no set plans and I am blessed with owners like that. I can wake up and change my mind. We were watching the entries and this wasn't the plan, she hasn't worked since Cork. I just threw her in. I thought it got to her today so we will give her a wee break and return for the Moyglare. It is a race I would love to put on my CV. We have a horse last night who ran well and we have this filly so we have a nice bunch of horses heading that route. Colin said after her run in Cork that she wants further. I would have thought sticking to an original plan that her next race would be seven but this presented itself during the week with very few entries so here we are - Ger Lyons, trainer.We fancied Suzie Songs at Cork and she won, the form has been franked since. She's a nice filly, with size and scope and is bred to go further so that's when you will see the best of her - Colin Keane, jockey.

quotes.race: Sumbe Prix Morny (Group 1) (2yo Colts & Fillies) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Venetian Sun is unbelievable, she does everything so relaxed. I've been saying for a little while that she's a special filly and she's proved it there. Early on in the year she stood out in the string and I said to her owners a month ago, she doesn't quite stand out as much and the others have caught her up a little bit, but she's still got so much ability. She'll be better with easier ground. I was very worried when I walked the ground earlier, it's as quick as I've known it here and I've been coming for a number of years now. That dampened my confidence. She'll be better over an extra furlong as well, I'm convinced of that, so we'll go for the Moyglare next and see where we go from there. It will tell us if she's a Classic filly but she's got to prove it. She's got a temperament to die for - Karl Burke, trainer, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Duchess Of Cambridge Stakes (Sponsored By bet365) (Group 2) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: When Venetian Sun cruised to the front, she went a couple of lengths clear and thought it was all over. Ed's [Walker] horse quickened up nicely and Cliff [Lee] just said Venetian Sun was just dossing a bit in front. She is a filly a bit like that as she's so laidback and I also think the ground was on the quick side for her today. She is a very high-class filly. I think the Lowther would be out for her now as she has won a Group Two. She is in the Prix Morny and we'll have a look at that but we are very keen to step up to seven furlongs in the Moyglare. Her pedigree says she is more a miler at least and a three-year-old. She's a very mature filly with a great engine. I think she can be better on better ground and over a trip. She relaxes so well in behind. I think racing against the very best horses will bring out the best in her - Karl Burke, trainer.

I think Venetian Sun is a a very special filly. The work she has done at home, I haven't had a 2yo filly do work like it; just the last six weeks. She's kicking older group horses out the way at home. I worked a good, old work horse with her about a month ago or just after her debut and I had to work her with another good one that I knew was reliable and she did the same to him and I'm talking 110 rated horses. I think she's very special - Karl Burke, trainer. Karl did say she was the 2yo filly he's had, so we had a lot of confidence in her. So we were confident and happy to back him, and he did a great job with her. I don't think everything went for her - tough draw and she didn't have much cover, so yeah - Ian McAleavy, joint-owner.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Cliff [Lee, jockey] gave Venetian Sun a lovely ride and he said he ended up hitting the front too soon and she was just dossing about a bit in front. It's just greenness and she'll learn loads from that. It was a very good race for the grade and I would say she's very, very useful. If she goes to Royal Ascot it will be the Albany I would think, she's a real six-furlong filly - Karl Burke, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: Her work has really come together over the past six weeks and I think she's good. She's a big filly with plenty of scope about her and she'll improve a bundle for her first start at Carlisle. She's definitely a six-furlong filly and the Albany looks the right race for her. 12-06-25

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

racecards.verdict: The unbeaten Venetian Sun may not have the stamina to resist COMPOSING whose liking for this trip is already established
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Composing, 13/8 Venetian Sun, 6/1 Beautify, 10/1 Suzie Songs, 12/1 Precise, 33/1 Pivotal Attack,  Skydance

racecards.race_name: Bar One Racing Flying Five Stakes (Group 1)

quotes.quote: Art Power tries like hell and he ran his heart out. He's got an unbelievable temperament. We thought he'd run well because he wanted a fast seven, he ran well at York over seven furlongs too. If it had been softer it would have been better but he goes on any ground. We'll want to be going to the big race [British Champions Sprint] in October again at Ascot. Where we'll take him between, we don't know yet - Tim Easterby, trainer.

quotes.race: Qipco British Champions Sprint Stakes (Group 1) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: Art Power looked great today. He lost a load of weight when he ran in France but he's just come back. He's just brilliant. We gelded him in January, because we couldn't get a job for him at stud. He takes everything well and is great to train. I thought David gave him a fantastic ride. They broke well and when he travels, he's hard to beat - Tim Easterby, trainer.

Art Power has been awesome and he just loves coming to Ireland for some reason. Nobody knows why, but he just seems to come to life here. Bringing him back to 5f we thought might be an issue, but I've always been confident 5f was going to be okay for him, especially when the ground is soft. It was right up his street there and it's all worked out. He was powering away and I heard the commentator shout that he had gone clear too. We'll have to come back to Ireland now for any race we can find here - David Allan, jockey
Art Power loves it here and he has never been beaten here. He is just a fantastic horse. I cannot say that I was fully confident during the race but it worked out - Oisin Murphy, rider

Art Power bounced the gates, travelled strongly and put it to bed quite nicely. He did it much the same last year, so hopefully he can go to Ascot now and get a bit closer than last year - David Allan, rider.

Art Power travelled strongly and quickened up well. Obviously it was a drop in class and it should have done his confidence a lot of good. Hopefully he can pick up a big one soon - David Allan, rider.

Art Power is a lovely horse, very relaxed and he picked up very sharp. He's very straightforward. He felt like a Group 1 horse. He has a lot of speed and he has a great mind to go with it, for a sprinter especially - Colin Keane, rider

We've always liked Art Power and we took our time with him last year and it seems to have paid off now. I think that could be the end of his handicap days. I don't think dad will want to overface him too soon, but I think things like that (the Nunthorpe) or the Abbaye could be of interest to him - William Easterby, assistant and son of trainer Tim. When I went out there, in my mind I was hoping to get a lead but he was so quick off the gates no one could give me a lead so I just rode the race from the front. He's a very talented horse and I don't think we'll see him in a handicap again. I'd like to see him next year in a Group 1 around here - Silvestre De Sousa, jockey.

Art Power is a nice horse and Tim thinks a lot of him. He's got some classy entries, but he had to prove he could do it. He had a big weight today. Mentally he's not there yet, he's still a big baby, going into the gate he didn't know who he was and on pulling up he was waiting for the others. There's a lot of improvement in him. He's a fast horse, and coming back to five (furlongs) would be no problem - Silvestre De Sousa, jockey.

quotes.race: Morag's 10 Years At Racing To School EBF Novice Stakes (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Art Power is a little machine. We've had to wait with him, he's a late foal so we had to give him time. But he's got a great temperament, a fantastic nature. There's no reason why he won't run again this year, though I'm not sure where - Tim Easterby, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been in great form and I'm very happy with him. He ran a decent race out in Saudi Arabia, where the ground was too quick and he was drawn badly. He's training well and will probably run in the Duke of York Stakes, then go back to Ireland, where he's won five of his six races; I couldn't tell you why he runs so well over there. The plan after that would be the Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Stakes at Royal Ascot. He's seven now, but he still seems as good as ever. 23-04-24

runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

prev_owners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

quotes.race: FBD Hotels And Resorts Phoenix Sprint Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: A lot of credit to Adrian (Murray) and Robson (Aguiar) for the training performance there and David gave Bucanero Fuerte an unbelievable ride, just the way he likes to be ridden. I'm glad to see him back happy on the track. We're definitely not taking him out of Ireland now for a while - Kia Joorabchian, owner.

It was great for us to see Bucanero Fuerte coming back but I suppose from a breeding point of view it was disappointing. We were on a fact finding mission today to see whether he was going to train on and he was brilliant. He's only back six or eight weeks with us and you'd never think he was away at stud. He's so cool and calm, we even said to David [Egan, jockey] going out to wake him up a little bit because he's so relaxed. He finished out the race well, he galloped right to the line. He put plenty of daylight between them. There is a race in Cork in three weeks time and he'll be entered for the Nunthorpe. He's not in Ascot. We'll see after a couple of days how he comes out of the race and then make a plan from there - Adrian Murray, trainer.

Bucanero Fuerte is unbelievable and had to tough it out today. We had to come here because we'd as much done at home as we could and that run is going to bring him on plenty. He had a lot left in the tank. He did a spectacular piece of work at the Curragh last Sunday. It's on now to Ascot and we'll probably go for the Commonwealth Cup. We're really looking forward to it - Adrian Murray, trainer.

I think Bucanero Fuerte is a serious horse. That's three runs and three wins here, he's the horse of a lifetime. I'd say it'll be the National Stakes for him now and I don't think he'll have any problem stepping up to 7f. He would prefer an ease in the ground but he has won on good ground. The ground today would be a bit easier than the last day. He was very impressive in his maiden here on heavy ground but it's not an issue for him as he's won on quick ground as well - Adrian Murray, trainer.

I knew Bucanero Fuerte would battle because he needs every yard of 6f. He's a tough horse, really tough. He's a beautiful-looking horse too and, you know what, he's going to improve again. When he won his maiden in April he was only a shell of a horse. He is twice the horse now than he was then. Please God, we will be back here again for the Phoenix Stakes - Adrian Murray, trainer

I think it is a very good track and we reckoned going the straight line was the best way home. To be fair to Billy, he was brave and it worked. Erosandpsyche is a very straightforward horse, has a high cruising speed and lots of ability. He has entries in stakes races and will mix and match between handicaps - Paddy Twomey, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Erosandpsyche was good in the stalls today. He was very professional and a real sprinter type. He's a grand horse - Declan McDonogh, jockey.
When everything goes smoothly for him and Night Raider is relaxed in the prelims and at the start, he's a horse with a lot of natural ability - Karl Burke, trainer.
Night Raider was very comfortable going a good gallop. He beat two decent benchmarks in Wiltshire and Annaf - Oisin Murphy, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Boost Your Acca At BetMGM Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: The idea was to go straight to the Guineas with a racecourse gallop at the Craven meeting. Another run is probably the right thing to do, Danny [Tudhope, jockey] said Night Raider could do with another run, he's still green in front but he's obviously a very good horse - Karl Burke, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been purchased by Wathnan and the plan is the King Charles III Stakes. He got tired late on in the Duke of York Stakes but he was galloping into a headwind and the ground was probably a little bit tacky for him. He's come out of the race in great shape and all he's done is get quicker and quicker. I'm excited to run him over five furlongs and I think he has a lot of ability.   12-06-25

runners.owner: Mrs Anne Gaffney & Tom Gaffney & Liam J Butler

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Anne Gaffney & Tom Gaffney & Liam J Butler
Mrs A & Late Mrs T Gaffney/L J Butler
Mrs A Gaffney/Exors of the Late Mrs T Gaffney/Liam J Butler
Mrs Anne Gaffney & Mrs T Gaffney & Liam J Butler

quotes.race: Tote Never Beaten By SP Handicap
quotes.quote: Two Stars is improving with age I'd say. He's just one of those late maturing sprinters. You don't need to get into a row with him as he's a big-striding horse. He ran well at Doncaster last year but you won't see him running over six furlongs again. He likes that ground and we'll have to go up in class now, probably to stakes races and we might go to Naas - Fozzy Stack, trainer.

Two Stars had a setback after he ran here last time. I always felt he was a little better than his mark. Hopefully he can be competitive in some of those better sprint handicaps next year - Fozzy Stack, trainer.

runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

prev_owners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

quotes.quote: The ground was going a little bit softer than Arizona Blaze likes it because he likes really quick ground, but he just handled it very well. I think we'll aim for the Breeders' Cup. I think he'll get a Group 1 win somewhere - Kia Joorabchian, owner.

Big run, but Arizona Blaze never runs a bad race - he always turns up on the day, so I'm delighted with him. When he came home the last time he was a little bit flat and not himself, he was probably a little under the weather when he ran, so we put that behind us and we were very confident of a good run here. I couldn't believe the price! We are most likely to go for the Nunthorpe. He's entered in the July Cup, as well, but probably the Nunthorpe - Adrian Murray, trainer.

We thought this race might fit Arizona Blaze's schedule. Originally we had planned to go down the Guineas route with him and we ran him over seven at Leopardstown where he probably told us at that stage he wasn't going to stay a mile. He might take in a Group 2 at Haydock first, but his main target is the Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot. That's the 12th race of his career and he improves with every run - Adrian Murray, trainer.

quotes.race: Horse Racing Every Friday Until 11th April Race
quotes.quote: Lovely horse. Arizona Blaze Did nothing wrong last year and it was good to see him train on, I thought he looked a picture in the paddock, he's done well physically. He was always a big horse that was going to be better at three, hopefully he does that. There's only one Guineas and if he's going to stay a mile we'll have to try. If he doesn't stay he can always drop back to 6f - David Egan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV
Arizona Blaze has run really good. He's versatile ground-wise. Five furlongs on quick ground is probably the sharpest he would want. Really proud of him, he ran a great race - David Egan, jockey.

quotes.race: GAIN Marble Hill Stakes (In Memory Of Theresa Marnane) (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Arizona Blaze was a bit keen early but after a furlong he relaxed and he saw out the trip well. He's learning all the time and he showed a lot more speed here on this quicker ground - David Egan, rider.

quotes.race: Castle Star And Alkumait At Capital Stud Irish EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: Arizona Blaze is a very nice horse with loads of improvement and hopefully we can build on this. The further he went the better he got. We will be aiming to go to Royal Ascot and we will see where we go in the meantime. He will improve on better ground - Adrian Murray, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was third in the Norfolk last year and won a Group 3 recently. He maybe wasn't at his best last time at Haydock but he's extremely consistent overall and can run a top-class race for us. 13-06-25

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Committed Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: You would have to be delighted with Ides Of March too. I thought the ground was way too soft for him, he's a very good mover and is way better on fast ground. He's a good traveller, a high cruiser and doesn't lie down easily - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Heider Family Stables Round Tower Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: Ides Of March is powerful, fast horse. He finds it very easy to go quick. He travelled very easy through the race and he ran straight today. He looks like a Middle Park horse - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Revamp Conservation And Restoration Irish EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: Ides Of March could be a group sprinter. He's big, powerful and he's fast. He could go for the Middle Park and maybe have a run before that - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He wants it quick. When he gets his ground he's a very fast sprinter, and everything has gone well in the build-up. We've always thought the Commonwealth Cup would suit him. 13-06-25

runners.owner: P M Brant & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Derrick Smith

prev_owners.owner: P M Brant & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Derrick Smith
D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Committed Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: We thought both our horses were just about ready to start and wanted to hopefully get two runs into him before Ascot. The plan was to come here, go to Naas and then go for the Commonwealth Cup. Ryan [Moore] was very happy with Whistlejacket. He said he didn't get any cover and got a bit tired, but he was very happy with him. He said he didn't want to get into a barging match when he was needing the run and didn't want to blow him up at that stage, so he did the right thing and took his time - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Whistlejacket has run a decent race but Ryan [Moore, jockey] said he thought he could have done with a stronger pace. He was a bit slow out of the gates and took a lead whereas he likes things his own way - Aidan O'Brien, trainer

quotes.race: Sumbe Prix Morny (Group 1) (2yo Colts & Fillies) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Whistlejacket is a very fast horse. He's very quick. At the Curragh, it didn't set up perfectly for him but because he's so fast and precocious we thought he could do both races. It's hard for a horse to do both, especially when they're only a week apart, but speed is his big thing really  He's a five and six furlong horse. He's very quick so I'd say it's the Middle Park for him and then go from there with the Breeders' Cup as an aim as well - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Kingdom Of Bahrain July Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Whistlejacket is obviously still a bit raw and is still learning. It was a good performance - he has given away a bit of ground in the last furlong and gone a long way ahead. He is a fast horse and I think there is a bit more in there which gives us hope for the rest of the year - Ryan Moore, rider.
Whistlejacket is one we thought would win first time but we are very happy with that. He could go five or six, he has plenty of speed. He will probably got straight to Ascot - Aidan O'Brien, trainer

stable_tour.quote: The Naas race was a trial for the Commonwealth Cup, but it was a very messy sort of a race. They went slow early and nothing went the way we hoped it would for him. We think you'll see a different horse at Ascot. He's in good shape. When he's on song, he's a very quick and classy colt.  13-06-25

quotes.quote: It seems surreal, silly and it's all been a novelty. I've really been on a holiday with Asfoora the last two years. It was all getting a bit hard before Ascot as we didn't have the transport, and if I'm 100 per cent honest, if I owned her I wouldn't have come back. Akram [El-Fahkri, owner] was very insistent we did and that she deserved another chance. I'd love to see the replay as I was under a scrum of people for the last bit, but that looked very convincing. When you know they're going to win at the two-furlong marker, you're on pretty good terms with yourself. It was an easy watch and really enjoyable. She'll tell us if she doesn't want to be racing any more, but after that you can't say that's the case. She's been a once-in-a-lifetime horse for two years in a row and who knows, maybe three. That'll be a decision for Akram. He's a sportsman and would love to keep her going, why wouldn't you? We can't rule out coming back next year, but she owes us nothing - Henry Dwyer, trainer.

quotes.race: Coolmore Wootton Bassett Nunthorpe Stakes (Group 1) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: Asfoora has run well but she was 6-4 favourite so you like to see those ones win. She didn't so we're probably a little bit disappointed but it's not as though she's run tenth. She's run well but probably not as well as her last couple. I didn't like her last 50 metres, she was there to run second or at least third and she was a little bit plain in that last little bit. Whether she's reached her peak or she's on the way down, or whether she didn't like the track, I'm not sure. We might go to Ireland for the Flying Five but if anything comes out of the run and we're not happy with her she'll be going home - Henry Dwyer, trainer.

Asfoora has gone as well as we could have hoped and we're very proud of her. You could just tell about 400 metres after the start she just came off the bridle. She's quick but they were a bit quick for her early. Big Evs controlled his own destiny whereas we were stuck behind a couple and got a bit of a bump which probably cost us the win but she's run well and we're more than happy just to be competing - Henry Dwyer, trainer.

quotes.race: King Charles III Stakes (Group 1) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: We needed a lead-up run at Haydock, so we were always going to come early, but just with the changing of seasons we wanted to be here before it was too cold in Australia and too warm. We came over in the nice interchange period and Asfoora settled in brilliantly. The proof of that was in the pudding today. I've got to have a look at the race conditions again but we'd love to showcase her a bit. Everyone has just been so good to us over here and I'd love to send her down to Goodwood and up to York, but we'll put the horse first - Henry Dwyer, trainer.

runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber And Arthur Mitchell

prev_owners.owner: European Bloodstock Management & Mme Marie-Christelle Lacail

quotes.quote: Mgheera is a good sprinter and it's very exciting. We've got plenty of work into her, but she's always slowly away and that certainly wouldn't be a problem at Ascot. It should really suit her, especially that stiff five furlongs from being held up. There's a chance she might ship down to Australia after Ascot but we will have to persuade the owners to stay a bit longer as she would have a real chance in a Nunthorpe - Ed Walker, trainer.

It's quick ground out there and Mgheera loved it. Tom Marquand rode her at home and told me not to worry, she's got the action of a real fast-ground filly. Oisin [Murphy, jockey] nailed it. I was worried she was stuck behind [fourth home] Keran and that she wasn't going anywhere, but he wheeled out and got a lovely clean trip - Ed Walker, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's in great nick and has done everything right since joining us from France for this season, winning at Longchamp and then in the Temple Stakes. I'm really excited to see her step up in grade. She'll have to improve again but you wouldn't rule it out. 10-06-25

quotes.race: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: Nighteyes definitely has ability and is improving all the time. She has progressed into a lovely three-year-old. She picked up really well and hit the line very strong - Danny Tudhope, rider

stable_tour.quote: She could improve a lot this year and be right up there. She shouldn't have won that Listed race at Naas but ended up winning going away. She had an injury after her last run, but she's in good order now. She's a big, strong filly who should be getting quicker all the time. She'll probably start off over six in the Kilvington at Newmarket next month. 14-04-25

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Abergwaun Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: She's Quality is a filly with a lot of speed. She broke okay and I was in front soon enough, but I wasn't going to break her stride in first-time blinkers. She put it to the floor today on a fast track - Billy Lee, rider.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
quotes.quote: She's Quality is a filly that was different class to those today. Hopefully she can continue to progress and should do her confidence a world of good - Billy Lee, rider

I'd be lying if I said that I wasn't delighted by the rain. Vadream just loves it. She's a very good filly but she has to have her conditions and she got them today. I'd say (the Duke of York Stakes) is unlikely and will come a bit soon, we'll just run her where the conditions are right - Charlie Fellowes, trainer.

quotes.race: Pertemps Network Cammidge Trophy Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Vadream has always been a very talented mare but she's not the most straightforward. But she's always improved with racing, which is one thing we learned last year, and that is why we've given her a few runs on the all-weather. She loves soft ground and she loves six furlongs. She's always threatened to do something like that. She was frustrating last year, she ran too many times on quick ground - we know her a lot better this year and we'll stick to a bit of cut. She's in the all-weather championships on Friday at Newcastle, which is probably the only all-weather track where you could run her and go with a big chance. She's the sort of filly who could run again quickly so we'll look at that. Then she'll have a break and have an entry at York and possibly in Ireland. I hope we have a wet summer! - Charlie Fellowes, trainer.

Vadream has always been a good filly and has been a bit unlucky this year. She's run in some proper races and hit the post a few times. This has been the plan for a long time and we felt it was a real chance for her to win a Group race - winning trainer Charlie Fellowes.

stable_tour.quote: I think she's run better this year than ever. She was just beaten a head in a Group 3 at Newmarket and has run really well in the Jubilee at Royal Ascot and the July Cup. The Flying Five has always been the plan, but she'll head to the Sprint Cup first, then go to the Curragh and Longchamp for the Prix de l'Abbaye. We really want her to be at least placed in a Group 1 and if it comes up soft she can do that. 11-09-24

Readers of this column will recall I've been a huge fan of Vadream from day one and, after a good three-year-old campaign when she won the Group 3 Bengough Stakes, things were rather quiet last season when she was placed in Group company without winning. However, she loves soft ground, and this spring has been right up her street as she won on her turf debut when taking the Listed Cammidge Trophy at Doncaster. Last time out at Newmarket the ground was still on the soft side and she delivered in the Group 3 Palace House Stakes in what looked a good race. I won't run her unless there is give in the ground and she will be entered for every 5f and 6f Group race throughout the season in the hope we will get her ground as she is a 20lb better filly on soft. She is not a difficult filly to keep fit ad she likes to be busy, so you can run her at the drop of a hat. I would love to think it would be soft at Royal Ascot with either the King's Stand or the Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Stakes as potential targets. If it came up soft on the opening day she would have a huge chance in the King's Stand. 28-05-23

quotes.quote: Fregada got a touch of sore shins six or seven weeks ago and we backed off her. She did that very well, was impressive. She gallops all over anything we have at home. When she gets a bit of racing she will be really good - Johnny Feane, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Abergwaun Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Grande Marques has been knocking on the door in a few of these races. We've taken her to England about '25 times' and then she goes and wins one closer to home. She had the right draw today, was able to get that stand rail, which is a big help, and nothing really bothered her in front and took her on. She is in the Flying Five and we have nothing to lose. The sprinters seem to be beating each other this year - Fozzy Stack, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Rated Race
quotes.quote: Grand Marques had a good run first time at the Curragh but she was too free in her two runs after that and got caught in no-mans-land at Naas last time, so she has had excuses. She won well and will step up in grade now - Fozzy Stack, trainer.

runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cork Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Lady With The Lamp doesn't get any credit and was a giant price again today, but has beaten good fillies and colts. I know it wasn't her day as such, but she went off at a big price. She had done well physically and mentally and seems to be growing up. You'd love to think she could run in the Commonwealth Cup if you were going to go mad, but she has won two Listed races now - Kevin Blake, part owner.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Rest' Qual') (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: There was a strong headwind here so I was keen to follow something early on, Lady With The Lamp has got a great cruising speed and knuckled down well when it came to it. For me, I'd be dropping her back to 5f, it was six the last day and she paddle a bit late on, it was the same today in the last half-furlong - Billy Loughnane, jockey, speaking to Sky Racing.

stable_tour.quote: She's had a great season and hopefully we can have another big run at Ascot. She should love the straight six furlongs.  13-06-25

runners.owner: Mrs M Roden & Mrs R Nolan

quotes.quote: Town And Country was keen. I was told at home she can be, but I didn't think she'd be that bad. She ran into a couple of pockets, but she only got out in the last half-furlong and she did well to win. I think when she gets used to sprinting, the quicker they go the better as she can relax, and then we'll see the best of her. I think she can win in Group company - Colin Keane, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

racecards.verdict: Winner of four of his seven races at this venue, ART POWER is tipped in the hope he can hit top form on his second start of the season
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Asfoora, 5/1 Bucanero Fuerte, 6/1 Arizona Blaze, 8/1 Night Raider, 10/1 Art Power,  Whistlejacket,  Mgheera,  She's Quality, 12/1 Two Stars, 16/1 Nighteyes, 20/1 Powerful Nation, 33/1 Erosandpsyche,  Grande Marques, 50/1 Lady With The Lamp,  Saratoga Special, 66/1 Vadream

racecards.race_name: Goffs Vincent O'Brien National Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)

quotes.quote: Ryan said Brussels was very babyish, he's never been away. He will come forward a lot for it and all these horses are running with Ascot in mind but Ryan said it might come too quick because he was so babyish. We'll see how much he comes on. He said he'd have no problem getting seven and he's a massive, big, powerful horse - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Barronstown Stud Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Lovely, straightforward horse, Dorset came forward lovely and he'll get further. Plenty of options, he could go for the Tyros or the Superlative - Aidan O'Brien, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

prev_owners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

quotes.race: Sumbe Prix Morny (Group 1) (2yo Colts & Fillies) (Straight Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Gstaad ran a great race, we're delighted. It's a long time since he ran but we're over the moon. We had it in our head he could go back to the National Stakes, so that's where he could go next. He'd have no problem with seven furlongs - Aidan O'Brien, trainer, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

Gstaad looks a very good horse, doesn't he? We've always loved him. He had only had the one run. He has always worked like a good horse. He's big, he's scopey, he has a good stride and is very genuine; he goes with his head down and he has loads of speed. He looks like he will get further than six [furlongs] - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.Gstaad was very impressive. I thought he travelled beautifully, and we got to the front a long way from home. He's a tall horse and is obviously improving with each run and that was a very good performance - Ryan Moore, jockey.

He was a little bit babyish going down but Gstaad was a pro in the race. When Wayne (Lordan) kicked he got a bit disorganised but from the furlong pole I always thought I was going to get there. I'll think he'll sharpen up for it, he'll get another furlong - Chris Hayes, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

stable_tour.quote: He's a Starspangledbanner colt who quickened really well to beat a filly of ours who we like at Navan. Chris Hayes rode him that day and liked him. He's come forward nicely at home since then and the Coventry is the plan.  13-06-25

quotes.quote: Italy ran very well. He's a lovely big horse. He will progress - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

He was never away before so Italy would have been very green, and they went a good gallop. He has a bit of quality and Wayne gave him a lovely ride. He taught him plenty and he will have leaned a lot from that. He could come back here for the Tyros - Aidan O'Brien, trainer

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

prev_owners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

quotes.quote: Saba Desert is a horse we liked from the get-go and were pleased with the way he did it at Sandown. He progressed from Sandown to here and we always try and find one for this race. He is a lovely deep-girthed horse and we'll work back from a Dewhurst now. Whether we go the National Stakes or go to the Champagne and then the Dewhurst - we'll see. He'll have a bit of a break now as he has a Group Two penalty - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: When you ask for Saba Desert, he's there immediately ? he's a very talented colt - William Buick, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.

Zavateri is just so tough, he had to win his race twice. He went to win it and then William [Buick] came at him so he had to win it again. He just amazes me, he's such a dude and a lovely horse. He cut through the pack like a knife through butter - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

quotes.race: Kingdom Of Bahrain July Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Zavateri and Havana Hurricane [winner of the Windsor Castle Stakes at Royal Ascot] are different horses and never work together - this one probably wants seven now, maybe a mile, and Havana Hurricane wants five or six furlongs. They don't win nothing at home so we don't do very much but they are both really, really nice horses. I think Zavateri could stretch out to a mile next season, he's got the pedigree for it. I love this horse, I always have done. Everyone said why don't you go for a novice but I said why do you want to go for a novice and get beaten with a penalty by a Group horse? Come to a Group race. I am Eve Johnson Houghton with 80 horses - not John Gosden with 150 or whoever but he [Zavateri] is one of the nicest I've had and he's done nothing wrong so far - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

quotes.race: Clive Cox Racing British EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (Qual') (GBB/IRE Inc) (Div II)
quotes.quote: Zavateri did it really nicely. He is still a big, green baby, but I'm very happy with the way he did it. Ascot is coming up a bit quick, so that's unlikely, but maybe we'll find something at the July meeting for him - Eve Johnson Houghton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a really nice horse who won impressively on his debut at Salisbury this month by two and a half lengths. We'll probably go to the July Stakes next at Newmarket. It's hard to win a novice with a penalty and he was so impressive at Salisbury that we might as well go to Newmarket. 02-07-25

racecards.verdict: Stron claims held by British challengers Saba Desert and Zavateri but GSTAAD could be the one to beat if staying
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Gstaad, 100/30 Saba Desert, 9/2 Zavateri, 8/1 North Coast, 9/1 Italy, 16/1 Dorset

racecards.race_name: Comer Group International Irish St. Leger (Group 1)

quotes.race: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Grand Cup Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Al Qareem is so honest and brave, and very sound. He just does it every time you ask him to. Even when the favourite came looming up, I had a little smile on my face when the commentator said he was coming at him as you know he's going to lengthen and keep finding. The Ebor I think he'd be handicapped out of it, but the Irish St Leger looks a perfect race for him. It's a lovely, big galloping track and stays the trip so well. There and the Curragh Cup are likely to be the next two races for him - Karl Burke, trainer.

quotes.race: Barry Hills Further Flight Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: The ground, I felt like Al Qareem probably just got away with it today. All he does is gallop when you let him down - Clifford Lee, jockey.

I tried not to fire Al Qareem out of the gates, but he naturally jumps quickly anyway and going into the first bend he was half taking me on. I've got a lot of confidence in him and the petrol gauge never really fades. He just keeps galloping. He tries so hard and you know he's going to give you 150%. Even on the wrong ground he finishes on the backsides of the leaders, but he loves it when the going gets soft - Clifford Lee, jockey

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Stand Cup Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Al Qarem ran really well and he's a great horse to own. Every time you think you're going to have a quiet spell, up he pops and he puts a smile on our faces. We had him in the Irish St Leger and at various times during this week I thought we were going to run in Ireland, but I'm happy with the decision to run here. We're planning to run him twice again this season and he has options at Newmarket in a couple of Listed races and then we'll look at something else - Nick Bradley, part-owner.
All Al Qareem does is gallop and he loves these stiff finishes. I always thought I was going to get back up because my lad tries hard and keeps going - Clifford Lee, jockey.

Everything went right and Sam James gave Al Qareem a great ride. I thought we were overpriced but you never know coming off a break like that how they're going to run. He picked up an illness when flying from Dubai which hit him pretty hard. We thought we might not get him back on the track this year but Karl has done a brilliant job - Nick Bradley, syndicate manager.

We thought Al Qareem would win and he's been crying out for a step up in trip. He'll come into his own this season and the form of his Kempton fourth last year has worked out amazingly - Nick Bradley, managing director of owners.

stable_tour.quote: He probably bumped into a good one [Internaute] on his comeback at Saint-Cloud but showed all his battling qualities to win at Nottingham on Wednesday. I don't think he really sees out the two miles like a strong stayer but he definitely needs soft or heavy ground over a mile and a half, and the Ormonde at Chester's May meeting looks the obvious next step. 11-04-25

He was a huge improver last year with his handicap mark shooting up from 81 to 112 (31lb), which included victory in the Group 2 Prix Chaudenay (1m7f) under a cracking ride from Ryan Moore. We took him to Riyadh and Meydan earlier this year but he disappointed and I don't think the travelling suited him. He is having a break now and will be back for the second half of the season with the Ebor a likely target. 07-05-23

runners.breeder: Sarl De Chambure Haras D'Etreham Et Al

quotes.quote: That was brilliant. Al Riffa has got a lot of class and he was last to first in a National Stakes here. No one ever felt that he needed that trip but it made sense. He was very relaxed and Dylan said he was a very easy ride over that trip. I'd say it's most likely that he'll go straight to the Irish St Leger from here. There is a possibility of the Pries Von Berlin he won last year but I'd say it's more likely he'll go straight to the Irish Leger. The Melbourne Cup is in the mind thereafter. We all know what comes with that but it's what we'd like to do, if we're allowed - Kevin Blake, stable representative.

Fantastic run, probably unlucky to meet a horse like Rebel Romance in that race, but we're very proud of Al Riffa, who is a real star for us and he's run his legs off for us again today. Hopefully there will be plenty more big days with him to come this season. He's a beautiful horse and he always runs to a rating not too far off 120, and it's hard to find horses like that. Obviously he's a multiple Group One winner and we were probably unlucky to meet a real champion in the Hardwicke. Rebels' Romance is one of the best horses in the world over the last number of years. I wouldn't be against trying a little bit further with him as well - he's got plenty of stamina in his pedigree. I think we'll probably think outside the box with him a little bit now - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

Al Riffa ran a really good race and came home strong. It was his first race, so he was entitled to that. We are delighted with him and we'll see how he pulls up and make plans from there - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

It's been a wonderful day and Al Riffa put up a really good performance so we're very proud. Dylan [Browne McMonagle] gave the horse a fantastic ride. He's had a busy season so far, racing in France, Britain, America and now Germany, so I think he'll have a little rest and then be prepared for the Arc - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

We'll probably make the rest of the season all about the Arc, which will be his main target. Al Riffa should enjoy the mile and a half as his family is all stamina. It looked like mile and a half will suit him on his run today - Jospeh O'Brien, trainer.

Al Riffa ran great. It was a tactical race, but I'm very proud of his run and looking forward to his season ahead. He came there with a strong run and maybe got a bit tired. He's a big colt and hopefully he has a long season ahead. He has options and the Tattersalls Gold Cup or the Prix d'Ispahan would be two obvious races for his next start. Also we're keen to explore 12 furlongs as we think the Arc is a longer-term target so we'll be plotting our route to there - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

Obviously the winner is a champion and we're very close to him. Christophe liked Al Riffa a lot and we've a lot to look forward to. The last run was decent but it's nice to see him back to his two-year-old form today, and he finished very well. It was good to keep him against three-year-olds again before taking on older horses next time, which will probably be in the Irish Champion Stakes. We'll look at the Champion Stakes afterwards - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Goffs Vincent O'Brien National Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: It was a special performance. Al Riffa won his maiden well and the owners were keen to supplement him for this. It was a very impressive performance to come from the back of the field like that. He was a big shell of a horse as a yearling and I thought he'd make a lovely 3yo, I didn't for a minute think he'd make the two-year-old that he has made. His family is all stamina, so for him to be doing this at this stage is very positive for the future. We always thought he was a middle-distance horse, and he's closely related to an Ascot Gold Cup winner, so that's real stamina, but he's not slow. I'd imagine he's a Guineas horse for sure and we'll take it from there. He's a classy horse and very exciting. The plan coming here was to go on to the race on Arc day (the Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere) but we'll see how he comes out of it today and discuss it with the owners - Joseph O'Brien, trainer

quotes.race: John Ormonde Wexford Sand Irish EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: Al Riffa is a grand horse. He was first off it, niggling, but he's a big horse and still immature. The future is bright, he could be anything. He goes on quick ground for a big horse but he might just be better with a bit of cut in it. The National Stakes has been mentioned. I think he'll be a cracking three-year-old - Brendan Powell, assistant trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He drops in grade and goes in the Hardwicke. He's been running consistently well at a high level and I think the drop in class should give him a live chance. 13-06-25

A smart horse and, after a particularly catching run on his debut when a fast-finishing second to Hans Anderson, he won his maiden at the Curragh as we expected. His work after that improved and we felt he was worth his place in the Group 1 National Stakes which he won very nicely from my brother Donnacha's colt Proud And Regal. That was his last run of the campaign and he looks a much stronger three-year-old this year, more the finished product. He was our nicest juvenile last year and carries our hopes this year. The plan will be to have his first run in the Irish Guineas at the end of May and we will then stick to the mile for his next run. After that, depending on how he is doing, I may consider stepping him up to 1m2f, but I don't see him taking up his Derby entry. His pedigree and the work he is doing where he shows a lot of pace says he is quick enough to stick to the mile. He has been doing all we have asked him this spring and I'm looking forward to getting him out on the track again. 12-04-23

quotes.quote: Arabian Crown is a very uncomplicated horse and I think everyone will be pleased with that, I certainly was and it's quite testing out there. It's early in the season and they are entitled to improve. I would be as confident as you can be that he'll stay a mile and a half - William Buick, jockey. He's got to be the main Derby horse at the moment, although there are a few other irons in the fire. I don't know if he needs another run beforehand but if he did it would be Chester as York may be a bit close to the race - Alex Merriam, assistant trainer.

Arabian Crown as it stands and on pedigree has more of a Derby profile. We will be going down more of the Derby route, and he could be a Dante horse or one we look at one of the other Derby trials. He is a very hard horse to assess this fellow as he is not a work horse. James (Doyle) rode him in work last week and he was out at the back of the gallop, whereas Ancient Wisdom does travel and he has a bit more natural pace. This horse has won a Listed race and won a Group Three and I will put him away now for the year. One of our two previous Derby winners was a decent two-year-old winning the Solario Stakes in Masar, and this fellow has a slightly different profile. He has a pedigree that suggests stepping up to the mile and a half as a three-year-old will suit him. He is an exciting horse there is no doubt about it - Charlie Appleby, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Stonehenge Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Arabian Crown has really progressed from every run he's had, that being the third of his life. Today was a test and he saw it out well. I like the way he does everything - William Buick, jockey.

quotes.race: Martin Densham Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
