quotes.quote: Arabian Crown has stepped forward nicely from his first run and it's nice for him to step forward and get his head in front. He handled the ground, its proper soft ground so he's never going to look electric on it. Stepping up to a mile would probably suit him in time. He's seen that out well and just speaking to Charlie (Appleby) there he mentioned stepping up to Listed level over a mile at Haydock next. He's done nothing wrong, he's a very straightforward Dubawi and he has been doing everything nicely. William mentioned that in future he will definitely be running over further - Alex Merriam, assistant trainer.

Crystal Black relaxed and switched off better than I thought he would, quickened up and got there sooner than I wanted. He was inclined to go a bit left towards the rail but once against the rail he went true to the line. The aim is the Melbourne Cup - Colin Keane, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

I was thinking that they were going a good pace. We were travelling, but I thought we got off a bit early. I knew Crystal Black would keep galloping. That was the first time without an ease in the ground - that was his first time on fast ground. We were more afraid of the ground than we were of the distance, because it was his first time over the trip as well. I imagine Group races and Listed races, that type of thing - he's probably finished in handicaps, I would imagine - Gerard Keane, trainer.

Crystal Black is a good horse, it's great to get a good one. He has to be a black-type horse now. The boys want to go to Ascot, it will be either one of the big handicaps or the mile-and-two Listed race. He'll be entered in two or three of them - Gerard Keane, trainer.

Crystal Black had a lovely run first time and came on a good bit, he didn't travel most of the way there but he hit the line well. He could be a nice horse and he'll get a bit further too - Oisin Orr, rider.

runners.breeder: E Dieuaide & Domaine Billard Et Fils

quotes.quote: Dallas Star will be entered in all the big races now I suppose and we'll see where we go. I'll have to chat to Kia [Joorabchian, owner] and Robson [Aguiar] and see where they want to go. He came to us over the winter and is a lovely horse - Adrian Murray, trainer.

quotes.race: British EBF Future Stayers' Novice Stakes (Sire And Dam Restricted Race) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Dallas Star is s a lovely horse and has always been a big, raw type. His first run was good at Haydock and then we pitched him in deep in the Chesham, and he ran well, but I stress he's big and raw and has taken time. Rossa says he'll be nicer again next year when he'll want further. I remember saying to someone at Haydock he could be an Ebor horse one day and I haven't changed my mind - he'll be a nice, staying type. This ground isn't essential, but he enjoys it - Dominic Ffrench Davis, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We've always thought he's a stayer, so we're going to have a crack at the Queen Alexandra. He's never run beyond two miles but I think he should get the trip. 13-06-25

He's been running really well. He came to us with a big reputation from Robson Aguiar, who pretrains all the Amo horses, and he thought a lot of him. He has proved to be a nice horse and I remember saying after his first run that he might be an Ebor horse one day and I still think he will be. I think he'll be a lovely staying type of horse, it's just a case of keeping him on the right side of the handicapper and bringing him along nice and slowly. He's had a bit of a break but you can expect to see him out before the end of the year and we'd like to find him a race he could win rather than pitching him in too deep. 21-09-23

quotes.quote: Harbour Wind has run a very good race. In that ground, it's probably as far as he wants to go. He's tried hard, he's run well and he looked like he was going to win a furlong down. The ground is probably a little bit slower than we'd like, because he's won on firm ground. He's been a long time between races and I think he'll come on for it. We'll think about it [the Melbourne Cup]. I'll have a chat with his owner, Eva-Maria Bucher-Haefner, and we'll go from there - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Martin Molony Stakes (Listed)
quotes.quote: It was a workmanlike, battling performance from Harbour Wind under a very good ride from Chris Hayes. We'll see how he comes out of the race and discuss future plans with the owner as to where we go next. He'll be sharper for that. The Melbourne Cup is in my thoughts, but it is early days and we will see how the year progresses. He has to step up considerably - Dermot Weld, trainer.
Harbour Wind has run a big race and we're very pleased. He'll make into a lovely horse next year and that's it for this year - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Aer Lingus College Football Classic Vinnie Roe Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: Harbour Wind is still a big, immature horse. He's a next-year horse and will be trained accordingly. We'll talk about whether to run him again this year - Dermot Weld, trainer.

Harbour Wind did it well. It was a funny sort of race as I think something happened to Colin Keane's reins and you can't read too much into the distances on testing ground. The one thing we didn't know was would he handle the ground and he handled it well. He came up the hill well. We'll take it from there and see how he progresses - Dermot Weld, trainer.

runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

quotes.quote: We thought Illinois would be a Gold Cup horse next year, and obviously it was the plan Kyprios would come here this year and he would step up next year. The plan was for him to go to the Coronation [Cup] and I was training him as a good mile and a half-horse, and then when Kyprios retired we switched him to this race. We have to be happy with the run and he had a lovely preparation the last day. Maybe if we knew he was coming here straightaway, we might have had him going earlier and maybe lighter. He might go to Goodwood and meet this horse again and I would imagine he will progress again then. He got tired at the two-furlong pole today, like a lot of horses do, and we will see if we can improve him another little bit for this distance. It shows how good Kyprios was as Trawlerman had been second to him a couple of times, I think - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Big-Value You Can Bet On Ormonde Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: That was very pleasing, he's a lovely horse. Illinois is a big, fine, strong animal so he will improve for the run. Ryan was very happy with him. He was waiting for someone at the bend to take him there and they didn't so he just had to go. He was just playing around in front at the end there but he's a lovely horse. He's very versatile. We've just been talking to Aidan and you could drop him back in trip for the Coronation Cup or you could step him up to Cup races. He'd do whatever you want for you. You can see what he did last year, he's a proper horse. He's a two-time Group 2 winner and carried the penalty today and did it really well. He's a lovely horse to have, we're lucky - Paul Smith, representing the owners.

quotes.race: Betfred St Leger Stakes (Group 1) (No Geldings) (British Champions Series)
quotes.quote: They are two very good horses and clearly there's nothing between them. Sean gave the winner a very good ride and neither stopped all the way down the straight. We knew the winner had improved a lot since Goodwood and he's a classy horse. Either of them could go to Melbourne, they're two classy stayers but Illinois would have no problem coming back to a mile and a half - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Great Voltigeur Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Illinois stays very well and looked like he was going to lose second, but fought back and that usually suggests that he wants that staying trip, as he showed at Ascot too - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

We felt any of the three could win. Ryan made the decision to ride Illinois, and he had the choice of the King Edward VII Stakes or this race. He's obviously a St Leger-type horse and is going to improve from three to four. He's a bit of a baby mentally still, so he might have a little rest now and maybe go to York on the way to the Leger, something like that. I think he might have a little bit more class than a Cup horse. His sister won the Arc, so he's that type  - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Illinois ran a lovely race and just got a bit tired in the deep ground. He was working lovely, but he's a big horse and we didn't want to over-squeeze him for his first run because we felt the season was going to be long for him. We've often felt with those type of horses that if you over-squeeze them early in the season, it can catch you in the middle of the season so we just had him ready to run and he ran a lovely race. Ryan said he struggled in the ground and he will come forward a lot from it - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.
Ryan was very happy with Illinois. He's still a baby and will improve plenty - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Shoda Market Cafe Irish EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: Illinois is a beautiful colt. There's not too many that when you go down past the two and a half, they say point me. I actually got there too soon because I thought he'd take a bit of stoking. He has a lot going for him - pedigree, scope, size, action, wind. He was pretty much a steering job and I'm going to miss these Galileos - Seamie Heffernan, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He was always there to slot in if anything happened to Kyprios and that's what has happened. Everything has gone well in the build-up. We were delighted with him at Chester as we felt he would come on a lot for it ? he hadn't run since the autumn. The thing about Illinois is that he stays very well but he's got a bit of class to go with it, and that's what you need in a Gold Cup. You never know for sure whether they will stay the Gold Cup trip until you try it. Once you go beyond two miles, you're entering unknown territory. We'll find out at the same time as everyone else, but we always thought he would stay. We've been delighted with him at home over the last few weeks. 13-06-25

runners.breeder: Al Shira'Aa Farms S A R L

quotes.race: Comer Group International Irish St. Leger Trial Stakes (Group 3)
quotes.quote: A steady pace, Leinster was a bit in my hands all the way and I would have loved to go a stride quicker. I needed to get him to relax today because he was doing too much the last couple of runs. Coming back in trip and up in grade I thought there would be more of an even gallop on but we did go slow again. I was comfortable at the 2f so I just let him flow, he's a good horse - Dylan Browne McMonagle, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

Leinster had a real nice run at Roscommon and had a setback so it was unfortunate he missed the rest of the season. Hopefully he can be a progressive stayer. He looked at Roscommon as if the trip was a bit short for him so we were looking forward to stepping him up and I don't think stamina will be any issue to him going forward. The form of the Roscommon race was strong and he handles soft ground well - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He had a really good run last time when fourth in the Chester Cup. He'd won at the Curragh over a mile and a half before that and dropping in trip for the Copper Horse should suit him. 13-06-25

He looks like he stays very well, but whether he will stay the extreme distance of the Queen Alexandra is another thing. You never know until you try and he's put up two good performances in defeat against Kyprios this season. 11-06-24

quotes.quote: Uxmal was very good. We had a nice prep run the last day in Killarney and he did it really well. He was always going to handle the ground and the step up in trip was never going to be a worry and Joseph had him spot on for the day. It's great to reward him like this. I know it's the last race and he's been hitting the crossbar but I would like to thank him and the owners for giving me this opportunity. This lad is really relaxed and when I hit the front I could really hear the noise. I just kept my head down and made sure I we got the job done - Dylan Browne McMonagle, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He was an impressive winner of the Queen Alexandra last year but picked up a little niggle in the race so didn't run again. We'll work our way back to the same race this year. He undoubtedly has plenty of stamina and it's a race that suits him. 24-04-25

quotes.quote: I thought the last half furlong Amiloc wasn't looking for the line, he was feeling the ground more than anything else. I think we'll bear that in mind in future but he's a gelding, we had conversations last night and this morning about whether we should come. Patrick Cooper, Mr Aykroyd's nephew and Mrs Aykroyd's racing manager, decided this was a good idea and I agreed so it all worked out. I think ground will dictate, I wouldn't be afraid to take anybody on with him on slower ground so he is in everything. He's going to take a bit of getting over today by the look of him; if it came up soft next month [for the King George], or with a bit of juice, I'm sure we'd be here - Ralph Beckett, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Cocked Hat Stakes (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Amiloc races a lot better than his work and he's obviously very talented. His dam [Colima] was tricky and it's never been that straightforward. Gelding him early helped him early so we'll see what happens. When he was racing lazily I was thinking come on pal, but when he loomed up it was all obvious that it was going to work out. It's a difficult one, I think the second is a Queen's Vase horse, but he might be as effective with some juice in the ground and that'll always be a help to him. The winner could be a Queen's Vase horse too. We'll put him in everything, he's in the Eclipse and so we'll see - Ralph Beckett, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

I knew Carmers would stay and I knew turning in Billy [Lee] looked comfortable and I was happy to see the horse come outside him and just not have him at the front and that he was able to get a breather into him round the bottom. He looked comfortable when the bell rang and I was thinking we had a chance. I think staying is his game and he's a pretty good horse. He's done everything we've asked of him and he will have an entry in the St Leger at Doncaster and we'll see, he's done what we've asked him to do today; he's run three times and won three times and hopefully he'll continue doing it - Paddy Twomey, trainer.Race went to plan all the way, jumped well, had a great position, when Harry came around I just let Carmers go on and was able to just hack away but got him rolling early from five out just to get him going forward; he takes a while to build up a head of steam and I know he'd stay well. I wanted to stretch them so I was always going to try and make it a stamina test and you can see him there pricking his ears going to the line, just doing enough. Staying was always going to be a forte of his. He's so laid back at home, he wouldn't pass out the dog at home, he's just that type of character, he saves plenty. I knew when I got stuck into him he'd keep finding away - Billy Lee, jockey.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Yeats Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: He's a horse who des just what he has to do, hence the headgear. I thought Carmers would be in the first three at Ballinrobe but he surprised me how well he won at the line. He came home, licked the pot, rode out this week and didn't care so we decided to come here and see what happens. We knew he'd stay but we didn't want to make it, he's a horse who probably doesn't want to be in front too long. To be fair he kept on well, he's keeping plenty to himself. The Queen's Vase is probably the race for him - Paddy Twomey, trainer, speaking to Racing TV

stable_tour.quote: He's unbeaten and has done nothing wrong. I liked what I saw from him at Navan last time and the Queen's Vase has always been the plan for him. I think the race will really suit him. 13-06-25

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

quotes.race: Sky Bet Great Voltigeur Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: They went very slow. Lambourn is a high-tempo, mile-and-a-half horse. Everything was perfect with him; we wanted to get a racecourse run or gallop in him before the Leger or the Arc. The race was kind of a mess really. It was a hack around to halfway, then a sprint up the stretch - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Lambourn is just so laid-back, I can't tell you. Ryan gave him a lovely, uncomplicated, straightforward ride. He knew him to a tee from winning on him at Chester and everything Wayne [Lordan] told him. He's so relaxed, and the horse coming with him [Sir Dinadan] was only going to help him, and you can see he was pricking his ears all the way up the straight. I knew Ryan would be very happy about that as he would have been delighted to have company and would love to get into a battle. He has that personality, that attitude, it's like everything in life when sports people have that attitude and he's that straightforward, genuine and just does whatever you want for you, whenever he can. He's like his dad, if you put him in first gear, he stays in first gear; if you put him in third gear, he'll stay in third gear. He's not influenced by anything around him, but always wants to please and never overdo himself. He could be a King George or an Arc type of horse. The way he races, I'd imagine he'd race that way regardless of the trip. You'd imagine he'd get further but he has lots of class - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

quotes.race: Betfred Derby (In Honour Of His Highness Aga Khan IV) (Group 1) (No Geldings)
quotes.quote: Australia [Lambourn's sire] is a great Derby winner, very genuine, and that's what Lambourn is - uncomplicated, genuine and committed. He always has been. His runs this year have been very good - he was second to Delacroix, and then was very impressive in Chester. Chester again - you have to be on your game and you have to stay, and that's the way he was. Ryan loved him in Chester and he obviously can't ride them all. He was always going to be an Irish Derby horse, a Leger horse, and Wayne said he definitely would stay further. He saves plenty for himself and he gives himself a chance to get any trip - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

Lambourn looked a thorough stayer and while he was a little on and off the bridle, once the penny dropped he came home really strongly. He got the trip really well and he's gone through the line nicely. We'll just have to see how they all get on in the trials but we were thinking of sending him to Epsom as they learn so much when they go to Chester - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We were very happy with his run when second in the Ballysax behind Delacroix and he's come out of it well. We were delighted with it for his first run of the season and he looks like a horse who will get further. 07-04-25

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.quote: We're delighted. This was Illinois's race, all was planned out, but when Ryan [Moore] said at Newmarket that Scandinavia felt very classy, it was the right thing to do to let them come and run the two of them together and see. Scandinavia looks a very classy stayer, he travelled very well through the race and Wayne [Lordan] gave him a beautiful ride. He was always a very smart horse. The lads actually backed him for the Derby very early in his career, he was considered a very good horse. He won his maiden over a mile and a quarter and Wayne was delighted with him at Ascot next time - he got trapped six wide but he still didn't lie down the whole way and came back at the line to try and win. He's a great stayer with a great mind, he's uncomplicated, and like we saw today he handled the ease in the ground well. You couldn't be more delighted with him really. You would imagine he looks a ready-made Leger horse. Lambourn didn't go to the King George, he is looking at the Voltigeur, and after that an Arc or the British or Irish Leger after that. Scandinavia doesn't have to run again before the Leger if he's going there, so all the options are there - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.Scandinavia has improved all year. He was stepping up to two miles today against the older horses. It's a tough task but I thought he stepped forward quite well. He was hanging onto a little bit even when he got there so I think there's plenty of improvement to come. Today was going to find out how smart he is and he's obviously a very smart stayer - Wayne Lordan, jockey.

I think the St Leger in England, or Ireland, would be targets and then hopefully Scandinavia can develop into a cup horse for next year. We can plot our way to those nice staying races for the rest of the year. We'll see how the horses perform over the festivals at Goodwood and York. He seems to have improved again since Ascot. Hopefully now we can have some fun with him over these longer distances - Paul Smith, co-owner.Scandinavia has been improving with every race and ran well at Ascot. He got into a lovely rhythm today and stayed on really well. I never rode him in a race before although I have rode him at home, so I wouldn't know about the [first-time] cheekpieces. He was impressive today - Ryan Moore, jockey.

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

quotes.race: Sky Bet Great Voltigeur Stakes (Group 2) (Colts & Geldings)
quotes.quote: Stay True always looked like a lovely Leger horse; you'd be happy with that - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Illinois is deserving of success at this level but AL RIFFA could just have that better turn of foot at this distance
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 Illinois, 9/4 Al Riffa, 7/2 Amiloc, 8/1 Al Qareem, 12/1 Crystal Black, 20/1 Leinster, 33/1 Queenstown, 50/1 Dallas Star,  Waldadler

quotes.race: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Apercu is a tough mare, she's improving and Billy knows her well. He gave her a great ride. She seems to go everywhere he wants her. She got it handy up front and he said himself that he probably got there a bit earlier than he thought. She was enjoying herself and was going sweetly. She battles and when the horse came back at her she just kept finding. She was tough out, she's a great filly to watch because she wears her heart on his sleeve. Barry Irwin, CEO of Team Valor, has been a great supporter of mine over the years. We have a few together as well at the moment. It's an exciting venture. This filly has done nothing but improve. She's been very easy to train and has given everybody a lot of fun - Andy Oliver, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She will probably go for the Northfields Handicap. She won nicely at Naas and she stepped up to a mile and half at the Curragh and finished sixth, which I was a bit disappointed with as I thought she had a great chance. I'm not quite sure she got the trip, so we're bringing her back in distance. She'll be near the bottom of the handicap but she has a fair chance of getting in. 11-09-24

quotes.race: Prix de Liancourt (Listed Race) (3yo Fillies) (Grande Course) (Turf)
quotes.quote: It was Henry [de Bromhead]'s idea to find a nice three-year-old race for fillies only, and there aren't too many of them in Ireland. Higher Leaves had a little break after the Ribblesdale and Henry says she's really strengthened up. She's progressive and that was good today. Henry says a mile and a quarter is more her trip, and, although as she gets stronger she might get further, she has a bit of pace. Alexis said she quickened up very well. Henry has always held her in very high regard and the Ribblesdale just didn't work out. She's had a little break and come on since so, on that performance, you'd have to say there's more to come. She's got a good pedigree and Henry feels she'll be a better four-year-old - Gerry Hogan, whose wife Ciara owns Higher Leaves in partnership with John Halley.

quotes.race: Bedford Lodge Hotel & Spa Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: Naomi Lapaglia was a breeze-up filly and we spent a long time last year getting her right mentally. She needs to settle, and the problem at Goodwood last time was she was too lit up. We always thought a mile would suit her and the more racing she has the better she'll be. Hopefully she'll be able to step back into black type races, although we don't have a plan as to where - Richard Spencer, trainer.

quotes.race: Al Shira'aa Racing Meadow Court Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: It is great to come here and win at the Curragh again. One Look seems to like it here. She is a lovely filly. Ascot didn't work out for her on ground quicker than she would like and a trip as short as she would want to go. She had ran this sort of distance last year at Gowran and we felt on pedigree that stepping her up would suit her. She was brave today, she didn't have the easiest passage and she won nicely at the death. Today was a sit and see. We have to step up now and look at better races. She is in the Nassau, she will be in the races on Arc weekend, she can run in the Blandford Stakes too. We will have a think and pick our spots but she will probably run more than she has in the past. We are lucky to have a good bunch of horses and have great owners -  Paddy Twomey, trainer.

quotes.race: Lodge Park Stud Irish EBF Park Express Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: She's tough and I think a mile is the minimum for now. Ten furlongs will be well within her compass. One Look will have an entry in the Lanwades Stakes. She's a Group winner now and we'll try and go as far up the ladder as we can. The ability was never in question it was just last spring she wasn't ready to do what the world expected her to do. She'll probably prove yet that she is that kind of filly - Paddy Twomey, trainer

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Race
quotes.quote: One Look did what we hoped she would do and it went to plan. I'm thrilled with the way she did it. That's her first piece of work this year. If she goes to Newmarket, she will probably go straight there - Paddy Twomey, trainer

One Look is a nice filly. I thought I would get her out earlier in the year, but it was just the way things worked out. She was just ready to run this week. I thought she was good enough to be competitive. The plan was to track Frankie (Dettori) on Cherry Blossom and see how we got on. I gave her a Guineas entry this week. It was a big performance to do that and I think that will be it for the year - Paddy Twomey, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's a lovely filly and I was delighted with her at the Curragh. She only found Porta Fortuna too good there and the Duke of Cambridge has been pencilled in for her. She's smart.  13-06-25

quotes.quote: With the four runners it was uncomplicated, Snellen likes to follow and she travelled into it nicely. We picked up well and got a length or so on Colin (Keane). With Gary (Carroll)  suspended it was a nice ride to get  - Shane Foley, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

We are delighted, it was a great run. Snellen has course form and that's always a great thing when you come here. Delighted with the run. I thought she was going to hold on for a few seconds, but nowadays I think a mile is her minimum and she will stay further. For that reason, Gary sent her quite early, but she stayed going all the way to the line. We've no plans. The owner just bought her at the London sale on Monday, so we'll have a chat and we'll work it all out - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

When I won at Limerick on Snellen she hung very badly left in the last two furlongs and I stopped riding to correct her and lost momentum. Today I should have pulled my stick through but she started to roll left and I told myself 'I'm not stopping my momentum'. She's still a frame of a filly and she's not fully furnished. She's had two runs in ten days and I'd love to give her a mid-season break and have a go at a big race in the autumn. She won't be at her best until she's three - Gary Carroll, rider.

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.race: Wesco Anixter EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Ballet Slippers has a great pedigree. She was entitled to win and will step forward. She has a great attitude and will be a nice filly next year - Ryan Moore, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She did well last season despite winning only once. She had a lovely run at the Curragh when second behind one of Donnacha's fillies [Falling Snow] - I know he loves her. She then ran very well when third in the Fillies' Mile, and we were going back to France with her for a mile-and-a-quarter race but she got injured. We'll train her for the Oaks. 07-04-25

quotes.race: Darley Irish EBF Kooyonga Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
quotes.quote: I think the draw helped and Barnavara made it a test down in grade. I think that's her perfect ground, good to yielding. She's huge and is going to develop. We will get her home and map out a plan - Kate Harrington, trainer's assistant.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Barnavara was good. We worked her on softish ground the other day and I was delighted with her. She will probably like better ground. We might go to Ascot for the Albany and she might have a run before then - Jessica Harrington, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She was a very smart two-year-old. She won on her debut at the Curragh and held her form together very well in Group races. She was seventh in a Group 3 at Leopardstown at the end of March, but all of ours have been needing their first runs and she'll definitely improve for it. She's a great big, galloping filly and we're really looking forward to stepping her up to a mile and a quarter in the Salsabil Stakes at Navan on Saturday. 23-04-25

runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

quotes.race: It's Family Day At Leopardstown Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: Butterfly Wings had run in a couple of very good maidens, and we thought she might have won by now. Hopefully that win will give her the confidence to send her to the next level. Wayne [Lordan, jockey] felt she was grand at that trip, but the best would probably be when she goes up to a mile and a half which gives plenty of options. It's great to get the maiden win and we'll see where she fits in with the other fillies and try to get some black type - Chris Armstrong, stable representative.

runners.breeder: Lynch Bages Ltd & Camas Park Stud
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

quotes.race: Dooley Insurance Group Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Merrily had a lovely run down in Listowel the first time but was a bit slow to jump and Wayne felt that she was a bit unlucky as she got squeezed at the wrong time. This isn't her ideal trip but the race presented itself. We thought we'd give it a go and it worked out - Chris Armstrong, stable representative

stable_tour.quote: She's a Group 3 winner, landing last year's Oh So Sharp at 25-1, and we thought she could be a Guineas filly. She was ninth on her return at Leopardstown but we were happy with it for her first run. 07-04-25

quotes.quote: We've just had to be so patient with Red Letter and we have a lovely horse to look forward to next year. Hopefully, she'll fill all of her potential in the next 12 months and is a filly to look forward to. We're looking forward to Champions Weekend, which is very important to us, and we'll have a look at the Blandford Stakes. The ground is very important as she needs ease. Obviously, the family have their say with her but we've been training her with all of this in mind, with both the backend of the season and next year. I think she can get a mile and a half, no problem - Ger Lyons, trainer.

quotes.race: Sycamore Lodge Equine Hospital Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Red Letter did what we probably expected. She stepped forward nicely from her debut. We will see how she comes out of it. It's just raw ability at the moment. She will tell me where we go with her, whether she comes back for the Moyglare or something like that - Ger Lyons, trainer.

runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.breeder: His Highness The Aga Khan's Studs S C
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

quotes.race: "Look De Vega" At Ballylinch Stud Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: That was a nice performance by Tarima and she'll learn a lot from it. Chris [Hayes, jockey] hadn't planned to sit quite so far back on her, but she just gets turned sideways when they came out of the stalls and he had to sit and do the right thing and ride her with confidence. She was a backward filly as a two-year-old and I didn't get to teach her that much - she was growing and developing. She's going to learn an awful lot from that race. I expect a lot of progress and hopefully she develops to be a stakes filly. I trained her two half-sisters to win seven Group Ones - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Salsabil Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
quotes.quote: Wemightakedlongway is a good filly and I thought her last run, finishing behind Delacroix, was really good for a first run back. We were looking at going to Chester but when the drop of rain came, we decided to come here and she has won impressively. I have campaigned her aggressively and she ran against colts on both of her last two runs. She's a smart filly. The Pretty Polly at the Curragh is the race we're really building towards. She's out of a No Nay Never mare, so she just wouldn't be guaranteed to go a mile and a half. We'll see whether we look at Epsom or whether we look at something else before the Pretty Polly - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: We thought it was a real good run when fifth in the Ballysax on her return, and she'll go to either Chester or to Navan for the Salsabil on Saturday. Hopefully she can be a real smart filly for the year. 24-04-25

racecards.verdict: Fourth in last year's Moyglare, RED LETTER has come good on her last two starts and should be suited by the trip and ground
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 Red Letter, 9/4 One Look, 13/2 Snellen, 7/1 Barnavara, 10/1 Ballet Slippers, 12/1 Naomi Lapaglia,  Tarima, 16/1 Higher Leaves, 20/1 Merrily, 33/1 Apercu, 50/1 Spicy Margarita

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Northfields Handicap (Premier Handicap)

quotes.quote: Thunder Run loves the track, but the step up in trip was the key. He needed to relax but after half a furlong he took off with Clifford [Lee] - the last thing I said to him was do not make it. I'd have dragged him off coming back in if he did!  Once he got a bit of cover he got him in a lovely rhythm. I didn't want him to hit the front too soon but didn't think it'd be that late. It nearly gave me a heart attack.  When he learns to race a bit more economically he's definitely a Listed or Group 3 horse. The Wolferton at Royal Ascot will be the plan. His new owners paid a nice bit of money for him before the Lincoln and they'd love to have a runner there - Karl Burke, trainer.

Thunder Run is still very raw and it wasn't the plan to go out and make it, but David (Egan) said he jumped out very well as they didn't feel they were going quick enough. But if something took him it probably would've cost him the race - he was on fresh air for the last 100 yards. We only ran Thunder Run at Thirsk last time as he needed a penalty to get in. Thank god he didn't win, because he might've got beat with a 6lb penalty - Karl Burke, trainer.

Thunder Run was entitled to improve a lot from his first run. We had loads of excuses at Wetherby, he was slow away and drawn wide, and William's (Haggas)  horse got the run of the race. I felt we were unlucky not to win. He's quite a relaxed horse and he quickened up well when Danny asked him. I'd say it was a good performance and he's a smart horse. Danny likes him and I'd say we can go upwards from here - Karl Burke, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ended up having to do the donkey work on his return in the Lincoln and just got tired late on but he showed the benefit of that run in winning at York when stepping up in trip last time. I've always thought he'll make up into a Group horse and the Wolferton looks an ideal next step. 12-06-25

runners.breeder: H H The Aga Khan's Studs Sc

quotes.race: Sherry FitzGerald Country Homes Ladies Derby Handicap
quotes.quote: That was my first ride in Ireland and it's gone lovely, over the moon to be here. Masoun was a little bit further back than I wanted and ran into a bit of trouble but we were lucky to find a gap and I had enough horse underneath me. My stepdad is Brian Harding so my love for racing was born in the jumps sphere and I didn't have much involvement in the Flat until recently and things are going really well, that's my fifth winner in the last fortnight - Elizabeth Gale, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

You'd have to say Mashhoor has improved immensely. He's been very straightforward this year. There was no pace today so I said to Ben (Coen) to just let him roll along as he leads the string at home. I knew when they turned in that they weren't going to get to him, as he wasn't going to stop because he won over a mile and a half last time - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

quotes.race: FBD Hotels & Resorts Orby Stakes (Listed Race)
quotes.quote: That was great. Mashoor has been training really well at home and showing all the right signs that he had improved. I'm really delighted the way he won today. He can win at 1m2f and now he can get 1m4f so there are plenty of races for him - Johnny Murtagh, trainer

Mashhoor ran very well in Cork on his first run and he had a very good draw today, and was given a very good ride by Shane. He's mentally immature but is toughening up now and being by Kingman I'd say he has loads of class - Johnny Murtagh, trainer.

Mashhoor ran well on debut last year and learnt plenty from that and will have done the same today. He's a strong traveller but picked up nicely and showed a good attitude to go through the gaps - Richard Kingscote, jockey.

runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

quotes.quote: Mississippi River always runs up to a nice mark. Seven is a lovely trip for him, he is in the handicap at Leopardstown next Saturday, so we will see how he comes out of it, if not, we will find another little race for him and probably try him in stakes company from seven to a mile. He'll have a winner's penalty, but in fairness, he has good form around Leopardstown and it is a good pot so we'll see how he is - Chris Armstrong, Aidan O'Brien's stable representative.

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

quotes.race: Tulfarris Hotel And Golf Resort Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Apercu is a tough mare, she's improving and Billy knows her well. He gave her a great ride. She seems to go everywhere he wants her. She got it handy up front and he said himself that he probably got there a bit earlier than he thought. She was enjoying herself and was going sweetly. She battles and when the horse came back at her she just kept finding. She was tough out, she's a great filly to watch because she wears her heart on his sleeve. Barry Irwin, CEO of Team Valor, has been a great supporter of mine over the years. We have a few together as well at the moment. It's an exciting venture. This filly has done nothing but improve. She's been very easy to train and has given everybody a lot of fun - Andy Oliver, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She will probably go for the Northfields Handicap. She won nicely at Naas and she stepped up to a mile and half at the Curragh and finished sixth, which I was a bit disappointed with as I thought she had a great chance. I'm not quite sure she got the trip, so we're bringing her back in distance. She'll be near the bottom of the handicap but she has a fair chance of getting in. 11-09-24

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

quotes.race: Kara Quinn Memorial Irish EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Acapulco Bay was very green in front. Ryan said he never picked up the bridle at all and felt he was in second gear - Aidan O'Brien, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well when second at Naas over a mile last month and should come forward for that run. He'll definitely get a mile and a quarter and could get further. 07-04-25

quotes.race: Happy 60th Birthday To Ger Dunne Of Punchestown Race
quotes.quote: Star Harbour was running over a trip well short of his best. We had him entered at Chester last weekend but we took him out because of the ground and because this race was there for him - Ado McGuinness, trainer.

quotes.race: Dubai Duty Free Irish EBF Ragusa Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Star Harbour has been very consistent, he won a good one last year in the Racing League as well. He's a great horse. We were to go to Yarmouth for the Racing League but that could put him out of the ratings. if it does he could go to Galway - Ado McGuinness, trainer.

Star Harbour got a top class ride. He is entered in the Galway Mile and if the handicapper isn't too hard on him he will go there instead of the Curragh on Sunday. He probably didn't quite stay at Down Royal but in time I think he will - Ado McGuinness, trainer.

We put blinkers on Star Harbour last time but he missed the break and got caught in behind. He has a lot of ability and he wasn't a cheap colt. We have castrated him as he was a bit of a boyo and wasn't giving us everything. We did feel he had a lot of ability and I said I'd run him in this even though we were a couple of pounds out of the handicap as he'd have a light weight on his back. He put it to bed fairly quickly. He'll be nice for a mile-and-a-half handicap at Galway or as regards closer plans I was talking about the Ulster Derby - Ado McGuinness, trainer.
Tony Montana is a lovely horse with a lovely pedigree. He's probably won't be high enough for the Ascot handicaps, so we'll box clever and look at places like Goodwood - Michael Bell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been second on both starts this year and has nudged up the handicap as a result. He's in the Royal Hunt Cup and the stiff mile at Ascot is an interesting angle for him. I'm not totally convinced that it'll work but we're very keen to give it a go. If it doesn't work he'd be aimed at something like the John Smith's Cup at York, in which he was third last year. He's now on a career-high mark of 99 but the Hunt Cup could work for him and, if it did, he'd be a live player. 09-06-25

quotes.race: Leopardstown Golf And Driving Range Handicap
quotes.quote: Cloud Seeker won four last year and had a blow in Tipperary, he just needed the run. We'll give him an entry at Royal Ascot or he could be one for something like the Ragusa at the Curragh. He jumps hurdles well so he has that option as well in the future - Andrew Slattery, trainer.

Viking Invasion improved a lot for stepping up in trip. It was just a bit sharp for him the last day. He handles this ground and quickened up well. He's a colt with plenty of potential and hopefully can keep on improving - Dylan Browne McMonagle, rider
State Actor got a terrific ride. It was a messy race here last time but he grew up today. The handicapper won't be easy on us but he is a decent horse - Bill Farrell, trainer

Booyea had a bit of an issue and couldn't run at Limerick last week. I was very keen to run before we went to the Curragh on Derby weekend and there weren't any suitable races for him. We were thinking of dropping back in trip to a mile at the Curragh anyway - Tom Gibney, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: This colt is so genuine and has given everyone a lot of fun with five wins from nine starts, which reads even better on the all-weather having a five from seven record. He does not go well on the grass so we will continue to race him on the artificial surface. He has risen through the ranks where he now finds himself with a rating of 93. There is a race at Wolverhampton on May 18 while there is a 1m2f option at Lingfield in mid-June. I would love to try him over 1m2f soon as I feel the extra trip could bring out improvement in him and add to his already commendable winning record. 28-04-21

quotes.quote: He jumped quick, went a nice gallop, ran down the hill and Genuine Article filled himself up and galloped out. When you have gate speed and your horse doesn't mind making the running just go with it - Seamie Heffernan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: Genuine Article took his time to come to hand and he will improve a ton. He seemed to be doing things well at home. There is a three-year-old handicap at Galway which might suit him - Chris Hayes, rider.

quotes.race: Silent Pool Gin 'Confined' Handicap (Have Won No More Than One Race)
quotes.quote: Crown Of Oaks has been waiting for the ground and he was good today in a trappy race. I thought he had half run off with him to start with, but he settled into a good rhythm and won reasonably well. He's in the Cambridgeshire and everyone else seems to think he has a chance, but he's got to get in. That penalty will help but I don't suppose he'll be off 85 again for a while, so we ought to aim for that - William Haggas, trainer.

Spoken Truth is a gorgeous big horse and wants better ground. A race like the Gallinule Stakes is a logical next step for him. I love the way he came home in the last furlong. He has a lot of talent - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Paddy Power Supporting Cancer Trials Ireland Irish Cambridgeshire (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: My right foot was gone and I just managed to keep the bottom of my heel in the iron. I don't know how I managed to stay on, but thank God I did. It was either hang on for dear life or come off - Wayne Hassett, rider. I'm delighted with Jagged Edge. He's a big horse, loves to be fresh and when he won for us in Cork, he looked like he was going to go on from it afterwards but just lost his way a bit. We left him alone and he's come back nicely. The ground was probably ideal for him and today was the day. I was delighted for Maurice Regan and Newtown Anner [owner], who are great supporters of mine and they bred him - Dermot Weld, trainer.

runners.owner: Dooley T'breds, J Fairhurst, Kabin Racing Synd, N O'Hare

prev_owners.owner: Dooley T'breds, J Fairhurst, Kabin Racing Synd, N O'Hare

quotes.quote: His last run wasn't too bad, although I thought six furlongs might be a little short for Glen To Glen. He's fairly straightforward, a suitable apprentice ride especially one claiming 10lb. She did it well - Jim Bolger, trainer

quotes.race: Kwiff: Home Of Supercharged Betting Handicap
quotes.quote: Lady In Paris ran really well here in Derby weekend when she was third but that was against the older horses when she was third. It is extraordinary the way she is bred that she gets a mile and a quarter well. You would imagine she would be a bit speedier but she stays well. Shane was banging the drum a little bit to get her to go up in trip and I think she could even go to a mile and a half based on that because they went a good gallop end to end and she outstayed all of them - Jessica Harrington, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: A Blue Point filly who ran in some hot maidens as a two-year-old and we think there's plenty more to come from her at three. She's nice. 23-04-25

quotes.quote: Lord Church was quite buzzy at two and it took us a good while to get him to relax and switch off which he does well now. He's due a break now and then we'll see where we go - Michael Halford, co-trainer
Lord Church did it well in the end. He's a smashing individual. He's a work in progress, he is going to go another furlong and will improve on better ground - Mick Halford, co-trainer.

runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

quotes.quote: Chris [Hayes] had Sujet in a lovely position throughout. When you are drawn wide here it is important to go and get a position otherwise you will be forced very wide over the nine furlongs, but he got him out and got him over. He tracked Ryan (Moore, on leader Mississippi Rover) and the plan was to have a go at him in the final furlong and that is just what he did. We'll see how he comes out of the race and another handicap will be the plan for him. He was gelded and just took a bit of time to come to himself. This was a lovely race for a three-year-old and he had been working nicely - Dermot Weld, trainer.

runners.owner: Don't Let The Old Man In Syndicate

quotes.quote: Money Dancer behaved herself better here than she does at home, she's very bouncy and not simple. But she has always shown us plenty. She has been tipping along all winter but we liked the couple of pieces of work she did. She found plenty and handled the trip well. We will roll the dice a bit with her now. With those fillies, you are as well off to get what you can when you can with them, so we will go for a Listed race somewhere - Noel Meade, trainer.
Mr Rango is tough and loves the ground, he just stays and gallops all day. He might jump a hurdle on the back of that - Katie O'Sullivan, assistant trainer

We were waiting on the rain all year, he is a grand type but he needs the rain for his stamina to kick in. It is great for his owners and hopefully he can do it again - Ross O'Sullivan, trainer Mr Rango

quotes.race: Curragh On Course Bookmakers Premier Handicap
quotes.quote: Rhythm King is very tough and genuine. He always wins around a bend so I did not think this would be his day, but he came good again. He had a light weight on his back today and everything came right - Mark Fahey, trainer.

The worse the conditions the more Rhythm King relishes them, he's only a small horse. I had a perfect run round, Colin (Keane) set lovely fractions in front, his horse rolled around a bit and I got a run up the rail. He really powered through those conditions - Gavin Ryan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Golf At Gowran Park Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Rhythm King did it well. He's an Elzaam so he loves this sort of ground. We'll see what's coming up but he is ground dependent - Mark Fahey, trainer.

We haven't had Areana a huge amount of time. She has always been a good moving filly and I just said to Jess, try and get her in a rhythm, don't worry about any of the others and she did nice fractions the whole way. I thought it was a very good ride by a 7lb claimer. She didn't panic at the finish; you didn't see her going for her stick, she just kept her going the last bit. I'm delighted, I said, that is the bottom limit of her stamina. She wouldn't be going any shorter. She had a ball out there. She could go back over hurdles but I think that ground suits her - Eddie Harty, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Competitive-looking but a worthy hot favourite in CROWN OF OAKS (nap), so impressive at Ascot on soft and 7lb well-in on future events
